277:   # Populate home feed of account from scratch
278:   # @param [Account] account
279:   # @return [void]
280:   def populate_home(account)
281:     limit        = FeedManager::MAX_ITEMS / 2
282:     aggregate    = account.user&.aggregates_reblogs?
283:     timeline_key = key(:home, account.id)
284:     over_limit = false
285: 
286:     account.statuses.limit(limit).each do |status|
287:       add_to_feed(:home, account.id, status, aggregate_reblogs: aggregate)
288:     end
289: 
290:     account.following.includes(:account_stat).reorder(nil).find_each do |target_account|
291:       query = target_account.statuses.list_eligible_visibility.includes(reblog: :account).limit(limit)
292: 
293:       over_limit ||= redis.zcard(timeline_key) >= limit
294:       if over_limit
295:         oldest_home_score = redis.zrange(timeline_key, 0, 0, with_scores: true).first.last.to_i
296:         last_status_score = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(target_account.last_status_at, with_random: false)
297: 
298:         # If the feed is full and this account has not posted more recently
299:         # than the last item on the feed, then we can skip the whole account
300:         # because none of its statuses would stay on the feed anyway
301:         next if last_status_score < oldest_home_score
302: 
303:         # No need to get older statuses
304:         query = query.where(id: oldest_home_score...)
305:       end
306: 
307:       statuses = query.to_a
308:       next if statuses.empty?
309: 
310:       crutches = build_crutches(account.id, statuses)
311: 
312:       statuses.each do |status|
313:         next if filter_from_home(status, account.id, crutches)
314: 
315:         add_to_feed(:home, account.id, status, aggregate_reblogs: aggregate)
316:       end
317: 
318:       trim(:home, account.id)
319:     end
320:   end
321: 
322:   # Populate list feed of account from scratch
323:   # @param [List] list
324:   # @return [void]
325:   def populate_list(list)
326:     limit        = FeedManager::MAX_ITEMS / 2
327:     aggregate    = list.account.user&.aggregates_reblogs?
328:     timeline_key = key(:list, list.id)
329:     over_limit = false
330: 
331:     list.active_accounts.includes(:account_stat).reorder(nil).find_each do |target_account|
332:       query = target_account.statuses.list_eligible_visibility.includes(reblog: :account).limit(limit)
333: 
334:       over_limit ||= redis.zcard(timeline_key) >= limit
335:       if over_limit
336:         oldest_home_score = redis.zrange(timeline_key, 0, 0, with_scores: true).first.last.to_i
337:         last_status_score = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(target_account.last_status_at, with_random: false)
338: 
339:         # If the feed is full and this account has not posted more recently
340:         # than the last item on the feed, then we can skip the whole account
341:         # because none of its statuses would stay on the feed anyway
342:         next if last_status_score < oldest_home_score
343: 
344:         # No need to get older statuses
345:         query = query.where(id: oldest_home_score...)
346:       end
347: 
348:       statuses = query.to_a
349:       next if statuses.empty?
350: 
351:       crutches = build_crutches(list.account_id, statuses, list: list)
352: 
353:       statuses.each do |status|
354:         next if filter_from_home(status, list.account_id, crutches, :list)
355: 
356:         add_to_feed(:list, list.id, status, aggregate_reblogs: aggregate)
357:       end
358: 
359:       trim(:list, list.id)
360:     end
361:   end
362: 
363:   # Completely clear multiple feeds at once
364:   # @param [Symbol] type
365:   # @param [Array<Integer>] ids
366:   # @return [void]
367:   def clean_feeds!(type, ids)
368:     reblogged_id_sets = {}
369: 
370:     redis.pipelined do |pipeline|
371:       ids.each do |feed_id|
372:         reblog_key = key(type, feed_id, 'reblogs')
373:         # We collect a future for this: we don't block while getting
374:         # it, but we can iterate over it later.
375:         reblogged_id_sets[feed_id] = pipeline.zrange(reblog_key, 0, -1)
376:         pipeline.del(key(type, feed_id), reblog_key)
377:       end
378:     end
379: 
380:     # Remove all of the reblog tracking keys we just removed the
381:     # references to.
382:     keys_to_delete = reblogged_id_sets.flat_map do |feed_id, future|
383:       future.value.map do |reblogged_id|
384:         key(type, feed_id, "reblogs:#{reblogged_id}")
385:       end
386:     end
387: 
388:     redis.del(keys_to_delete) unless keys_to_delete.empty?
389: 
390:     nil
391:   end
392: 
393:   private
394: 
395:   # Trim a feed to maximum size by removing older items
396:   # @param [Symbol] type
397:   # @param [Integer] timeline_id
398:   # @return [void]
399:   def trim(type, timeline_id)
400:     timeline_key = key(type, timeline_id)
401:     reblog_key   = key(type, timeline_id, 'reblogs')
402: 
403:     # Remove any items past the MAX_ITEMS'th entry in our feed
404:     redis.zremrangebyrank(timeline_key, 0, -(FeedManager::MAX_ITEMS + 1))
405: 
406:     # Get the score of the REBLOG_FALLOFF'th item in our feed, and stop
407:     # tracking anything after it for deduplication purposes.
408:     falloff_rank  = FeedManager::REBLOG_FALLOFF
409:     falloff_range = redis.zrevrange(timeline_key, falloff_rank, falloff_rank, with_scores: true)
410:     falloff_score = falloff_range&.first&.last&.to_i
411: 
412:     return if falloff_score.nil?
413: 
414:     # Get any reblogs we might have to clean up after.
415:     redis.zrangebyscore(reblog_key, 0, falloff_score).each do |reblogged_id|
416:       # Remove it from the set of reblogs we're tracking *first* to avoid races.
417:       redis.zrem(reblog_key, reblogged_id)
418:       # Just drop any set we might have created to track additional reblogs.
419:       # This means that if this reblog is deleted, we won't automatically insert
420:       # another reblog, but also that any new reblog can be inserted into the
421:       # feed.
422:       redis.del(key(type, timeline_id, "reblogs:#{reblogged_id}"))
423:     end
424:   end
425: 
426:   # Check if there is a streaming API client connected
427:   # for the given feed
428:   # @param [String] timeline_key
429:   # @return [Boolean]
430:   def push_update_required?(timeline_key)
431:     redis.exists?("subscribed:#{timeline_key}")
432:   end
433: 
434:   # Check if the account is blocking or muting any of the given accounts
435:   # @param [Integer] receiver_id
436:   # @param [Array<Integer>] account_ids
437:   # @param [Symbol] context
438:   def blocks_or_mutes?(receiver_id, account_ids, context)
439:     Block.where(account_id: receiver_id, target_account_id: account_ids).any? ||
440:       (context == :home ? Mute.where(account_id: receiver_id, target_account_id: account_ids).any? : Mute.where(account_id: receiver_id, target_account_id: account_ids, hide_notifications: true).any?)
441:   end
442: 
443:   # Check if status should not be added to the home feed
444:   # @param [Status] status
445:   # @param [Integer] receiver_id
446:   # @param [Hash] crutches
447:   # @return [void|Symbol] nil, :skip_home, or :filter
448:   def filter_from_home(status, receiver_id, crutches, timeline_type = :home)
449:     return            if receiver_id == status.account_id
450:     return :filter    if status.reply? && (status.in_reply_to_id.nil? || status.in_reply_to_account_id.nil?)
451:     return :skip_home if timeline_type != :list && crutches[:exclusive_list_users][status.account_id].present?
452:     return :filter    if crutches[:languages][status.account_id].present? && status.language.present? && !crutches[:languages][status.account_id].include?(status.language)
453:     return :filter    if status.reblog? && status.reblog.blank?
454: 
455:     check_for_blocks = crutches[:active_mentions][status.id] || []
456:     check_for_blocks.push(status.account_id)
457: 
458:     if status.reblog?
459:       check_for_blocks.push(status.reblog.account_id)
460:       check_for_blocks.concat(crutches[:active_mentions][status.reblog_of_id] || [])
461:     end
462: 
463:     return :filter if check_for_blocks.any? { |target_account_id| crutches[:blocking][target_account_id] || crutches[:muting][target_account_id] }
464:     return :filter if crutches[:blocked_by][status.account_id]
465: 
466:     if status.reply? && !status.in_reply_to_account_id.nil?                                                                      # Filter out if it's a reply
467:       should_filter   = !crutches[:following][status.in_reply_to_account_id]                                                     # and I'm not following the person it's a reply to
468:       should_filter &&= receiver_id != status.in_reply_to_account_id                                                             # and it's not a reply to me
469:       should_filter &&= status.account_id != status.in_reply_to_account_id                                                       # and it's not a self-reply
470:     elsif status.reblog?                                                                                                         # Filter out a reblog
471:       should_filter   = crutches[:hiding_reblogs][status.account_id]                                                             # if the reblogger's reblogs are suppressed
472:       should_filter ||= crutches[:blocked_by][status.reblog.account_id]                                                          # or if the author of the reblogged status is blocking me
473:       should_filter ||= crutches[:domain_blocking][status.reblog.account.domain]                                                 # or the author's domain is blocked
474:     else
475:       should_filter = false
476:     end
477: 
478:     should_filter ? :filter : nil
479:   end
480: 
481:   # Check if status should not be added to the mentions feed
482:   # @see NotifyService
483:   # @param [Status] status
484:   # @param [Integer] receiver_id
485:   # @return [Boolean]
486:   def filter_from_mentions?(status, receiver_id)
487:     return true if receiver_id == status.account_id
488: 
489:     # This filter is called from NotifyService, but already after the sender of
490:     # the notification has been checked for mute/block. Therefore, it's not
491:     # necessary to check the author of the toot for mute/block again
492:     check_for_blocks = status.active_mentions.pluck(:account_id)
493:     check_for_blocks.push(status.in_reply_to_account) if status.reply? && !status.in_reply_to_account_id.nil?
494: 
495:     blocks_or_mutes?(receiver_id, check_for_blocks, :mentions) # Filter if it's from someone I blocked, in reply to someone I blocked, or mentioning someone I blocked (or muted)
496:   end
497: 
498:   # Check if status should not be added to the list feed
499:   # @param [Status] status
500:   # @param [List] list
501:   # @return [Boolean]
502:   def filter_from_list?(status, list)
503:     if status.reply? && status.in_reply_to_account_id != status.account_id                                                       # Status is a reply to account other than status account
504:       should_filter = status.in_reply_to_account_id != list.account_id                                                           # Status replies to account id other than list account
505:       should_filter &&= !list.show_followed?                                                                                     # List show_followed? is false
506:       should_filter &&= !(list.show_list? && ListAccount.exists?(list_id: list.id, account_id: status.in_reply_to_account_id))   # If show_list? true, check for a ListAccount with list and reply to account
507: 
508:       return !!should_filter
509:     end
510: 
511:     false
512:   end
513: 
514:   # Check if a status should not be added to the home feed when it comes
515:   # from a followed hashtag
516:   # @param [Status] status
517:   # @param [Integer] receiver_id
518:   # @param [Hash] crutches
519:   # @return [Boolean]
520:   def filter_from_tags?(status, receiver_id, crutches)
521:     receiver_id == status.account_id ||                                                                                          # Receiver is status account?
522:       ((crutches[:active_mentions][status.id] || []) + [status.account_id])                                                      # For mentioned accounts or status account:
523:         .any? { |target_account_id| crutches[:blocking][target_account_id] || crutches[:muting][target_account_id] } ||          #   - Target account is muted or blocked?
524:       crutches[:blocked_by][status.account_id] ||                                                                                # Blocked by status account?
525:       crutches[:domain_blocking][status.account.domain]                                                                          # Blocking domain of status account?
526:   end
527: 
528:   # Adds a status to an account's feed, returning true if a status was
529:   # added, and false if it was not added to the feed. Note that this is
530:   # an internal helper: callers must call trim or push updates if
531:   # either action is appropriate.
532:   # @param [Symbol] timeline_type
533:   # @param [Integer] account_id
534:   # @param [Status] status
535:   # @param [Boolean] aggregate_reblogs
536:   # @return [Boolean]
537:   def add_to_feed(timeline_type, account_id, status, aggregate_reblogs: true)
538:     timeline_key = key(timeline_type, account_id)
539:     reblog_key   = key(timeline_type, account_id, 'reblogs')
540: 
541:     if status.reblog? && (aggregate_reblogs.nil? || aggregate_reblogs)
542:       # If the original status or a reblog of it is within
543:       # REBLOG_FALLOFF statuses from the top, do not re-insert it into
544:       # the feed
545:       rank = redis.zrevrank(timeline_key, status.reblog_of_id)
546: 
547:       return false if !rank.nil? && rank < FeedManager::REBLOG_FALLOFF
548: 
549:       # The ordered set at `reblog_key` holds statuses which have a reblog
550:       # in the top `REBLOG_FALLOFF` statuses of the timeline
551:       if redis.zadd(reblog_key, status.id, status.reblog_of_id, nx: true)
552:         # This is not something we've already seen reblogged, so we
553:         # can just add it to the feed (and note that we're reblogging it).
554:         redis.zadd(timeline_key, status.id, status.id)
555:       else
556:         # Another reblog of the same status was already in the
557:         # REBLOG_FALLOFF most recent statuses, so we note that this
558:         # is an "extra" reblog, by storing it in reblog_set_key.
559:         reblog_set_key = key(timeline_type, account_id, "reblogs:#{status.reblog_of_id}")
560:         redis.sadd(reblog_set_key, status.id)
561:         return false
562:       end
563:     else
564:       # A reblog may reach earlier than the original status because of the
565:       # delay of the worker delivering the original status, the late addition
566:       # by merging timelines, and other reasons.
567:       # If such a reblog already exists, just do not re-insert it into the feed.
568:       return false unless redis.zscore(reblog_key, status.id).nil?
569: 
570:       redis.zadd(timeline_key, status.id, status.id)
571:     end
572: 
573:     true
574:   end
575: 
576:   # Removes an individual status from a feed, correctly handling cases
577:   # with reblogs, and returning true if a status was removed. As with
578:   # `add_to_feed`, this does not trigger push updates, so callers must
579:   # do so if appropriate.
580:   # @param [Symbol] timeline_type
581:   # @param [Integer] account_id
582:   # @param [Status] status
583:   # @param [Boolean] aggregate_reblogs
584:   # @return [Boolean]
585:   def remove_from_feed(timeline_type, account_id, status, aggregate_reblogs: true)
586:     timeline_key = key(timeline_type, account_id)
587:     reblog_key   = key(timeline_type, account_id, 'reblogs')
588: 
589:     if status.reblog? && (aggregate_reblogs.nil? || aggregate_reblogs)
590:       # 1. If the reblogging status is not in the feed, stop.
591:       status_rank = redis.zrevrank(timeline_key, status.id)
592:       return false if status_rank.nil?
593: 
594:       # 2. Remove reblog from set of this status's reblogs.
595:       reblog_set_key = key(timeline_type, account_id, "reblogs:#{status.reblog_of_id}")
596: 
597:       redis.srem(reblog_set_key, status.id)
598:       redis.zrem(reblog_key, status.reblog_of_id)
599:       # 3. Re-insert another reblog or original into the feed if one
600:       # remains in the set. We could pick a random element, but this
601:       # set should generally be small, and it seems ideal to show the
602:       # oldest potential such reblog.
603:       other_reblog = redis.smembers(reblog_set_key).map(&:to_i).min
604: 
605:       redis.zadd(timeline_key, other_reblog, other_reblog) if other_reblog
606:       redis.zadd(reblog_key, other_reblog, status.reblog_of_id) if other_reblog
607: 
608:       # 4. Remove the reblogging status from the feed (as normal)
609:       # (outside conditional)
610:     else
611:       # If the original is getting deleted, no use for reblog references
612:       redis.del(key(timeline_type, account_id, "reblogs:#{status.id}"))
613:       redis.zrem(reblog_key, status.id)
614:     end
615: 
616:     redis.zrem(timeline_key, status.id)
617:   end
618: 
619:   # Pre-fetch various objects and relationships for given statuses that
620:   # are going to be checked by the filtering methods
621:   # @param [Integer] receiver_id
622:   # @param [Array<Status>] statuses
623:   # @param [List] list
624:   # @return [Hash]
625:   def build_crutches(receiver_id, statuses, list: nil)
626:     crutches = {}
627: 
628:     crutches[:active_mentions] = crutches_active_mentions(statuses)
629: 
630:     check_for_blocks = statuses.flat_map do |s|
631:       arr = crutches[:active_mentions][s.id] || []
632:       arr.push(s.account_id)
633: 
634:       if s.reblog? && s.reblog.present?
635:         arr.push(s.reblog.account_id)
636:         arr.concat(crutches[:active_mentions][s.reblog_of_id] || [])
637:       end
638: 
639:       arr
640:     end
641: 
642:     crutches[:following]            = crutches_following(receiver_id, statuses, list)
643:     crutches[:languages]            = Follow.where(account_id: receiver_id, target_account_id: statuses.map(&:account_id)).pluck(:target_account_id, :languages).to_h
644:     crutches[:hiding_reblogs]       = Follow.where(account_id: receiver_id, target_account_id: statuses.filter_map { |s| s.account_id if s.reblog? }, show_reblogs: false).pluck(:target_account_id).index_with(true)
645:     crutches[:blocking]             = Block.where(account_id: receiver_id, target_account_id: check_for_blocks).pluck(:target_account_id).index_with(true)
646:     crutches[:muting]               = Mute.where(account_id: receiver_id, target_account_id: check_for_blocks).pluck(:target_account_id).index_with(true)
647:     crutches[:domain_blocking]      = AccountDomainBlock.where(account_id: receiver_id, domain: statuses.flat_map { |s| [s.account.domain, s.reblog&.account&.domain] }.compact).pluck(:domain).index_with(true)
648:     crutches[:blocked_by]           = Block.where(target_account_id: receiver_id, account_id: statuses.map { |s| [s.account_id, s.reblog&.account_id] }.flatten.compact).pluck(:account_id).index_with(true)
649:     crutches[:exclusive_list_users] = crutches_exclusive_list_users(receiver_id, statuses) if list.blank?
650: 
651:     crutches
652:   end
653: 
654:   def crutches_exclusive_list_users(recipient_id, statuses)
655:     lists = List.where(account_id: recipient_id, exclusive: true)
656:     ListAccount.where(list: lists, account_id: statuses.map(&:account_id)).pluck(:account_id).index_with(true)
657:   end
658: 
659:   def crutches_following(recipient_id, statuses, list)
660:     if list.blank? || list.show_followed?
661:       Follow.where(account_id: recipient_id, target_account_id: statuses.filter_map(&:in_reply_to_account_id)).pluck(:target_account_id).index_with(true)
662:     elsif list.show_list?
663:       ListAccount.where(list_id: list.id, account_id: statuses.filter_map(&:in_reply_to_account_id)).pluck(:account_id).index_with(true)
664:     else
665:       {}
666:     end
667:   end
668: 
669:   def crutches_active_mentions(statuses)
670:     Mention
671:       .active
672:       .where(status_id: statuses.flat_map { |status| [status.id, status.reblog_of_id] }.compact)
673:       .pluck(:status_id, :account_id)
674:       .each_with_object({}) { |(id, account_id), mapping| (mapping[id] ||= []).push(account_id) }
675:   end
676: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class FanOutOnWriteService < BaseService
4:   include Redisable
5: 
6:   # Push a status into home and mentions feeds
7:   # @param [Status] status
8:   # @param [Hash] options
9:   # @option options [Boolean] update
10:   # @option options [Array<Integer>] silenced_account_ids
11:   # @option options [Boolean] skip_notifications
12:   def call(status, options = {})
13:     @status    = status
14:     @account   = status.account
15:     @options   = options
16: 
17:     return if @status.proper.account.suspended?
18: 
19:     check_race_condition!
20:     warm_payload_cache!
21: 
22:     fan_out_to_local_recipients!
23:     fan_out_to_public_recipients! if broadcastable?
24:     fan_out_to_public_streams! if broadcastable?
25:   end
26: 
27:   private
28: 
29:   def check_race_condition!
30:     # I don't know why but at some point we had an issue where
31:     # this service was being executed with status objects
32:     # that had a null visibility - which should not be possible
33:     # since the column in the database is not nullable.
34:     #
35:     # This check re-queues the service to be run at a later time
36:     # with the full object, if something like it occurs
37: 
38:     raise Mastodon::RaceConditionError if @status.visibility.nil?
39:   end
40: 
41:   def fan_out_to_local_recipients!
42:     deliver_to_self!
43: 
44:     unless @options[:skip_notifications]
45:       notify_quoted_account!
46:       notify_mentioned_accounts!
47:       notify_about_update! if update?
48:     end
49: 
50:     case @status.visibility.to_sym
51:     when :public, :unlisted, :private
52:       deliver_to_all_followers!
53:       deliver_to_lists!
54:     when :limited
55:       deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
56:     else
57:       deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
58:       deliver_to_conversation!
59:     end
60:   end
61: 
62:   def fan_out_to_public_recipients!
63:     deliver_to_hashtag_followers!
64:   end
65: 
66:   def fan_out_to_public_streams!
67:     broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!
68:     broadcast_to_public_streams!
69:   end
70: 
71:   def deliver_to_self!
72:     FeedManager.instance.push_to_home(@account, @status, update: update?) if @account.local?
73:   end
74: 
75:   def notify_quoted_account!
76:     return unless @status.quote&.quoted_account&.local? && @status.quote&.accepted?
77: 
78:     LocalNotificationWorker.perform_async(@status.quote.quoted_account_id, @status.quote.id, 'Quote', 'quote')
79:   end
80: 
81:   def notify_mentioned_accounts!
82:     @status.active_mentions.joins(:account).merge(Account.local).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |mentions|
83:       LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(mentions) do |mention|
84:         options = { 'silenced' => true } if @options[:silenced_account_ids]&.include?(mention.account_id)
85: 
86:         [mention.account_id, mention.id, 'Mention', 'mention', options].compact
87:       end
88: 
89:       next unless update?
90: 
91:       # This may result in duplicate update payloads, but this ensures clients
92:       # are aware of edits to posts only appearing in mention notifications
93:       # (e.g. private mentions or mentions by people they do not follow)
94:       PushUpdateWorker.push_bulk(mentions.filter { |mention| subscribed_to_streaming_api?(mention.account_id) }) do |mention|
95:         [mention.account_id, @status.id, "timeline:#{mention.account_id}:notifications", { 'update' => true }]
96:       end
97:     end
98:   end
99: 
100:   def notify_about_update!
101:     @status.reblogged_by_accounts.merge(Account.local).select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |accounts|
102:       LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(accounts) do |account|
103:         [account.id, @status.id, 'Status', 'update']
104:       end
105:     end
106: 
107:     @status.quotes.accepted.find_in_batches do |quotes|
108:       LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(quotes) do |quote|
109:         [quote.account_id, quote.status_id, 'Status', 'quoted_update']
110:       end
111:     end
112:   end
113: 
114:   def deliver_to_all_followers!
115:     @account.followers_for_local_distribution.select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |followers|
116:       FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(followers) do |follower|
117:         [@status.id, follower.id, 'home', { 'update' => update? }]
118:       end
119:     end
120:   end
121: 
122:   def deliver_to_hashtag_followers!
123:     TagFollow.for_local_distribution.where(tag_id: @status.tags.map(&:id)).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |follows|
124:       FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(follows) do |follow|
125:         [@status.id, follow.account_id, 'tags', { 'update' => update? }]
126:       end
127:     end
128:   end
129: 
130:   def deliver_to_lists!
131:     @account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |lists|
132:       FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(lists) do |list|
133:         [@status.id, list.id, 'list', { 'update' => update? }]
134:       end
135:     end
136:   end
137: 
138:   def deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
139:     @status.mentions.joins(:account).merge(@account.followers_for_local_distribution).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |mentions|
140:       FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(mentions) do |mention|
141:         [@status.id, mention.account_id, 'home', { 'update' => update? }]
142:       end
143:     end
144:   end
145: 
146:   def broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!
147:     @status.tags.map(&:name).each do |hashtag|
148:       redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}", anonymous_payload)
149:       redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}:local", anonymous_payload) if @status.local?
150:     end
151:   end
152: 
153:   def broadcast_to_public_streams!
154:     return if @status.reply? && @status.in_reply_to_account_id != @account.id
155: 
156:     redis.publish('timeline:public', anonymous_payload)
157:     redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', anonymous_payload)
158: 
159:     if @status.with_media?
160:       redis.publish('timeline:public:media', anonymous_payload)
161:       redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', anonymous_payload)
162:     end
163:   end
164: 
165:   def deliver_to_conversation!
166:     AccountConversation.add_status(@account, @status) unless update?
167:   end
168: 
169:   def warm_payload_cache!
170:     Rails.cache.write("fan-out/#{@status.id}", rendered_status)
171:   end
172: 
173:   def anonymous_payload
174:     @anonymous_payload ||= JSON.generate({
175:       event: update? ? :'status.update' : :update,
176:       payload: rendered_status,
177:     }.as_json)
178:   end
179: 
180:   def rendered_status
181:     @rendered_status ||= InlineRenderer.render(@status, nil, :status)
182:   end
183: 
184:   def update?
185:     @options[:update]
186:   end
187: 
188:   def broadcastable?
189:     @status.public_visibility? && !@status.reblog? && !@account.silenced?
190:   end
191: 
192:   def subscribed_to_streaming_api?(account_id)
193:     redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{account_id}") || redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{account_id}:notifications")
194:   end
195: end

(End of file - total 195 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ProcessMentionsService < BaseService
4:   include Payloadable
5: 
6:   # Scan status for mentions and fetch remote mentioned users,
7:   # and create local mention pointers
8:   # @param [Status] status
9:   def call(status)
10:     @status = status
11: 
12:     return unless @status.local?
13: 
14:     @previous_mentions = @status.mentions.includes(:account).to_a
15:     @current_mentions  = []
16: 
17:     Status.transaction do
18:       scan_text!
19:       assign_mentions!
20:     end
21:   end
22: 
23:   private
24: 
25:   def scan_text!
26:     @status.text = @status.text.gsub(Account::MENTION_RE) do |match|
27:       username, domain = Regexp.last_match(1).split('@')
28: 
29:       domain = if TagManager.instance.local_domain?(domain)
30:                  nil
31:                else
32:                  TagManager.instance.normalize_domain(domain)
33:                end
34: 
35:       mentioned_account = Account.find_remote(username, domain)
36: 
37:       # Unapproved and unconfirmed accounts should not be mentionable
38:       next match if mentioned_account&.local? && !(mentioned_account.user_confirmed? && mentioned_account.user_approved?)
39: 
40:       # If the account cannot be found or isn't the right protocol,
41:       # first try to resolve it
42:       if mention_undeliverable?(mentioned_account)
43:         begin
44:           mentioned_account = ResolveAccountService.new.call(Regexp.last_match(1))
45:         rescue Webfinger::Error, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS, Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError
46:           mentioned_account = nil
47:         end
48:       end
49: 
50:       # If after resolving it still isn't found or isn't the right
51:       # protocol, then give up
52:       next match if mention_undeliverable?(mentioned_account) || mentioned_account&.unavailable?
53: 
54:       mention   = @previous_mentions.find { |x| x.account_id == mentioned_account.id }
55:       mention ||= @current_mentions.find  { |x| x.account_id == mentioned_account.id }
56:       mention ||= @status.mentions.new(account: mentioned_account)
57: 
58:       mention.silent = false
59: 
60:       @current_mentions << mention
61: 
62:       "@#{mentioned_account.acct}"
63:     end
64: 
65:     @status.save! if @status.persisted?
66:   end
67: 
68:   def assign_mentions!
69:     # Make sure we never mention blocked accounts
70:     unless @current_mentions.empty?
71:       mentioned_domains = @current_mentions.filter_map { |m| m.account.domain }.uniq
72:       blocked_domains   = Set.new(mentioned_domains.empty? ? [] : AccountDomainBlock.where(account_id: @status.account_id, domain: mentioned_domains).pluck(:domain))
73:       mentioned_account_ids = @current_mentions.map(&:account_id)
74:       blocked_account_ids = Set.new(@status.account.block_relationships.where(target_account_id: mentioned_account_ids).pluck(:target_account_id))
75: 
76:       dropped_mentions, @current_mentions = @current_mentions.partition { |mention| blocked_account_ids.include?(mention.account_id) || blocked_domains.include?(mention.account.domain) }
77:       dropped_mentions.each(&:destroy)
78:     end
79: 
80:     return unless @status.persisted?
81: 
82:     @current_mentions.each do |mention|
83:       mention.save if mention.new_record? || mention.silent_changed?
84:     end
85: 
86:     # If previous mentions are no longer contained in the text, convert them
87:     # to silent mentions, since withdrawing access from someone who already
88:     # received a notification might be more confusing
89:     removed_mentions = @previous_mentions - @current_mentions
90: 
91:     Mention.where(id: removed_mentions.map(&:id), silent: false).update_all(silent: true) unless removed_mentions.empty?
92:   end
93: 
94:   def mention_undeliverable?(mentioned_account)
95:     mentioned_account.nil? || (!mentioned_account.local? && !mentioned_account.activitypub?)
96:   end
97: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class NotifyService < BaseService
4:   include Redisable
5: 
6:   # TODO: the severed_relationships and annual_report types probably warrants email notifications
7:   NON_EMAIL_TYPES = %i(
8:     admin.report
9:     admin.sign_up
10:     update
11:     quoted_update
12:     poll
13:     status
14:     moderation_warning
15:     severed_relationships
16:     annual_report
17:     added_to_collection
18:     collection_update
19:   ).freeze
20: 
21:   class BaseCondition
22:     NEW_ACCOUNT_THRESHOLD = 30.days.freeze
23: 
24:     NEW_FOLLOWER_THRESHOLD = 3.days.freeze
25: 
26:     def initialize(notification, **options)
27:       @recipient = notification.account
28:       @sender = notification.from_account
29:       @notification = notification
30:       @policy = NotificationPolicy.find_or_initialize_by(account: @recipient)
31:       @from_staff = @sender.local? && @sender.user.present? && @sender.user_role&.bypass_block?(@recipient.user_role)
32:       @options = options
33:     end
34: 
35:     private
36: 
37:     def filterable_type?
38:       Notification::PROPERTIES[@notification.type][:filterable]
39:     end
40: 
41:     def not_following?
42:       !@recipient.following?(@sender)
43:     end
44: 
45:     def not_follower?
46:       follow = Follow.find_by(account: @sender, target_account: @recipient)
47:       follow.nil? || follow.created_at > NEW_FOLLOWER_THRESHOLD.ago
48:     end
49: 
50:     def new_account?
51:       @sender.created_at > NEW_ACCOUNT_THRESHOLD.ago
52:     end
53: 
54:     def override_for_sender?
55:       NotificationPermission.exists?(account: @recipient, from_account: @sender)
56:     end
57: 
58:     def message?
59:       @notification.type == :mention
60:     end
61: 
62:     def from_staff?
63:       @from_staff
64:     end
65: 
66:     def private_mention_not_in_response?
67:       @notification.type == :mention && @notification.target_status.direct_visibility? && !response_to_recipient?
68:     end
69: 
70:     def response_to_recipient?
71:       return false if @notification.target_status.in_reply_to_id.nil?
72: 
73:       statuses_that_mention_sender.positive?
74:     end
75: 
76:     def statuses_that_mention_sender
77:       # This queries private mentions from the recipient to the sender up in the thread.
78:       # This allows up to 100 messages that do not match in the thread, allowing conversations
79:       # involving multiple people.
80:       Status.count_by_sql([<<~SQL.squish, id: @notification.target_status.in_reply_to_id, recipient_id: @recipient.id, sender_id: @sender.id, depth_limit: 100])
81:         WITH RECURSIVE ancestors(id, in_reply_to_id, mention_id, path, depth) AS (
82:             SELECT s.id, s.in_reply_to_id, m.id, ARRAY[s.id], 0
83:             FROM statuses s
84:             LEFT JOIN mentions m ON m.silent = FALSE AND m.account_id = :sender_id AND m.status_id = s.id
85:             WHERE s.id = :id
86:           UNION ALL
87:             SELECT s.id, s.in_reply_to_id, m.id, ancestors.path || s.id, ancestors.depth + 1
88:             FROM ancestors
89:             JOIN statuses s ON s.id = ancestors.in_reply_to_id
90:             /* early exit if we already have a mention matching our requirements */
91:             LEFT JOIN mentions m ON m.silent = FALSE AND m.account_id = :sender_id AND m.status_id = s.id AND s.account_id = :recipient_id
92:             WHERE ancestors.mention_id IS NULL AND NOT s.id = ANY(path) AND ancestors.depth < :depth_limit
93:         )
94:         SELECT COUNT(*)
95:         FROM ancestors
96:         JOIN statuses s ON s.id = ancestors.id
97:         WHERE ancestors.mention_id IS NOT NULL AND s.account_id = :recipient_id AND s.visibility = 3
98:       SQL
99:     end
100: 
101:     def from_bot?
102:       @sender.bot?
103:     end
104:   end
105: 
106:   class DropCondition < BaseCondition
107:     def drop?
108:       blocked   = @recipient.unavailable?
109:       blocked ||= from_self? && %i(poll severed_relationships moderation_warning annual_report).exclude?(@notification.type)
110: 
111:       return blocked if message? && from_staff?
112: 
113:       blocked ||= domain_blocking?
114:       blocked ||= @recipient.blocking?(@sender)
115:       blocked ||= @recipient.muting_notifications?(@sender)
116:       blocked ||= conversation_muted?
117:       blocked ||= blocked_mention? if message?
118: 
119:       return true if blocked
120:       return false unless filterable_type?
121:       return false if override_for_sender?
122: 
123:       blocked_by_limited_accounts_policy? ||
124:         blocked_by_not_following_policy? ||
125:         blocked_by_not_followers_policy? ||
126:         blocked_by_new_accounts_policy? ||
127:         blocked_by_private_mentions_policy? ||
128:         blocked_by_bots_policy?
129:     end
130: 
131:     private
132: 
133:     def blocked_mention?
134:       FeedManager.instance.filter?(:mentions, @notification.target_status, @recipient)
135:     end
136: 
137:     def from_self?
138:       @recipient.id == @sender.id
139:     end
140: 
141:     def domain_blocking?
142:       @recipient.domain_blocking?(@sender.domain) && not_following?
143:     end
144: 
145:     def conversation_muted?
146:       @notification.target_status && @recipient.muting_conversation?(@notification.target_status.conversation)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def blocked_by_not_following_policy?
150:       @policy.drop_not_following? && not_following?
151:     end
152: 
153:     def blocked_by_not_followers_policy?
154:       @policy.drop_not_followers? && not_follower?
155:     end
156: 
157:     def blocked_by_new_accounts_policy?
158:       @policy.drop_new_accounts? && new_account? && not_following?
159:     end
160: 
161:     def blocked_by_private_mentions_policy?
162:       @policy.drop_private_mentions? && not_following? && private_mention_not_in_response?
163:     end
164: 
165:     def blocked_by_limited_accounts_policy?
166:       @policy.drop_limited_accounts? && (@options[:silenced] || @sender.silenced?) && not_following?
167:     end
168: 
169:     def blocked_by_bots_policy?
170:       @policy.drop_bots? && from_bot? && not_following?
171:     end
172:   end
173: 
174:   class FilterCondition < BaseCondition
175:     def filter?
176:       return false unless filterable_type?
177:       return false if override_for_sender?
178:       return false if message? && from_staff?
179: 
180:       filtered_by_limited_accounts_policy? ||
181:         filtered_by_not_following_policy? ||
182:         filtered_by_not_followers_policy? ||
183:         filtered_by_new_accounts_policy? ||
184:         filtered_by_private_mentions_policy? ||
185:         filtered_by_bots_policy?
186:     end
187: 
188:     private
189: 
190:     def filtered_by_not_following_policy?
191:       @policy.filter_not_following? && not_following?
192:     end
193: 
194:     def filtered_by_not_followers_policy?
195:       @policy.filter_not_followers? && not_follower?
196:     end
197: 
198:     def filtered_by_new_accounts_policy?
199:       @policy.filter_new_accounts? && new_account? && not_following?
200:     end
201: 
202:     def filtered_by_private_mentions_policy?
203:       @policy.filter_private_mentions? && not_following? && private_mention_not_in_response?
204:     end
205: 
206:     def filtered_by_limited_accounts_policy?
207:       @policy.filter_limited_accounts? && (@options[:silenced] || @sender.silenced?) && not_following?
208:     end
209: 
210:     def filtered_by_bots_policy?
211:       @policy.filter_bots? && from_bot? && not_following?
212:     end
213:   end
214: 
215:   def call(recipient, type, activity, **options)
216:     return if recipient.user.nil?
217: 
218:     @options      = options
219:     @recipient    = recipient
220:     @activity     = activity
221:     @notification = Notification.new(account: @recipient, type: type, activity: @activity)
222: 
223:     # For certain conditions we don't need to create a notification at all
224:     return if drop?
225: 
226:     @notification.filtered = filter?
227:     @notification.set_group_key!
228:     @notification.save!
229: 
230:     # It's possible the underlying activity has been deleted
231:     # between the save call and now
232:     return if @notification.activity.nil?
233: 
234:     if @notification.filtered?
235:       update_notification_request!
236:     else
237:       push_notification!
238:       push_to_conversation! if direct_message?
239:       send_email! if email_needed?
240:     end
241:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
242:     nil
243:   end
244: 
245:   private
246: 
247:   def drop?
248:     DropCondition.new(@notification, silenced: @options[:silenced]).drop?
249:   end
250: 
251:   def filter?
252:     FilterCondition.new(@notification, silenced: @options[:silenced]).filter?
253:   end
254: 
255:   def update_notification_request!
256:     return unless %i(mention quote).include?(@notification.type)
257: 
258:     notification_request = NotificationRequest.find_or_initialize_by(account_id: @recipient.id, from_account_id: @notification.from_account_id)
259:     notification_request.last_status_id = @notification.target_status.id
260:     notification_request.save
261:   end
262: 
263:   def push_notification!
264:     push_to_streaming_api! if subscribed_to_streaming_api?
265:     push_to_web_push_subscriptions!
266:   end
267: 
268:   def push_to_streaming_api!
269:     redis.publish("timeline:#{@recipient.id}:notifications", { event: :notification, payload: InlineRenderer.render(@notification, @recipient, :notification) }.to_json)
270:   end
271: 
272:   def subscribed_to_streaming_api?
273:     redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{@recipient.id}") || redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{@recipient.id}:notifications")
274:   end
275: 
276:   def push_to_conversation!
277:     AccountConversation.add_status(@recipient, @notification.target_status)
278:   end
279: 
280:   def direct_message?
281:     @notification.type == :mention && @notification.target_status.direct_visibility?
282:   end
283: 
284:   def push_to_web_push_subscriptions!
285:     ::Web::PushNotificationWorker.push_bulk(web_push_subscriptions.select { |subscription| subscription.pushable?(@notification) }) { |subscription| [subscription.id, @notification.id] }
286:   end
287: 
288:   def web_push_subscriptions
289:     @web_push_subscriptions ||= ::Web::PushSubscription.where(user_id: @recipient.user.id).to_a
290:   end
291: 
292:   def subscribed_to_web_push?
293:     web_push_subscriptions.any?
294:   end
295: 
296:   def send_email!
297:     return unless NotificationMailer.respond_to?(@notification.type)
298: 
299:     NotificationMailer
300:       .with(recipient: @recipient, notification: @notification)
301:       .public_send(@notification.type)
302:       .deliver_later(wait: 2.minutes)
303:   end
304: 
305:   def email_needed?
306:     (!recipient_online? || always_send_emails?) && send_email_for_notification_type?
307:   end
308: 
309:   def recipient_online?
310:     subscribed_to_streaming_api? || subscribed_to_web_push?
311:   end
312: 
313:   def always_send_emails?
314:     @recipient.user.settings.always_send_emails
315:   end
316: 
317:   def send_email_for_notification_type?
318:     NON_EMAIL_TYPES.exclude?(@notification.type) && @recipient.user.settings["notification_emails.#{@notification.type}"]
319:   end
320: end

3: class ProcessHashtagsService < BaseService
4:   def call(status, raw_tags = [])
5:     @status        = status
6:     @account       = status.account
7:     @raw_tags      = status.local? ? Extractor.extract_hashtags(status.text) : raw_tags
8:     @previous_tags = status.tags.to_a
9:     @current_tags  = []
10: 
11:     assign_tags!
12:     update_featured_tags!
13:   end
14: 
15:   private
16: 
17:   def assign_tags!
18:     @status.tags = @current_tags = Tag.find_or_create_by_names(@raw_tags)
19:   end
20: 
21:   def update_featured_tags!
22:     return unless @status.distributable?
23: 
24:     added_tags = @current_tags - @previous_tags
25: 
26:     unless added_tags.empty?
27:       @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: added_tags.map(&:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
28:         featured_tag.increment(@status.created_at)
29:       end
30:     end
31: 
32:     removed_tags = @previous_tags - @current_tags
33: 
34:     unless removed_tags.empty?
35:       @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: removed_tags.map(&:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
36:         featured_tag.decrement(@status)
37:       end
38:     end
39:   end
40: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ProcessLinksService < BaseService
4:   include Payloadable
5: 
6:   # Scan status for links to ActivityPub objects and attach them to statuses
7:   # @param [Status] status
8:   def call(status)
9:     return unless status.local?
10: 
11:     @status = status
12:     @previous_objects = @status.tagged_objects.includes(:object).to_a
13:     @current_objects  = []
14: 
15:     Status.transaction do
16:       scan_text!
17:       assign_tagged_objects!
18:     end
19:   end
20: 
21:   private
22: 
23:   def scan_text!
24:     urls = @status.text.scan(FetchLinkCardService::URL_PATTERN).map { |array| Addressable::URI.parse(array[1]).normalize }
25: 
26:     domains = urls.map(&:host).uniq
27:     valid_domains = Instance.searchable.where(domain: domains).pluck(:domain)
28: 
29:     urls.each do |url|
30:       # We only support `FeaturedCollection` at this time
31: 
32:       object = ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(url.to_s, Collection)
33:       object ||= ResolveURLService.new.call(url.to_s) if valid_domains.include?(url.host)
34:       next unless object.is_a?(Collection)
35: 
36:       tagged_object = @previous_objects.find { |x| x.object == object || x.uri == url }
37:       tagged_object ||= @current_objects.find { |x| x.object == object || x.uri == url }
38:       tagged_object ||= @status.tagged_objects.new(object: object, ap_type: 'FeaturedCollection', uri: ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(object))
39: 
40:       @current_objects << tagged_object
41:     end
42:   end
43: 
44:   def assign_tagged_objects!
45:     return unless @status.persisted?
46: 
47:     @current_objects.each do |object|
48:       object.save if object.new_record?
49:     end
50: 
51:     # If previous objects are no longer contained in the text, remove them to lighten the database
52:     removed_objects = @previous_objects - @current_objects
53: 
54:     TaggedObject.where(id: removed_objects.map(&:id)).delete_all unless removed_objects.empty?
55:   end
56: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ActivityPub::ProcessStatusUpdateService < BaseService
4:   include JsonLdHelper
5:   include Redisable
6:   include Lockable
7: 
8:   CRAWL_DELAY = 1.minute
9:   PROCESSING_DELAY = (30.seconds)..(10.minutes)
10: 
11:   def call(status, activity_json, object_json, request_id: nil)
12:     raise ArgumentError, 'Status has unsaved changes' if status.changed?
13: 
14:     @activity_json             = activity_json
15:     @json                      = object_json
16:     @status_parser             = ActivityPub::Parser::StatusParser.new(@json, followers_collection: status.account.followers_url, following_collection: status.account.following_url, actor_uri: ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(status.account))
17:     @uri                       = @status_parser.uri
18:     @status                    = status
19:     @account                   = status.account
20:     @media_attachments_changed = false
21:     @poll_changed              = false
22:     @quote_changed             = false
23:     @request_id                = request_id
24:     @quote                     = nil
25: 
26:     return @status if !expected_type? || already_updated_more_recently?
27: 
28:     if @status_parser.edited_at.present? && (@status.edited_at.nil? || @status_parser.edited_at > @status.edited_at)
29:       handle_explicit_update!
30:     elsif @status.edited_at.present? && (@status_parser.edited_at.nil? || @status_parser.edited_at < @status.edited_at)
31:       # This is an older update, reject it
32:       return @status
33:     else
34:       handle_implicit_update!
35:     end
36: 
37:     @status
38:   end
39: 
40:   private
41: 
42:   def handle_explicit_update!
43:     last_edit_date = @status.edited_at.presence || @status.created_at
44: 
45:     # Only allow processing one create/update per status at a time
46:     with_redis_lock("create:#{@uri}") do
47:       Status.transaction do
48:         record_previous_edit!
49:         update_media_attachments!
50:         update_interaction_policies!
51:         update_poll!
52:         update_immediate_attributes!
53:         update_metadata!
54:         update_counts!
55:         create_edits!
56:       end
57: 
58:       fetch_and_verify_quote!(@quote, @quote_approval_uri, @status_parser.quote_uri) if @quote.present?
59:       download_media_files!
60:       queue_poll_notifications!
61: 
62:       next unless significant_changes?
63: 
64:       reset_preview_card!
65:       broadcast_updates!
66:     end
67: 
68:     forward_activity! if significant_changes? && @status_parser.edited_at > last_edit_date
69:   end
70: 
71:   def handle_implicit_update!
72:     with_redis_lock("create:#{@uri}") do
73:       update_interaction_policies!
74:       update_poll!(allow_significant_changes: false)
75:       queue_poll_notifications!
76:       update_quote_approval!
77:       update_counts!
78:     end
79: 
80:     broadcast_updates! if @status.quote&.state_previously_changed?
81:   end
82: 
83:   def update_interaction_policies!
84:     @status.update(quote_approval_policy: @status_parser.quote_policy)
85:   end
86: 
87:   def update_media_attachments!
88:     previous_media_attachments     = @status.media_attachments.to_a
89:     previous_media_attachments_ids = @status.ordered_media_attachment_ids || previous_media_attachments.map(&:id)
90:     @next_media_attachments        = []
91: 
92:     as_array(@json['attachment']).each do |attachment|
93:       media_attachment_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::MediaAttachmentParser.new(attachment)
94: 
95:       next if media_attachment_parser.remote_url.blank? || @next_media_attachments.size > Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT
96: 
97:       begin
98:         media_attachment   = previous_media_attachments.find { |previous_media_attachment| previous_media_attachment.remote_url == media_attachment_parser.remote_url }
99:         media_attachment ||= MediaAttachment.new(account: @account, remote_url: media_attachment_parser.remote_url)
100: 
101:         # If a previously existing media attachment was significantly updated, mark
102:         # media attachments as changed even if none were added or removed
103:         @media_attachments_changed = true if media_attachment_parser.significantly_changes?(media_attachment)
104: 
105:         media_attachment.description          = media_attachment_parser.description
106:         media_attachment.focus                = media_attachment_parser.focus
107:         media_attachment.thumbnail_remote_url = media_attachment_parser.thumbnail_remote_url
108:         media_attachment.blurhash             = media_attachment_parser.blurhash
109:         media_attachment.status_id            = @status.id
110:         media_attachment.skip_download        = unsupported_media_type?(media_attachment_parser.file_content_type) || skip_download?
111:         media_attachment.save!
112: 
113:         @next_media_attachments << media_attachment
114:       rescue Addressable::URI::InvalidURIError => e
115:         Rails.logger.debug { "Invalid URL in attachment: #{e}" }
116:       end
117:     end
118: 
119:     @status.ordered_media_attachment_ids = @next_media_attachments.map(&:id)
120: 
121:     @media_attachments_changed = true if @status.ordered_media_attachment_ids != previous_media_attachments_ids
122: 
123:     @status.media_attachments.reload if @media_attachments_changed
124:   end
125: 
126:   def download_media_files!
127:     @next_media_attachments.each do |media_attachment|
128:       next if media_attachment.skip_download
129: 
130:       media_attachment.download_file! if media_attachment.remote_url_previously_changed?
131:       media_attachment.download_thumbnail! if media_attachment.thumbnail_remote_url_previously_changed?
132:       media_attachment.save
133:     rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
134:       RedownloadMediaWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), media_attachment.id)
135:     rescue Seahorse::Client::NetworkingError => e
136:       Rails.logger.warn "Error storing media attachment: #{e}"
137:     end
138: 
139:     @status.media_attachments.reload
140:   end
141: 
142:   def update_poll!(allow_significant_changes: true)
143:     previous_poll        = @status.preloadable_poll
144:     @previous_expires_at = previous_poll&.expires_at
145:     poll_parser          = ActivityPub::Parser::PollParser.new(@json)
146: 
147:     if poll_parser.valid?
148:       poll = previous_poll || @account.polls.new(status: @status)
149: 
150:       # If for some reasons the options were changed, it invalidates all previous
151:       # votes, so we need to remove them
152:       @poll_changed = true if poll_parser.significantly_changes?(poll)
153:       return if @poll_changed && !allow_significant_changes
154: 
155:       poll.last_fetched_at = Time.now.utc
156:       poll.options         = poll_parser.options
157:       poll.multiple        = poll_parser.multiple
158:       poll.expires_at      = poll_parser.expires_at
159:       poll.voters_count    = poll_parser.voters_count
160:       poll.cached_tallies  = poll_parser.cached_tallies
161:       poll.reset_votes! if @poll_changed
162:       poll.save!
163: 
164:       @status.poll_id = poll.id
165:     elsif previous_poll.present?
166:       return unless allow_significant_changes
167: 
168:       previous_poll.destroy!
169:       @poll_changed = true
170:       @status.poll_id = nil
171:     end
172:   end
173: 
174:   def update_immediate_attributes!
175:     @status.text         = @status_parser.processed_text
176:     @status.spoiler_text = @status_parser.processed_spoiler_text
177:     @status.sensitive    = @account.sensitized? || @status_parser.sensitive || false
178:     @status.language     = @status_parser.language
179: 
180:     @significant_changes = text_significantly_changed? || @status.spoiler_text_changed? || @media_attachments_changed || @poll_changed || @quote_changed
181: 
182:     @status.edited_at = @status_parser.edited_at if significant_changes?
183: 
184:     @status.save!
185:   end
186: 
187:   def update_metadata!
188:     @raw_tags = []
189:     @raw_mentions = []
190:     @raw_tagged_objects = []
191:     @raw_emojis = []
192: 
193:     as_array(@json['tag']).each do |tag|
194:       if equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Hashtag')
195:         @raw_tags << tag['name'] if tag['name'].present?
196:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Mention')
197:         @raw_mentions << tag['href'] if tag['href'].present?
198:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Emoji')
199:         @raw_emojis << tag
200:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'FeaturedCollection')
201:         @raw_tagged_objects << tag if tag['id']
202:       end
203:     end
204: 
205:     update_tags!
206:     update_tagged_objects!
207:     update_mentions!
208:     update_emojis!
209:     update_quote!
210:   end
211: 
212:   def update_tags!
213:     previous_tags = @status.tags.to_a
214:     current_tags = @status.tags = @raw_tags.flat_map do |tag|
215:       Tag.find_or_create_by_names([tag])
216:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
217:       []
218:     end.uniq
219: 
220:     return unless @status.distributable?
221: 
222:     added_tags = current_tags - previous_tags
223: 
224:     unless added_tags.empty?
225:       @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: added_tags.pluck(:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
226:         featured_tag.increment(@status.created_at)
227:       end
228:     end
229: 
230:     removed_tags = previous_tags - current_tags
231: 
232:     unless removed_tags.empty?
233:       @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: removed_tags.pluck(:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
234:         featured_tag.decrement(@status)
235:       end
236:     end
237:   end
238: 
239:   def update_tagged_objects!
240:     unresolved_tagged_objects = []
241: 
242:     current_tagged_objects = @raw_tagged_objects.filter_map do |tagged_object|
243:       url = tagged_object['id']
244: 
245:       # TODO: We probably want to resolve unknown objects at authoring time
246:       collection = ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(url, Collection)
247:       collection ||= ActivityPub::FetchRemoteFeaturedCollectionService.new.call(url, request_id: @request_id)
248: 
249:       collection
250:     rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
251:       # Since previous tagged objects are about already-known collections,
252:       # they don't try to resolve again and won't fall into this case.
253:       # In other words, this failure case is only for new collections and can safely be retried later
254:       unresolved_tagged_objects << url
255:       nil
256:     end
257: 
258:     # Any previously-unresolved URI would be resolved here
259:     @status.tagged_objects.upsert_all(
260:       current_tagged_objects.uniq.map { |object| { object_type: object.class.name, object_id: object.id, uri: ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(object), ap_type: 'FeaturedCollection' } },
261:       unique_by: %w(status_id uri)
262:     )
263: 
264:     # Remove unused links
265:     @status.tagged_objects.where.not(uri: current_tagged_objects.map { |object| ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(object) }).delete_all
266: 
267:     # Queue unresolved collections for later
268:     unresolved_tagged_objects.uniq.each do |uri|
269:       TaggedCollectionResolveWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), @status.id, uri, { 'request_id' => @request_id })
270:     end
271:   end
272: 
273:   def update_mentions!
274:     unresolved_mentions = []
275: 
276:     currently_mentioned_account_ids = @raw_mentions.filter_map do |href|
277:       next if href.blank?
278: 
279:       account   = ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(href, Account)
280:       account ||= ActivityPub::FetchRemoteAccountService.new.call(href, request_id: @request_id)
281: 
282:       account&.id
283:     rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
284:       # Since previous mentions are about already-known accounts,
285:       # they don't try to resolve again and won't fall into this case.
286:       # In other words, this failure case is only for new mentions and won't
287:       # affect `removed_mentions` so they can safely be retried asynchronously
288:       unresolved_mentions << href
289:       nil
290:     end
291: 
292:     @status.mentions.upsert_all(currently_mentioned_account_ids.uniq.map { |id| { account_id: id, silent: false } }, unique_by: %w(status_id account_id))
293: 
294:     # If previous mentions are no longer contained in the text, convert them
295:     # to silent mentions, since withdrawing access from someone who already
296:     # received a notification might be more confusing
297:     @status.mentions.where.not(account_id: currently_mentioned_account_ids).update_all(silent: true)
298: 
299:     # Queue unresolved mentions for later
300:     unresolved_mentions.uniq.each do |uri|
301:       MentionResolveWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), @status.id, uri, { 'request_id' => @request_id })
302:     end
303:   end
304: 
305:   def update_emojis!
306:     return if skip_download?
307: 
308:     @raw_emojis.each do |raw_emoji|
309:       custom_emoji_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::CustomEmojiParser.new(raw_emoji)
310: 
311:       next if custom_emoji_parser.shortcode.blank? || custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url.blank?
312: 
313:       emoji = CustomEmoji.find_by(shortcode: custom_emoji_parser.shortcode, domain: @account.domain)
314: 
315:       next unless emoji.nil? || custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url != emoji.image_remote_url || (custom_emoji_parser.updated_at && custom_emoji_parser.updated_at >= emoji.updated_at)
316: 
317:       begin
318:         emoji ||= CustomEmoji.new(domain: @account.domain, shortcode: custom_emoji_parser.shortcode, uri: custom_emoji_parser.uri)
319:         emoji.image_remote_url = custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url
320:         emoji.save
321:       rescue Seahorse::Client::NetworkingError => e
322:         Rails.logger.warn "Error storing emoji: #{e}"
323:       end
324:     end
325:   end
326: 
327:   # This method is only concerned with approval and skips other meaningful changes,
328:   # as it is used instead of `update_quote!` in implicit updates
329:   def update_quote_approval!
330:     quote_uri = @status_parser.quote_uri
331:     return unless quote_uri.present? && @status.quote.present?
332: 
333:     quote = @status.quote
334:     return if quote.quoted_status.present? && (ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(quote.quoted_status) != quote_uri || quote.quoted_status.local?)
335: 
336:     approval_uri = @status_parser.quote_approval_uri
337:     approval_uri = nil if unsupported_uri_scheme?(approval_uri) || TagManager.instance.local_url?(approval_uri)
338: 
339:     quote.update(approval_uri: nil, state: :pending, legacy: @status_parser.legacy_quote?) if quote.approval_uri.present? && quote.approval_uri != @status_parser.quote_approval_uri
340: 
341:     fetch_and_verify_quote!(quote, approval_uri, quote_uri)
342:   end
343: 
344:   def update_quote!
345:     quote_uri = @status_parser.quote_uri
346: 
347:     if @status_parser.quote?
348:       approval_uri = @status_parser.quote_approval_uri
349:       approval_uri = nil if unsupported_uri_scheme?(approval_uri) || TagManager.instance.local_url?(approval_uri)
350: 
351:       if @status.quote.present?
352:         # If the quoted post has changed, discard the old object and create a new one
353:         if @status.quote.quoted_status.present? && ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(@status.quote.quoted_status) != quote_uri
354:           # Revoke the quote while we get a chance… maybe this should be a `before_destroy` hook?
355:           RevokeQuoteService.new.call(@status.quote) if @status.quote.quoted_account&.local? && @status.quote.accepted?
356:           @status.quote.destroy
357:           quote = Quote.create(status: @status, approval_uri: nil, legacy: @status_parser.legacy_quote?, state: @status_parser.deleted_quote? ? :deleted : :pending)
358:           @quote_changed = true
359:         else
360:           quote = @status.quote
361:           quote.update(approval_uri: nil, state: :pending, legacy: @status_parser.legacy_quote?) if quote.approval_uri.present? && quote.approval_uri != approval_uri
362:         end
363:       else
364:         quote = Quote.create(status: @status, approval_uri: nil, legacy: @status_parser.legacy_quote?)
365:         @quote_changed = true
366:       end
367: 
368:       @quote = quote
369:       @quote_approval_uri = approval_uri
370: 
371:       quote.save
372:     elsif @status.quote.present?
373:       @quote = nil
374:       @status.quote.destroy!
375:       @quote_changed = true
376:     end
377:   end
378: 
379:   def fetch_and_verify_quote!(quote, approval_uri, quote_uri)
380:     embedded_quote = safe_prefetched_embed(@account, @status_parser.quoted_object, @activity_json['context'])
381:     ActivityPub::VerifyQuoteService.new.call(quote, approval_uri, fetchable_quoted_uri: quote_uri, prefetched_quoted_object: embedded_quote, request_id: @request_id)
382:   rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
383:     ActivityPub::RefetchAndVerifyQuoteWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), quote.id, quote_uri, { 'request_id' => @request_id, 'approval_uri' => approval_uri })
384:   end
385: 
386:   def update_counts!
387:     likes = @status_parser.favourites_count
388:     shares =  @status_parser.reblogs_count
389:     return if likes.nil? && shares.nil?
390: 
391:     @status.status_stat.tap do |status_stat|
392:       status_stat.untrusted_reblogs_count = shares unless shares.nil?
393:       status_stat.untrusted_favourites_count = likes unless likes.nil?
394: 
395:       status_stat.save if status_stat.changed?
396:     end
397:   end
398: 
399:   def expected_type?
400:     equals_or_includes_any?(@json['type'], ActivityPub::Activity::SUPPORTED_TYPES) || equals_or_includes_any?(@json['type'], ActivityPub::Activity::CONVERTED_TYPES)
401:   end
402: 
403:   def record_previous_edit!
404:     @previous_edit = @status.build_snapshot(at_time: @status.created_at, rate_limit: false) if @status.edits.empty?
405:   end
406: 
407:   def create_edits!
408:     return unless significant_changes?
409: 
410:     @previous_edit&.save!
411:     @status.snapshot!(account_id: @account.id, rate_limit: false)
412:   end
413: 
414:   def skip_download?
415:     return @skip_download if defined?(@skip_download)
416: 
417:     @skip_download ||= DomainBlock.reject_media?(@account.domain)
418:   end
419: 
420:   def unsupported_media_type?(mime_type)
421:     mime_type.present? && !MediaAttachment.supported_mime_types.include?(mime_type)
422:   end
423: 
424:   def significant_changes?
425:     @significant_changes
426:   end
427: 
428:   def text_significantly_changed?
429:     return false unless @status.text_changed?
430: 
431:     old, new = @status.text_change
432:     HtmlAwareFormatter.new(old, false).to_s != HtmlAwareFormatter.new(new, false).to_s
433:   end
434: 
435:   def already_updated_more_recently?
436:     @status.edited_at.present? && @status_parser.edited_at.present? && @status.edited_at > @status_parser.edited_at
437:   end
438: 
439:   def reset_preview_card!
440:     @status.reset_preview_card!
441:     LinkCrawlWorker.perform_in(rand(CRAWL_DELAY), @status.id)
442:   end
443: 
444:   def broadcast_updates!
445:     ::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id, { 'update' => true })
446:   end
447: 
448:   def queue_poll_notifications!
449:     poll = @status.preloadable_poll
450: 
451:     # If the poll had no expiration date set but now has, or now has a sooner
452:     # expiration date, and people have voted, schedule a notification
453: 
454:     return unless poll.present? && poll.expires_at.present? && poll.votes.exists?
455: 
456:     # If the poll had previously expired, notifications should have already been sent out (or scheduled),
457:     # and re-scheduling them would cause duplicate notifications for people who had already dismissed them
458:     # (see #37948)
459:     return if @previous_expires_at&.past?
460: 
461:     PollExpirationNotifyWorker.remove_from_scheduled(poll.id) if @previous_expires_at.present? && @previous_expires_at > poll.expires_at
462:     PollExpirationNotifyWorker.perform_at(poll.expires_at + 5.minutes, poll.id)
463:   end
464: 
465:   def forward_activity!
466:     forwarder.forward! if forwarder.forwardable?
467:   end
468: 
469:   def forwarder
470:     @forwarder ||= ActivityPub::Forwarder.new(@account, @activity_json, @status)
471:   end
472: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ActivityPub::FetchRemoteStatusService < BaseService
4:   include JsonLdHelper
5:   include DomainControlHelper
6:   include Redisable
7: 
8:   DISCOVERIES_PER_REQUEST = 1000
9: 
10:   # Should be called when uri has already been checked for locality
11:   def call(uri, prefetched_body: nil, on_behalf_of: nil, expected_actor_uri: nil, request_id: nil, depth: nil)
12:     return if domain_not_allowed?(uri)
13: 
14:     @depth = depth || 0
15:     @request_id = request_id || "#{Time.now.utc.to_i}-status-#{uri}"
16:     @json = if prefetched_body.nil?
17:               fetch_status(uri, true, on_behalf_of)
18:             else
19:               body_to_json(prefetched_body, compare_id: uri)
20:             end
21: 
22:     return unless supported_context?
23: 
24:     actor_uri     = nil
25:     activity_json = nil
26:     object_uri    = nil
27: 
28:     if expected_object_type?
29:       actor_uri     = value_or_id(first_of_value(@json['attributedTo']))
30:       activity_json = { 'type' => 'Create', 'actor' => actor_uri, 'object' => @json }
31:       object_uri    = uri_from_bearcap(@json['id'])
32:     elsif expected_activity_type?
33:       actor_uri     = value_or_id(first_of_value(@json['actor']))
34:       activity_json = @json
35:       object_uri    = uri_from_bearcap(value_or_id(@json['object']))
36:     end
37: 
38:     return if activity_json.nil? || object_uri.nil? || !trustworthy_attribution?(@json['id'], actor_uri)
39:     return if expected_actor_uri.present? && actor_uri != expected_actor_uri
40:     return ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(object_uri, Status) if ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.local_uri?(object_uri)
41: 
42:     actor = account_from_uri(actor_uri)
43: 
44:     return if actor.nil? || actor.suspended?
45: 
46:     # If we fetched a status that already exists, then we need to treat the
47:     # activity as an update rather than create
48:     activity_json['type'] = 'Update' if equals_or_includes_any?(activity_json['type'], %w(Create)) && Status.exists?(uri: object_uri, account_id: actor.id)
49: 
50:     with_redis do |redis|
51:       discoveries = redis.incr("status_discovery_per_request:#{@request_id}")
52:       redis.expire("status_discovery_per_request:#{@request_id}", 5.minutes.seconds)
53:       return nil if discoveries > DISCOVERIES_PER_REQUEST
54:     end
55: 
56:     ActivityPub::Activity.factory(activity_json, actor, request_id: @request_id, depth: @depth).perform
57:   end
58: 
59:   private
60: 
61:   def trustworthy_attribution?(uri, attributed_to)
62:     return false if uri.nil? || attributed_to.nil?
63:     return false if unsupported_uri_scheme?(uri) || unsupported_uri_scheme?(attributed_to)
64: 
65:     Addressable::URI.parse(uri).normalized_host.casecmp(Addressable::URI.parse(attributed_to).normalized_host).zero?
66:   end
67: 
68:   def account_from_uri(uri)
69:     actor = ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(uri, Account)
70:     actor = ActivityPub::FetchRemoteAccountService.new.call(uri, request_id: @request_id) if actor.nil? || actor.possibly_stale?
71:     actor
72:   end
73: 
74:   def supported_context?
75:     super(@json)
76:   end
77: 
78:   def expected_activity_type?
79:     equals_or_includes_any?(@json['type'], %w(Create Announce))
80:   end
81: 
82:   def expected_object_type?
83:     equals_or_includes_any?(@json['type'], ActivityPub::Activity::Create::SUPPORTED_TYPES + ActivityPub::Activity::Create::CONVERTED_TYPES)
84:   end
85: 
86:   def fetch_status(uri, id_is_known, on_behalf_of = nil)
87:     begin
88:       fetch_resource(uri, id_is_known, on_behalf_of, raise_on_error: :all)
89:     rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError => e
90:       return unless e.response.code == 404
91: 
92:       # If this is a 404 from a public status from a remote account, delete it
93:       existing_status = Status.remote.find_by(uri: uri)
94:       if existing_status&.distributable?
95:         Rails.logger.debug { "FetchRemoteStatusService - Got 404 for orphaned status with URI #{uri}, deleting" }
96:         RemoveStatusService.new.call(existing_status, redraft: false)
97:       end
98:     end
99:   end
100: end

(End of file - total 100 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class AccountStatusesCleanupService < BaseService
4:   # @param [AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy] account_policy
5:   # @param [Integer] budget
6:   # @return [Integer]
7:   def call(account_policy, budget = 50)
8:     return 0 unless account_policy.enabled?
9: 
10:     cutoff_id = account_policy.compute_cutoff_id
11:     return 0 if cutoff_id.blank?
12: 
13:     num_deleted = 0
14:     last_deleted = nil
15: 
16:     account_policy.statuses_to_delete(budget, cutoff_id, account_policy.last_inspected).reorder(nil).find_each(order: :asc) do |status|
17:       status.discard_with_reblogs
18:       RemovalWorker.perform_async(status.id, { 'redraft' => false })
19:       num_deleted += 1
20:       last_deleted = status.id
21:     end
22: 
23:     account_policy.record_last_inspected(last_deleted.presence || cutoff_id)
24: 
25:     num_deleted
26:   end
27: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class BatchedRemoveStatusService < BaseService
4:   include Redisable
5: 
6:   # Delete multiple statuses and reblogs of them as efficiently as possible
7:   # @param [Enumerable<Status>] statuses An array of statuses
8:   # @param [Hash] options
9:   # @option [Boolean] :skip_side_effects Do not modify feeds and send updates to streaming API
10:   def call(statuses, **options)
11:     ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
12:       records: statuses,
13:       associations: options[:skip_side_effects] ? :reblogs : [:account, :tags, reblogs: :account]
14:     ).call
15: 
16:     statuses_and_reblogs = statuses.flat_map { |status| [status] + status.reblogs }
17: 
18:     # The conversations for direct visibility statuses also need
19:     # to be manually updated. This part is not efficient but we
20:     # rely on direct visibility statuses being relatively rare.
21:     statuses_with_account_conversations = statuses.select(&:direct_visibility?)
22: 
23:     ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
24:       records: statuses_with_account_conversations,
25:       associations: [mentions: :account]
26:     ).call
27: 
28:     statuses_with_account_conversations.each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
29: 
30:     # We do not batch all deletes into one to avoid having a long-running
31:     # transaction lock the database, but we use the delete method instead
32:     # of destroy to avoid all callbacks. We rely on foreign keys to
33:     # cascade the delete faster without loading the associations.
34:     statuses_and_reblogs.each_slice(50) { |slice| Status.unscoped.where(id: slice.pluck(:id)).delete_all }
35: 
36:     # Since we skipped all callbacks, we also need to manually
37:     # deindex the statuses
38:     if Chewy.enabled?
39:       Chewy.strategy.current.update(StatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
40:       Chewy.strategy.current.update(PublicStatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
41:     end
42: 
43:     return if options[:skip_side_effects]
44: 
45:     # Batch by source account
46:     statuses_and_reblogs.group_by(&:account_id).each_value do |account_statuses|
47:       account = account_statuses.first.account
48: 
49:       next unless account
50: 
51:       unpush_from_home_timelines(account, account_statuses)
52:       unpush_from_list_timelines(account, account_statuses)
53:     end
54: 
55:     # Cannot be batched
56:     @status_id_cutoff = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(2.weeks.ago)
57:     redis.pipelined do |pipeline|
58:       statuses.each do |status|
59:         unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
60:       end
61:     end
62:   end
63: 
64:   private
65: 
66:   def unpush_from_home_timelines(account, statuses)
67:     account.followers_for_local_distribution.includes(:user).reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
68:       statuses.each do |status|
69:         FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(follower, status)
70:       end
71:     end
72:   end
73: 
74:   def unpush_from_list_timelines(account, statuses)
75:     account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id, :account_id).includes(account: :user).reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
76:       statuses.each do |status|
77:         FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_list(list, status)
78:       end
79:     end
80:   end
81: 
82:   def unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
83:     return unless status.public_visibility? && status.id > @status_id_cutoff
84: 
85:     payload = { event: :delete, payload: status.id.to_s }.to_json
86: 
87:     pipeline.publish('timeline:public', payload)
88:     pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', payload)
89: 
90:     if status.media_attachments.any?
91:       pipeline.publish('timeline:public:media', payload)
92:       pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', payload)
93:     end
94: 
95:     status.tags.map { |tag| tag.name.downcase }.each do |hashtag|
96:       pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}", payload)
97:       pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}:local", payload) if status.local?
98:     end
99:   end
100: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class TranslateStatusService < BaseService
4:   CACHE_TTL = 1.day.freeze
5: 
6:   include ERB::Util
7:   include FormattingHelper
8: 
9:   def call(status, target_language)
10:     @status = status
11:     @source_texts = source_texts
12: 
13:     target_language = target_language.split(/[_-]/).first unless target_languages.include?(target_language)
14:     @target_language = target_language
15: 
16:     raise Mastodon::NotPermittedError unless permitted?
17: 
18:     status_translation = Rails.cache.fetch("v2:translations/#{@status.language}/#{@target_language}/#{content_hash}", expires_in: CACHE_TTL) do
19:       translations = translation_backend.translate(@source_texts.values, @status.language, @target_language)
20:       build_status_translation(translations)
21:     end
22: 
23:     status_translation.status = @status
24: 
25:     status_translation
26:   end
27: 
28:   private
29: 
30:   def translation_backend
31:     @translation_backend ||= TranslationService.configured
32:   end
33: 
34:   def permitted?
35:     return false unless @status.distributable? && TranslationService.configured?
36: 
37:     target_languages.include?(@target_language)
38:   end
39: 
40:   def languages
41:     Rails.cache.fetch('translation_service/languages', expires_in: 7.days, race_condition_ttl: 1.hour) { translation_backend.languages }
42:   end
43: 
44:   def target_languages
45:     languages[@status.language] || []
46:   end
47: 
48:   def content_hash
49:     Digest::SHA256.base64digest(@source_texts.transform_keys { |key| key.respond_to?(:id) ? "#{key.class}-#{key.id}" : key }.to_json)
50:   end
51: 
52:   def source_texts
53:     texts = {}
54:     texts[:content] = wrap_emoji_shortcodes(status_content_format(@status)) if @status.content.present?
55:     texts[:spoiler_text] = wrap_emoji_shortcodes(html_escape(@status.spoiler_text)) if @status.spoiler_text.present?
56: 
57:     @status.preloadable_poll&.loaded_options&.each do |option|
58:       texts[option] = wrap_emoji_shortcodes(html_escape(option.title))
59:     end
60: 
61:     @status.media_attachments.each do |media_attachment|
62:       texts[media_attachment] = html_escape(media_attachment.description)
63:     end
64: 
65:     texts
66:   end
67: 
68:   def build_status_translation(translations)
69:     status_translation = Translation.new(
70:       detected_source_language: translations.first&.detected_source_language,
71:       language: @target_language,
72:       provider: translations.first&.provider,
73:       content: '',
74:       spoiler_text: '',
75:       poll_options: [],
76:       media_attachments: []
77:     )
78: 
79:     @source_texts.keys.each_with_index do |source, index|
80:       translation = translations[index]
81: 
82:       case source
83:       when :content
84:         node = unwrap_emoji_shortcodes(translation.text)
85:         Sanitize.node!(node, Sanitize::Config::MASTODON_STRICT)
86:         status_translation.content = node.to_html
87:       when :spoiler_text
