<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Schema Information
4: #
5: # Table name: account_statuses_cleanup_policies
6: #
7: #  id                 :bigint(8)        not null, primary key
8: #  enabled            :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
9: #  keep_direct        :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
10: #  keep_media         :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
11: #  keep_pinned        :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
12: #  keep_polls         :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
13: #  keep_self_bookmark :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
14: #  keep_self_fav      :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
15: #  min_favs           :integer
16: #  min_reblogs        :integer
17: #  min_status_age     :integer          default(1209600), not null
18: #  created_at         :datetime         not null
19: #  updated_at         :datetime         not null
20: #  account_id         :bigint(8)        not null
21: #
22: class AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy < ApplicationRecord
23:   include Redisable
24: 
25:   ALLOWED_MIN_STATUS_AGE = [
26:     1.week.seconds,
27:     2.weeks.seconds,
28:     1.month.seconds,
29:     2.months.seconds,
30:     3.months.seconds,
31:     6.months.seconds,
32:     1.year.seconds,
33:     2.years.seconds,
34:   ].freeze
35: 
36:   EXCEPTION_BOOLS      = %w(keep_direct keep_pinned keep_polls keep_media keep_self_fav keep_self_bookmark).freeze
37:   EXCEPTION_THRESHOLDS = %w(min_favs min_reblogs).freeze
38: 
39:   # Depending on the cleanup policy, the query to discover the next
40:   # statuses to delete my get expensive if the account has a lot of old
41:   # statuses otherwise excluded from deletion by the other exceptions.
42:   #
43:   # Therefore, `EARLY_SEARCH_CUTOFF` is meant to be the maximum number of
44:   # old statuses to be considered for deletion prior to checking exceptions.
45:   #
46:   # This is used in `compute_cutoff_id` to provide a `max_id` to
47:   # `statuses_to_delete`.
48:   EARLY_SEARCH_CUTOFF = 5_000
49: 
50:   belongs_to :account
51: 
52:   validates :min_status_age, inclusion: { in: ALLOWED_MIN_STATUS_AGE }
53:   validates :min_favs, numericality: { greater_than_or_equal_to: 1, allow_nil: true }
54:   validates :min_reblogs, numericality: { greater_than_or_equal_to: 1, allow_nil: true }
55:   validate :validate_local_account
56: 
57:   before_save :update_last_inspected
58: 
59:   def statuses_to_delete(limit = 50, max_id = nil, min_id = nil)
60:     scope = account_statuses
61:     scope.merge!(old_enough_scope(max_id))
62:     scope = scope.where(id: min_id..) if min_id.present?
63:     scope.merge!(without_popular_scope) unless min_favs.nil? && min_reblogs.nil?
64:     scope.merge!(without_direct_scope) if keep_direct?
65:     scope.merge!(without_pinned_scope) if keep_pinned?
66:     scope.merge!(without_poll_scope) if keep_polls?
67:     scope.merge!(without_media_scope) if keep_media?
68:     scope.merge!(without_self_fav_scope) if keep_self_fav?
69:     scope.merge!(without_self_bookmark_scope) if keep_self_bookmark?
70: 
71:     scope.reorder(id: :asc).limit(limit)
72:   end
73: 
74:   # This computes a toot id such that:
75:   # - the toot would be old enough to be candidate for deletion
76:   # - there are at most EARLY_SEARCH_CUTOFF toots between the last inspected toot and this one
77:   #
78:   # The idea is to limit expensive SQL queries when an account has lots of toots excluded from
79:   # deletion, while not starting anew on each run.
80:   def compute_cutoff_id
81:     min_id = last_inspected || 0
82:     max_id = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(min_status_age.seconds.ago, with_random: false)
83:     subquery = account_statuses.where(id: min_id..max_id)
84:     subquery = subquery.select(:id).reorder(id: :asc).limit(EARLY_SEARCH_CUTOFF)
85: 
86:     # We're textually interpolating a subquery here as ActiveRecord seem to not provide
87:     # a way to apply the limit to the subquery
88:     Status.connection.execute("SELECT MAX(id) FROM (#{subquery.to_sql}) t").values.first.first
89:   end
90: 
91:   # The most important thing about `last_inspected` is that any toot older than it is guaranteed
92:   # not to be kept by the policy regardless of its age.
93:   def record_last_inspected(last_id)
94:     redis.set("account_cleanup:#{account_id}", last_id, ex: 2.weeks.seconds)
95:   end
96: 
97:   def last_inspected
98:     redis.get("account_cleanup:#{account_id}")&.to_i
99:   end
100: 
101:   def invalidate_last_inspected(status, action)
102:     last_value = last_inspected
103:     return if last_value.nil? || status.id > last_value || status.account_id != account_id
104: 
105:     case action
106:     when :unbookmark
107:       return unless keep_self_bookmark?
108:     when :unfav
109:       return unless keep_self_fav?
110:     when :unpin
111:       return unless keep_pinned?
112:     end
113: 
114:     record_last_inspected(status.id)
115:   end
116: 
117:   private
118: 
119:   def update_last_inspected
120:     if EXCEPTION_BOOLS.filter_map { |name| attribute_change_to_be_saved(name) }.include?([true, false])
121:       # Policy has been widened in such a way that any previously-inspected status
122:       # may need to be deleted, so we'll have to start again.
123:       redis.del("account_cleanup:#{account_id}")
124:     end
125:     redis.del("account_cleanup:#{account_id}") if EXCEPTION_THRESHOLDS.filter_map { |name| attribute_change_to_be_saved(name) }.any? { |old, new| old.present? && (new.nil? || new > old) }
126:   end
127: 
128:   def validate_local_account
129:     errors.add(:account, :invalid) unless account&.local?
130:   end
131: 
132:   def without_direct_scope
133:     Status.not_direct_visibility
134:   end
135: 
136:   def old_enough_scope(max_id = nil)
137:     # Filtering on `id` rather than `min_status_age` ago will treat
138:     # non-snowflake statuses as older than they really are, but Mastodon
139:     # has switched to snowflake IDs significantly over 2 years ago anyway.
140:     snowflake_id = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(min_status_age.seconds.ago, with_random: false)
141: 
142:     max_id = snowflake_id if max_id.nil? || snowflake_id < max_id
143: 
144:     Status.where(id: ..max_id)
145:   end
146: 
147:   def without_self_fav_scope
148:     Status.where.not(self_status_reference_exists(Favourite))
149:   end
150: 
151:   def without_self_bookmark_scope
152:     Status.where.not(self_status_reference_exists(Bookmark))
153:   end
154: 
155:   def without_pinned_scope
156:     Status.where.not(self_status_reference_exists(StatusPin))
157:   end
158: 
159:   def without_media_scope
160:     Status.where.not(status_media_reference_exists)
161:   end
162: 
163:   def without_poll_scope
164:     Status.without_polls
165:   end
166: 
167:   def without_popular_scope
168:     scope = Status.left_joins(:status_stat)
169:     scope = scope.where('COALESCE(status_stats.reblogs_count, 0) < ?', min_reblogs) unless min_reblogs.nil?
170:     scope = scope.where('COALESCE(status_stats.favourites_count, 0) < ?', min_favs) unless min_favs.nil?
171:     scope
172:   end
173: 
174:   def account_statuses
175:     Status.where(account_id: account_id)
176:   end
177: 
178:   def status_media_reference_exists
179:     MediaAttachment
180:       .where(MediaAttachment.arel_table[:status_id].eq Status.arel_table[:id])
181:       .select(1)
182:       .arel
183:       .exists
184:   end
185: 
186:   def self_status_reference_exists(model)
187:     model
188:       .where(model.arel_table[:account_id].eq Status.arel_table[:account_id])
189:       .where(model.arel_table[:status_id].eq Status.arel_table[:id])
190:       .select(1)
191:       .arel
192:       .exists
193:   end
194: end

(End of file - total 194 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Schema Information
4: #
5: # Table name: quotes
6: #
7: #  id                :bigint(8)        not null, primary key
8: #  activity_uri      :string
9: #  approval_uri      :string
10: #  legacy            :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
11: #  state             :integer          default("pending"), not null
12: #  created_at        :datetime         not null
13: #  updated_at        :datetime         not null
14: #  account_id        :bigint(8)        not null
15: #  quoted_account_id :bigint(8)
16: #  quoted_status_id  :bigint(8)
17: #  status_id         :bigint(8)        not null
18: #
19: class Quote < ApplicationRecord
20:   include Paginable
21: 
22:   has_one :notification, as: :activity, dependent: :destroy
23: 
24:   BACKGROUND_REFRESH_INTERVAL = 1.week.freeze
25:   REFRESH_DEADLINE = 6.hours
26: 
27:   enum :state,
28:        { pending: 0, accepted: 1, rejected: 2, revoked: 3, deleted: 4 },
29:        validate: true
30: 
31:   belongs_to :status
32:   belongs_to :quoted_status, class_name: 'Status', optional: true
33: 
34:   belongs_to :account
35:   belongs_to :quoted_account, class_name: 'Account', optional: true
36: 
37:   before_validation :set_accounts
38:   before_validation :set_activity_uri, on: :create, if: -> { account.local? && quoted_account&.remote? }
39:   validates :activity_uri, presence: true, if: -> { account.local? && quoted_account&.remote? }
40:   validates :approval_uri, absence: true, if: -> { quoted_account&.local? }
41:   validate :validate_visibility
42:   validate :validate_original_quoted_status
43: 
44:   after_create_commit :increment_counter_caches!
45:   after_destroy_commit :decrement_counter_caches!
46:   after_update_commit :update_counter_caches!
47: 
48:   def accept!(approval_uri: nil)
49:     if approval_uri.present?
50:       update!(state: :accepted, approval_uri:)
51:     else
52:       update!(state: :accepted)
53:     end
54: 
55:     reset_parent_cache! if attribute_previously_changed?(:state)
56:   end
57: 
58:   def reject!
59:     if accepted?
60:       update!(state: :revoked, approval_uri: nil)
61:     elsif !revoked?
62:       update!(state: :rejected, approval_uri: nil)
63:     end
64:   end
65: 
66:   def acceptable?
67:     accepted? || !legacy?
68:   end
69: 
70:   def ensure_quoted_access
71:     status.mentions.create!(account: quoted_account, silent: true)
72:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
73:     nil
74:   end
75: 
76:   def schedule_refresh_if_stale!
77:     return unless quoted_status_id.present? && approval_uri.present? && updated_at <= BACKGROUND_REFRESH_INTERVAL.ago
78: 
79:     ActivityPub::QuoteRefreshWorker.perform_in(rand(REFRESH_DEADLINE), id)
80:   end
81: 
82:   def sign?
83:     true
84:   end
85: 
86:   private
87: 
88:   def reset_parent_cache!
89:     return if status_id.nil?
90: 
91:     Rails.cache.delete("v3:statuses/#{status_id}")
92: 
93:     # This clears the web cache for the ActivityPub representation
94:     Rails.cache.delete("statuses/show:v3:statuses/#{status_id}")
95:   end
96: 
97:   def set_accounts
98:     self.account = status.account
99:     self.quoted_account = quoted_status&.account
100:   end
101: 
102:   def validate_visibility
103:     return if account_id == quoted_account_id || quoted_status.nil? || quoted_status.distributable?
104: 
105:     errors.add(:quoted_status_id, :visibility_mismatch)
106:   end
107: 
108:   def validate_original_quoted_status
109:     errors.add(:quoted_status_id, :reblog_unallowed) if quoted_status&.reblog?
110:   end
111: 
112:   def set_activity_uri
113:     self.activity_uri = [ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(account), '/quote_requests/', SecureRandom.uuid].join
114:   end
115: 
116:   def increment_counter_caches!
117:     return unless accepted?
118: 
119:     quoted_status&.increment_count!(:quotes_count)
120:   end
121: 
122:   def decrement_counter_caches!
123:     return unless accepted?
124: 
125:     quoted_status&.decrement_count!(:quotes_count)
126:   end
127: 
128:   def update_counter_caches!
129:     return if legacy? || !state_previously_changed?
130: 
131:     if accepted?
132:       quoted_status&.increment_count!(:quotes_count)
133:     else
134:       # TODO: are there cases where this would not be correct?
135:       quoted_status&.decrement_count!(:quotes_count)
136:     end
137:   end
138: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Schema Information
4: #
5: # Table name: status_stats
6: #
7: #  id                         :bigint(8)        not null, primary key
8: #  favourites_count           :bigint(8)        default(0), not null
9: #  quotes_count               :bigint(8)        default(0), not null
10: #  reblogs_count              :bigint(8)        default(0), not null
11: #  replies_count              :bigint(8)        default(0), not null
12: #  untrusted_favourites_count :bigint(8)
13: #  untrusted_reblogs_count    :bigint(8)
14: #  created_at                 :datetime         not null
15: #  updated_at                 :datetime         not null
16: #  status_id                  :bigint(8)        not null
17: #
18: 
19: class StatusStat < ApplicationRecord
20:   belongs_to :status, inverse_of: :status_stat
21: 
22:   before_validation :clamp_untrusted_counts
23: 
24:   MAX_UNTRUSTED_COUNT = 100_000_000
25: 
26:   def replies_count
27:     [attributes['replies_count'], 0].max

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class StatusPolicy < ApplicationPolicy
4:   def show?
5:     return false if author.unavailable?
6: 
7:     if requires_mention?
8:       owned? || mention_exists?
9:     elsif private?
10:       owned? || following_author? || mention_exists?
11:     else
12:       current_account.nil? || (!author_blocking? && !author_blocking_domain?)
13:     end
14:   end
15: 
16:   def quote?
17:     show? && !blocking_author? && record.quote_policy_for_account(current_account) != :denied
18:   end
19: 
20:   def reblog?
21:     !requires_mention? && (!private? || owned?) && show? && !blocking_author?
22:   end
23: 
24:   def favourite?
25:     show? && !blocking_author?
26:   end
27: 
28:   def destroy?
29:     owned?
30:   end
31: 
32:   alias unreblog? destroy?
33: 
34:   def update?
35:     owned?
36:   end
37: 
38:   private
39: 
40:   def requires_mention?
41:     record.direct_visibility? || record.limited_visibility?
42:   end
43: 
44:   def owned?
45:     author.id == current_account&.id
46:   end
47: 
48:   def private?
49:     record.private_visibility?
50:   end
51: 
52:   def mention_exists?
53:     return false if current_account.nil?
54: 
55:     if record.mentions.loaded?
56:       record.mentions.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == current_account.id }
57:     else
58:       record.mentions.exists?(account: current_account)
59:     end
60:   end
61: 
62:   def author_blocking_domain?
63:     return false if current_account.nil? || current_account.domain.nil?
64: 
65:     author.domain_blocking?(current_account.domain)
66:   end
67: 
68:   def blocking_author?

167:   def target_collection
168:     case type
169:     when :added_to_collection
170:       collection_item&.collection
171:     when :collection_update
172:       collection
173:     end
174:   end
175: 
176:   class << self
177:     def browserable(types: [], exclude_types: [], from_account_id: nil, include_filtered: false)
178:       requested_types = if types.empty?
179:                           TYPES
180:                         else
181:                           types.map(&:to_sym) & TYPES
182:                         end
183: 
184:       requested_types -= exclude_types.map(&:to_sym)
185: 
186:       all.tap do |scope|
187:         scope.merge!(where(filtered: false)) unless include_filtered || from_account_id.present?
188:         scope.merge!(where(from_account_id: from_account_id)) if from_account_id.present?
189:         scope.merge!(where(type: requested_types)) unless requested_types.size == TYPES.size
190:       end
191:     end
192: 
193:     def preload_cache_collection_target_statuses(notifications, &_block)
194:       notifications.group_by(&:type).each do |type, grouped_notifications|
195:         associations = TARGET_STATUS_INCLUDES_BY_TYPE[type]
196:         next unless associations
197: 
198:         # Instead of using the usual `includes`, manually preload each type.
199:         # If polymorphic associations are loaded with the usual `includes`, other types of associations will be loaded more.
200:         ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(records: grouped_notifications, associations: associations).call
201:       end
202: 
203:       unique_target_statuses = notifications.filter_map(&:target_status).uniq
204:       # Call cache_collection in block
205:       cached_statuses_by_id = yield(unique_target_statuses).index_by(&:id)
206: 
207:       notifications.each do |notification|
208:         next if notification.target_status.nil?
209: 
210:         cached_status = cached_statuses_by_id[notification.target_status.id]
211: 
212:         case notification.type
213:         when :status, :update, :quoted_update
214:           notification.status = cached_status
215:         when :reblog
216:           notification.status.reblog = cached_status
217:         when :favourite
218:           notification.favourite.status = cached_status
219:         when :mention
220:           notification.mention.status = cached_status
221:         when :poll
222:           notification.poll.status = cached_status
223:         when :quote
224:           notification.quote.status = cached_status
225:         end
226:       end
227: 
228:       notifications
229:     end
230:   end
231: 
232:   after_initialize :set_from_account
233:   before_validation :set_from_account
234: 
235:   after_destroy :remove_from_notification_request
236: 
237:   private
238: 
239:   def set_from_account
240:     return unless new_record?
241: 
242:     case activity_type
243:     when 'Status'
244:       self.from_account_id = type == :quoted_update ? activity&.quote&.quoted_account_id : activity&.account_id
245:     when 'Follow', 'Favourite', 'FollowRequest', 'Poll', 'Report', 'Quote', 'Collection'
246:       self.from_account_id = activity&.account_id
247:     when 'CollectionItem'
248:       self.from_account_id = activity&.collection&.account_id
249:     when 'Mention'
250:       self.from_account_id = activity&.status&.account_id
251:     when 'Account'
252:       self.from_account_id = activity&.id
253:     when 'AccountRelationshipSeveranceEvent', 'AccountWarning', 'GeneratedAnnualReport'
254:       # These do not really have an originating account, but this is mandatory
255:       # in the data model, and the recipient's account will by definition
256:       # always exist
257:       self.from_account_id = account_id
258:     end
259:   end
260: 
261:   def remove_from_notification_request
262:     notification_request = NotificationRequest.find_by(account_id: account_id, from_account_id: from_account_id)
263:     notification_request&.reconsider_existence!
264:   end
265: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Schema Information
4: #
5: # Table name: account_conversations
6: #
7: #  id                      :bigint(8)        not null, primary key
8: #  lock_version            :integer          default(0), not null
9: #  participant_account_ids :bigint(8)        default([]), not null, is an Array
10: #  status_ids              :bigint(8)        default([]), not null, is an Array
11: #  unread                  :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
12: #  account_id              :bigint(8)        not null
13: #  conversation_id         :bigint(8)        not null
14: #  last_status_id          :bigint(8)
15: #
16: 
17: class AccountConversation < ApplicationRecord
18:   include Redisable
19: 
20:   attr_writer :participant_accounts
21: 
22:   before_validation :set_last_status
23:   after_commit :push_to_streaming_api
24: 
25:   belongs_to :account
26:   belongs_to :conversation
27:   belongs_to :last_status, class_name: 'Status'
28: 
29:   def participant_account_ids=(arr)
30:     self[:participant_account_ids] = arr.sort
31:     @participant_accounts = nil
32:   end
33: 
34:   def participant_accounts
35:     @participant_accounts ||= Account.where(id: participant_account_ids).to_a
36:     @participant_accounts.presence || [account]
37:   end
38: 
39:   class << self
40:     def to_a_paginated_by_id(limit, options = {})
41:       array = begin
42:         if options[:min_id]
43:           paginate_by_min_id(limit, options[:min_id], options[:max_id]).reverse
44:         else
45:           paginate_by_max_id(limit, options[:max_id], options[:since_id]).to_a
46:         end
47:       end
48: 
49:       # Preload participants
50:       participant_ids = array.flat_map(&:participant_account_ids)
51:       accounts_by_id = Account.where(id: participant_ids).index_by(&:id)
52: 
53:       array.each do |conversation|
54:         conversation.participant_accounts = conversation.participant_account_ids.filter_map { |id| accounts_by_id[id] }
55:       end
56: 
57:       array
58:     end
59: 
60:     def paginate_by_min_id(limit, min_id = nil, max_id = nil)
61:       query = order(arel_table[:last_status_id].asc).limit(limit)
62:       query = query.where(arel_table[:last_status_id].gt(min_id)) if min_id.present?
63:       query = query.where(arel_table[:last_status_id].lt(max_id)) if max_id.present?
64:       query
65:     end
66: 
67:     def paginate_by_max_id(limit, max_id = nil, since_id = nil)
68:       query = order(arel_table[:last_status_id].desc).limit(limit)
69:       query = query.where(arel_table[:last_status_id].lt(max_id)) if max_id.present?
70:       query = query.where(arel_table[:last_status_id].gt(since_id)) if since_id.present?
71:       query
72:     end
73: 
74:     def add_status(recipient, status)
75:       conversation = find_or_initialize_by(account: recipient, conversation_id: status.conversation_id, participant_account_ids: participants_from_status(recipient, status))
76: 
77:       return conversation if conversation.status_ids.include?(status.id)
78: 
79:       conversation.status_ids << status.id
80:       conversation.unread = status.account_id != recipient.id
81:       conversation.save
82:       conversation
83:     rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
84:       retry
85:     end
86: 
87:     def remove_status(recipient, status)
88:       conversation = find_by(account: recipient, conversation_id: status.conversation_id, participant_account_ids: participants_from_status(recipient, status))
89: 
90:       return if conversation.nil?
91: 
92:       conversation.status_ids.delete(status.id)
93: 
94:       if conversation.status_ids.empty?
95:         conversation.destroy
96:       else
97:         conversation.save
98:       end
99: 
100:       conversation
101:     rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
102:       retry
103:     end
104: 
105:     private
106: 
107:     def participants_from_status(recipient, status)
108:       ((status.active_mentions.pluck(:account_id) + [status.account_id]).uniq - [recipient.id]).sort
109:     end
110:   end
111: 
112:   private
113: 
114:   def set_last_status
115:     self.status_ids     = status_ids.sort
116:     self.last_status_id = status_ids.last
117:   end
118: 
119:   def push_to_streaming_api
120:     return if destroyed? || !subscribed_to_timeline?
121: 
122:     PushConversationWorker.perform_async(id)
123:   end
124: 
125:   def subscribed_to_timeline?
126:     redis.exists?("subscribed:#{streaming_channel}")
127:   end
128: 
129:   def streaming_channel
130:     "timeline:direct:#{account_id}"
131:   end
132: end

306:       identifier.size == SHORTCODE_LENGTH ? find_by!(shortcode: identifier) : find(identifier)
307:     end
308: 
309:     def supported_mime_types
310:       IMAGE_MIME_TYPES + VIDEO_MIME_TYPES + AUDIO_MIME_TYPES
311:     end
312: 
313:     def supported_file_extensions
314:       IMAGE_FILE_EXTENSIONS + VIDEO_FILE_EXTENSIONS + AUDIO_FILE_EXTENSIONS
315:     end
316: 
317:     def combined_media_file_size
318:       arel_table.coalesce(arel_table[:file_file_size], 0) + arel_table.coalesce(arel_table[:thumbnail_file_size], 0)
319:     end
320: 
321:     private
322: 
323:     def file_styles(attachment)
324:       if attachment.instance.file_content_type == 'image/gif' || VIDEO_CONVERTIBLE_MIME_TYPES.include?(attachment.instance.file_content_type)
325:         VIDEO_CONVERTED_STYLES
326:       elsif IMAGE_CONVERTIBLE_MIME_TYPES.include?(attachment.instance.file_content_type)
327:         IMAGE_CONVERTED_STYLES
328:       elsif IMAGE_MIME_TYPES.include?(attachment.instance.file_content_type)
329:         IMAGE_STYLES
330:       elsif VIDEO_MIME_TYPES.include?(attachment.instance.file_content_type)
331:         VIDEO_STYLES
332:       else
333:         AUDIO_STYLES
334:       end
335:     end
336: 
337:     def file_processors(instance)
338:       if instance.file_content_type == 'image/gif'
339:         [:gif_transcoder, :blurhash_transcoder]
340:       elsif VIDEO_MIME_TYPES.include?(instance.file_content_type)
341:         [:transcoder, :blurhash_transcoder, :type_corrector]
342:       elsif AUDIO_MIME_TYPES.include?(instance.file_content_type)
343:         [:image_extractor, :transcoder, :type_corrector]
344:       else
345:         [:lazy_thumbnail, :blurhash_transcoder, :type_corrector]
346:       end
347:     end
348:   end
349: 
350:   private
351: 
352:   def set_unknown_type
353:     self.type = :unknown if file.blank? && !type_changed?
354:   end
355: 
356:   def set_type_and_extension
357:     self.type = begin
358:       if VIDEO_MIME_TYPES.include?(file_content_type)
359:         :video
360:       elsif AUDIO_MIME_TYPES.include?(file_content_type)
361:         :audio
362:       else
363:         :image
364:       end
365:     end
366:   end
367: 
368:   def set_processing
369:     self.processing = delay_processing? ? :queued : :complete
370:   end
371: 
372:   def check_video_dimensions
373:     return unless (video? || gifv?) && file.queued_for_write[:original].present?
374: 
375:     movie = ffmpeg_data(file.queued_for_write[:original].path)
376: 
377:     return unless movie.valid?
378: 
379:     raise Mastodon::StreamValidationError, 'Video has no video stream' if movie.width.nil? || movie.frame_rate.nil?
380:     raise Mastodon::DimensionsValidationError, "#{movie.width}x#{movie.height} videos are not supported" if movie.width * movie.height > MAX_VIDEO_MATRIX_LIMIT
381:     raise Mastodon::DimensionsValidationError, "#{movie.frame_rate.floor}fps videos are not supported" if movie.frame_rate.floor > MAX_VIDEO_FRAME_RATE
382:   end
383: 
384:   def set_meta
385:     file.instance_write :meta, populate_meta
386:   end
387: 
388:   def populate_meta
389:     meta = (file.instance_read(:meta) || {}).with_indifferent_access.slice(*META_KEYS)
390: 
391:     file.queued_for_write.each do |style, file|
392:       meta[style] = style == :small || image? ? image_geometry(file) : video_metadata(file)
393:     end
394: 
395:     meta[:small] = image_geometry(thumbnail.queued_for_write[:original]) if thumbnail.queued_for_write.key?(:original)
396: 
397:     meta
398:   end
399: 
400:   def image_geometry(file)
401:     width, height = FastImage.size(file.path)
402: 
403:     return {} if width.nil?
404: 
405:     {
406:       width: width,
407:       height: height,
408:       size: "#{width}x#{height}",
409:       aspect: width.to_f / height,
410:     }
411:   end
412: 
413:   def video_metadata(file)
414:     movie = ffmpeg_data(file.path)
415: 
416:     return {} unless movie.valid?
417: 
418:     {
419:       width: movie.width,
420:       height: movie.height,
421:       frame_rate: movie.frame_rate,
422:       duration: movie.duration,
423:       bitrate: movie.bitrate,
424:     }.compact
425:   end
426: 
427:   # We call this method about 3 different times on potentially different
428:   # paths but ultimately the same file, so it makes sense to memoize the
429:   # result while disregarding the path
430:   def ffmpeg_data(path = nil)
431:     @ffmpeg_data ||= VideoMetadataExtractor.new(path)
432:   end
433: 
434:   def enqueue_processing
435:     PostProcessMediaWorker.perform_async(id) if delay_processing?
436:   end
437: 
438:   def reset_parent_cache
439:     Rails.cache.delete("v3:statuses/#{status_id}") if status_id.present?
440:   end
441: 
442:   # Record the cache keys to burst before the file get actually deleted
443:   def prepare_cache_bust!
444:     return unless Rails.configuration.x.cache_buster.enabled
445: 
446:     @paths_to_cache_bust = MediaAttachment.attachment_definitions.keys.flat_map do |attachment_name|
447:       attachment = public_send(attachment_name)
448:       next if attachment.blank?
449: 
450:       styles = DEFAULT_STYLES | attachment.styles.keys
451:       styles.map { |style| attachment.url(style) }
452:     end.compact
453:   rescue => e
454:     # We really don't want any error here preventing media deletion
455:     Rails.logger.warn "Error #{e.class} busting cache: #{e.message}"
456:   end
457: 
458:   # Once Paperclip has deleted the files, we can't recover the cache keys,
459:   # so use the previously-saved ones
460:   def bust_cache!
461:     return unless Rails.configuration.x.cache_buster.enabled
462: 
463:     CacheBusterWorker.push_bulk(@paths_to_cache_bust) { |path| [path] }
464:   rescue => e
465:     # We really don't want any error here preventing media deletion
466:     Rails.logger.warn "Error #{e.class} busting cache: #{e.message}"
467:   end
468: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class StatusFilter
4:   attr_reader :status, :account
5: 
6:   def initialize(status, account)
7:     @status              = status
8:     @account             = account
9:   end
10: 
11:   def filtered?
12:     return false if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id
13: 
14:     blocked_by_policy? || (account_present? && filtered_status?) || silenced_account?
15:   end
16: 
17:   def filter_state_for_quote
18:     if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id
19:       nil
20:     elsif blocked_by_policy?
21:       'unauthorized'
22:     elsif account_present? && blocking_domain?
23:       'blocked_domain'
24:     elsif account_present? && blocking_account?
25:       'blocked_account'
26:     elsif account_present? && muting_account?
27:       'muted_account'
28:     end
29:   end
30: 
31:   private
32: 
33:   def account_present?
34:     !account.nil?
35:   end
36: 
37:   def filtered_status?
38:     blocking_account? || blocking_domain? || muting_account?
39:   end
40: 
41:   def blocking_account?
42:     account.blocking?(status.account_id)
43:   end
44: 
45:   def blocking_domain?
46:     account.domain_blocking?(status.account_domain)
47:   end
48: 
49:   def muting_account?
50:     account.muting?(status.account_id)
51:   end
52: 
53:   def silenced_account?
54:     status_account_silenced? && !account_following_status_account?
55:   end
56: 
57:   def status_account_silenced?
58:     status.account.silenced?
59:   end
60: 
61:   def account_following_status_account?
62:     account&.following?(status.account_id)
63:   end
64: 
65:   def blocked_by_policy?
66:     !policy_allows_show?
67:   end
68: 
69:   def policy_allows_show?
70:     StatusPolicy.new(account, status).show?
71:   end
72: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class StatusReachFinder
4:   # @param [Status] status
5:   # @param [Hash] options
6:   # @option options [Boolean] :unsafe
7:   def initialize(status, options = {})
8:     @status  = status
9:     @options = options
10:   end
11: 
12:   def inboxes
13:     (reached_account_inboxes + followers_inboxes + relay_inboxes).uniq
14:   end
15: 
16:   private
17: 
18:   def reached_account_inboxes
19:     scope = Account.where(id: reached_account_ids)
20:     inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
21:   end
22: 
23:   def reached_account_ids
24:     # When the status is a reblog, there are no interactions with it
25:     # directly, we assume all interactions are with the original one
26: 
27:     if @status.reblog?
28:       [reblog_of_account_id]
29:     else
30:       [
31:         replied_to_account_id,
32:         reblog_of_account_id,
33:         quote_of_account_id,
34:         mentioned_account_ids,
35:         reblogs_account_ids,
36:         quotes_account_ids,
37:         favourites_account_ids,
38:         replies_account_ids,
39:       ].tap do |arr|
40:         arr.flatten!
41:         arr.compact!
42:         arr.uniq!
43:       end
44:     end
45:   end
46: 
47:   def replied_to_account_id
48:     @status.in_reply_to_account_id if distributable?
49:   end
50: 
51:   def quote_of_account_id
52:     @status.quote&.quoted_account_id
53:   end
54: 
55:   def reblog_of_account_id
56:     @status.reblog.account_id if @status.reblog?
57:   end
58: 
59:   def mentioned_account_ids
60:     @status.mentions.pluck(:account_id)
61:   end
62: 
63:   # Beware: Quotes can be created without the author having had access to the status
64:   def quotes_account_ids
65:     @status.quotes.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
66:   end
67: 
68:   # Beware: Reblogs can be created without the author having had access to the status
69:   def reblogs_account_ids
70:     @status.reblogs.rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at]).pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
71:   end
72: 
73:   # Beware: Favourites can be created without the author having had access to the status
74:   def favourites_account_ids
75:     @status.favourites.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
76:   end
77: 
78:   # Beware: Replies can be created without the author having had access to the status
79:   def replies_account_ids
80:     @status.replies.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
81:   end
82: 
83:   def followers_inboxes
84:     scope = followers_scope
85:     inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
86:   end
87: 
88:   def relay_inboxes
89:     if @status.public_visibility?
90:       Relay.enabled.pluck(:inbox_url)
91:     else
92:       []
93:     end
94:   end
95: 
96:   def distributable?
97:     @status.public_visibility? || @status.unlisted_visibility?
98:   end
99: 
100:   def unsafe?
101:     @options[:unsafe]
102:   end
103: 
104:   def followers_scope
105:     if @status.in_reply_to_local_account? && distributable?
106:       @status.account.followers.or(@status.thread.account.followers.not_domain_blocked_by_account(@status.account))
107:     elsif @status.direct_visibility? || @status.limited_visibility?
108:       Account.none
109:     else
110:       @status.account.followers
111:     end
112:   end
113: 
114:   def inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
115:     scope.merge!(Account.without_suspended) unless unsafe?
116:     scope.inboxes
117:   end
118: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class StatusCacheHydrator
4:   def initialize(status)
5:     @status = status
6:   end
7: 
8:   def hydrate(account_or_id, nested: false)
9:     account = account_or_id.is_a?(Account) ? account_or_id : Account.find(account_or_id)
10: 
11:     # The cache of the serialized hash is generated by the fan-out-on-write service
12:     payload = Rails.cache.fetch("fan-out/#{@status.id}") { InlineRenderer.render(@status, nil, :status) }
13: 
14:     # If we're delivering to the author who disabled the display of the application used to create the
15:     # status, we need to hydrate the application, since it was not rendered for the basic payload
16:     payload[:application] = payload_application if payload[:application].nil? && @status.account_id == account.id
17: 
18:     # We take advantage of the fact that some relationships can only occur with an original status, not
19:     # the reblog that wraps it, so we can assume that some values are always false
20:     if payload[:reblog]
21:       hydrate_reblog_payload(payload, account, nested:)
22:     else
23:       hydrate_non_reblog_payload(payload, account, nested:)
24:     end
25:   end
26: 
27:   private
28: 
29:   def hydrate_non_reblog_payload(empty_payload, account, nested: false)
30:     empty_payload.tap do |payload|
31:       fill_status_payload(payload, @status, account, fresh: !nested, nested:)
32:     end
33:   end
34: 
35:   def hydrate_reblog_payload(empty_payload, account, nested: false)
36:     empty_payload.tap do |payload|
37:       payload[:muted]      = false
38:       payload[:bookmarked] = false
39:       payload[:pinned]     = false if @status.account_id == account.id
40: 
41:       # If the reblogged status is being delivered to the author who disabled the display of the application
42:       # used to create the status, we need to hydrate it here too
43:       payload[:reblog][:application] = payload_reblog_application if payload[:reblog][:application].nil? && @status.reblog.account_id == account.id
44: 
45:       hydrate_account(payload[:account], account)
46:       fill_status_payload(payload[:reblog], @status.reblog, account, fresh: false, nested:)
47: 
48:       payload[:filtered]   = payload[:reblog][:filtered]
49:       payload[:favourited] = payload[:reblog][:favourited]
50:       payload[:reblogged]  = payload[:reblog][:reblogged]
51:       payload[:quote_approval] = payload[:reblog][:quote_approval]
52:     end
53:   end
54: 
55:   def fill_status_payload(payload, status, account, nested: false, fresh: true)
56:     payload[:favourited] = Favourite.exists?(account_id: account.id, status_id: status.id)
57:     payload[:reblogged]  = Status.exists?(account_id: account.id, reblog_of_id: status.id)
58:     payload[:muted]      = ConversationMute.exists?(account_id: account.id, conversation_id: status.conversation_id)
59:     payload[:bookmarked] = Bookmark.exists?(account_id: account.id, status_id: status.id)
60:     payload[:pinned]     = StatusPin.exists?(account_id: account.id, status_id: status.id) if status.account_id == account.id
61:     payload[:filtered]   = mapped_applied_custom_filter(account, status)
62:     payload[:quote_approval][:current_user] = status.quote_policy_for_account(account) if payload[:quote_approval]
63:     payload[:quote] = hydrate_quote_payload(payload[:quote], status.quote, account, nested:) if payload[:quote]
64: 
65:     if payload[:poll]
66:       if fresh
67:         # If the status is brand new, we don't need to look up votes in database
68:         payload[:poll][:voted] = status.account_id == account.id
69:         payload[:poll][:own_votes] = []
70:       elsif status.account_id == account.id
71:         payload[:poll][:voted] = true
72:         payload[:poll][:own_votes] = []
73:       else
74:         own_votes = PollVote.where(poll_id: status.poll_id, account_id: account.id).pluck(:choice)
75:         payload[:poll][:voted] = !own_votes.empty?
76:         payload[:poll][:own_votes] = own_votes
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     payload[:card][:missing_attribution] = status.preview_card.unverified_author_account_id == account.id if payload[:card]
81: 
82:     # Nested statuses are more likely to have a stale cache
83:     fill_status_stats(payload, status) if nested
84: 
85:     hydrate_account(payload[:account], account)
86:   end
87: 
88:   def fill_status_stats(payload, status)
89:     payload[:replies_count] = status.replies_count
90:     payload[:reblogs_count] = status.untrusted_reblogs_count || status.reblogs_count
91:     payload[:favourites_count] = status.untrusted_favourites_count || status.favourites_count
92:     payload[:quotes_count] = status.quotes_count
93:   end
94: 
95:   def hydrate_quote_payload(empty_payload, quote, account, nested: false)
96:     return unless quote&.acceptable?
97: 
98:     empty_payload.tap do |payload|
99:       payload.delete(:quoted_status) if nested
100: 
101:       # TODO: performance improvements
102:       if quote.accepted?
103:         if quote.quoted_status.nil?
104:           payload[nested ? :quoted_status_id : :quoted_status] = nil
105:           payload[:state] = 'deleted'
106:         else
107:           filter_state = StatusFilter.new(quote.quoted_status, account).filter_state_for_quote
108:           payload[:state] = filter_state || 'accepted'
109:           if filter_state == 'unauthorized'
110:             payload[nested ? :quoted_status_id : :quoted_status] = nil
111:           elsif nested
112:             payload[:quoted_status_id] = quote.quoted_status_id&.to_s
113:           else
114:             payload[:quoted_status] = StatusCacheHydrator.new(quote.quoted_status).hydrate(account, nested: true)
115:           end
116:         end
117:       else
118:         payload[nested ? :quoted_status_id : :quoted_status] = nil
119:       end
120:     end
121:   end
122: 
123:   def hydrate_account(payload, account)
124:     return unless payload[:id]
125: 
126:     stale_account = Account.find_by(id: payload[:id])
127:     return if stale_account.nil?
128: 
129:     payload[:feature_approval][:current_user] = stale_account.feature_policy_for_account(account)
130:   end
131: 
132:   def mapped_applied_custom_filter(account, status)
133:     CustomFilter
134:       .apply_cached_filters(CustomFilter.cached_filters_for(account), status)
135:       .map { |filter| serialized_filter(filter) }
136:   end
137: 
138:   def serialized_filter(filter)
139:     ActiveModelSerializers::SerializableResource.new(
140:       filter,
141:       serializer: REST::FilterResultSerializer
142:     ).as_json
143:   end
144: 
145:   def payload_application
146:     @status.application.present? ? serialized_status_application_json : nil
147:   end
148: 
149:   def serialized_status_application_json
150:     ActiveModelSerializers::SerializableResource.new(
151:       @status.application,
152:       serializer: REST::StatusSerializer::ApplicationSerializer
153:     ).as_json
154:   end
155: 
156:   def payload_reblog_application
157:     @status.reblog.application.present? ? serialized_status_reblog_application_json : nil
158:   end
159: 
160:   def serialized_status_reblog_application_json
161:     ActiveModelSerializers::SerializableResource.new(
162:       @status.reblog.application,
163:       serializer: REST::StatusSerializer::ApplicationSerializer
164:     ).as_json
165:   end
166: end

(End of file - total 166 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class AccountStatusesFilter
4:   KEYS = %i(
5:     pinned
6:     tagged
7:     only_media
8:     exclude_replies
9:     exclude_reblogs
10:   ).freeze
11: 
12:   attr_reader :params, :account, :current_account
13: 
14:   def initialize(account, current_account, params = {})
15:     @account         = account
16:     @current_account = current_account
17:     @params          = params
18:   end
19: 
20:   def results
21:     scope = initial_scope
22: 
23:     scope.merge!(pinned_scope)     if pinned?
24:     scope.merge!(only_media_scope) if only_media?
25:     scope.merge!(no_replies_scope) if exclude_replies?
26:     scope.merge!(no_reblogs_scope) if exclude_reblogs?
27:     scope.merge!(hashtag_scope)    if tagged?
28: 
29:     scope
30:   end
31: 
32:   private
33: 
34:   def initial_scope
35:     return Status.none if account.unavailable?
36: 
37:     if anonymous?
38:       account.statuses.distributable_visibility
39:     elsif author?
40:       exclude_direct? ? account.statuses.where(visibility: %i(public unlisted private)) : account.statuses.all # NOTE: #merge! does not work without the #all
41:     elsif blocked?
42:       Status.none
43:     else
44:       filtered_scope
45:     end
46:   end
47: 
48:   def filtered_scope
49:     scope = account.statuses
50: 
51:     if exclude_direct?
52:       scope = scope.where(visibility: follower? ? %i(public unlisted private) : %i(public unlisted))
53:     else
54:       scope = account.statuses.left_outer_joins(:mentions)
55:       scope.merge!(scope.where(visibility: follower? ? %i(public unlisted private) : %i(public unlisted)).or(scope.where(mentions: { account_id: current_account.id })).group(Status.arel_table[:id]))
56:     end
57: 
58:     scope.merge!(filtered_reblogs_scope) if reblogs_may_occur?
59: 
60:     scope
61:   end
62: 
63:   def filtered_reblogs_scope
64:     scope = Status.left_outer_joins(reblog: :account)
65:     scope
66:       .where(reblog_of_id: nil)
67:       .or(
68:         scope
69:           # This is basically `Status.not_domain_blocked_by_account(current_account)`
70:           # and `Status.not_excluded_by_account(current_account)` but on the
71:           # `reblog` association. Unfortunately, there seem to be no clean way
72:           # to re-use those scopes in our case.
73:           .where(reblog: { accounts: { domain: nil } }).or(scope.where.not(reblog: { accounts: { domain: current_account.excluded_from_timeline_domains } }))
74:           .where.not(reblog: { account_id: current_account.excluded_from_timeline_account_ids })
75:       )
76:   end
77: 
78:   def only_media_scope
79:     Status.without_empty_attachments.joins(:media_attachments).merge(account.media_attachments).group(Status.arel_table[:id])
80:   end
81: 
82:   def no_replies_scope
83:     Status.without_replies
84:   end
85: 
86:   def no_reblogs_scope
87:     Status.without_reblogs
88:   end
89: 
90:   def pinned_scope
91:     account.pinned_statuses.group(Status.arel_table[:id], StatusPin.arel_table[:created_at])
92:   end
93: 
94:   def hashtag_scope
95:     tag = Tag.find_normalized(params[:tagged])
96: 
97:     if tag
98:       Status.tagged_with(tag.id)
99:     else
100:       Status.none
101:     end
102:   end
103: 
104:   def anonymous?
105:     current_account.nil?
106:   end
107: 
108:   def author?
109:     current_account.id == account.id
110:   end
111: 
112:   def blocked?
113:     account.blocking?(current_account) || (current_account.domain.present? && account.domain_blocking?(current_account.domain))
114:   end
115: 
116:   def follower?
117:     current_account.following?(account)
118:   end
119: 
120:   def reblogs_may_occur?
121:     !exclude_reblogs? && !only_media? && !tagged?
122:   end

124:   def pinned?

126:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class Vacuum::StatusesVacuum
4:   include Redisable
5: 
6:   def initialize(retention_period)
7:     @retention_period = retention_period
8:   end
9: 
10:   def perform
11:     vacuum_statuses! if @retention_period.present?
12:   end
13: 
14:   private
15: 
16:   def vacuum_statuses!
17:     statuses_scope.in_batches do |statuses|
18:       # Side-effects not covered by foreign keys, such
19:       # as the search index, must be handled first.
20:       statuses.direct_visibility
21:         .includes(mentions: :account)
22:         .find_each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
23:       if Chewy.enabled?
24:         remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:StatusesIndex')
25:         remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:PublicStatusesIndex')
26:       end
27: 
28:       # Foreign keys take care of most associated records for us.
29:       # Media attachments will be orphaned.
30:       statuses.delete_all
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   def statuses_scope
35:     Status.unscoped.kept
36:       .joins(:account).merge(Account.remote)
37:       .where(statuses: { id: ...retention_period_as_id })
38:   end
39: 
40:   def retention_period_as_id
41:     Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(@retention_period.ago, with_random: false)
42:   end
43: 
44:   def remove_from_index(status_ids, index)
45:     with_redis { |redis| redis.sadd(index, status_ids) }
46:   end
47: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class Importer::StatusesIndexImporter < Importer::BaseImporter
4:   def import!
5:     # The idea is that instead of iterating over all statuses in the database
6:     # and calculating the searchable_by for each of them (majority of which
7:     # would be empty), we approach the index from the other end
8: 
9:     scopes.each do |scope|
10:       # We could be tempted to keep track of status IDs we have already processed
11:       # from a different scope to avoid indexing them multiple times, but that
12:       # could end up being a very large array
13: 
14:       scope.reorder(nil).find_in_batches(batch_size: @batch_size) do |tmp|
15:         in_work_unit(tmp.map(&:status_id)) do |status_ids|
16:           deleted = 0
17: 
18:           bulk = ActiveRecord::Base.connection_pool.with_connection do
19:             to_index = index.adapter.default_scope.where(id: status_ids)
20:             crutches = Chewy::Index::Crutch::Crutches.new index, to_index
21:             to_index.map do |object|
22:               # This is unlikely to happen, but the post may have been
23:               # un-interacted with since it was queued for indexing
24:               if object.searchable_by.empty?
25:                 deleted += 1
26:                 { delete: { _id: object.id } }
27:               else
28:                 { index: { _id: object.id, data: index.compose(object, crutches, fields: []) } }
29:               end
30:             end
31:           end
32: 
33:           indexed = bulk.size - deleted
34: 
35:           Chewy::Index::Import::BulkRequest.new(index).perform(bulk)
36: 
37:           [indexed, deleted]
38:         end
39:       end
40:     end
41: 
42:     wait!
43:   end
44: 
45:   private
46: 
47:   def index
48:     StatusesIndex
49:   end
50: 
51:   def scopes
52:     [
53:       local_statuses_scope,
54:       local_mentions_scope,
55:       local_favourites_scope,
56:       local_votes_scope,
57:       local_bookmarks_scope,
58:     ]
59:   end
60: 
61:   def local_mentions_scope
62:     Mention.where(account: Account.local, silent: false).select(:id, :status_id)
63:   end
64: 
65:   def local_favourites_scope
66:     Favourite.where(account: Account.local).select(:id, :status_id)
67:   end
68: 
69:   def local_bookmarks_scope
70:     Bookmark.select(:id, :status_id)
71:   end
72: 
73:   def local_votes_scope
74:     Poll.joins(:votes).where(votes: { account: Account.local }).select(polls: [:id, :status_id])
75:   end
76: 
77:   def local_statuses_scope
78:     Status.local.select('"statuses"."id", COALESCE("statuses"."reblog_of_id", "statuses"."id") AS status_id')
79:   end
80: end

3: class Importer::PublicStatusesIndexImporter < Importer::BaseImporter
4:   def import!
5:     scope.select(:id).find_in_batches(batch_size: @batch_size) do |batch|
6:       in_work_unit(batch.pluck(:id)) do |status_ids|
7:         bulk = ActiveRecord::Base.connection_pool.with_connection do
8:           build_bulk_body(index.adapter.default_scope.where(id: status_ids))
9:         end
10: 
11:         indexed = bulk.size
12:         deleted = 0
13: 
14:         Chewy::Index::Import::BulkRequest.new(index).perform(bulk)
15: 
16:         [indexed, deleted]
17:       end
18:     end
19: 
20:     wait!
21:   end
22: 
23:   private
24: 
25:   def index
26:     PublicStatusesIndex
27:   end
28: 
29:   def scope
30:     Status.indexable.reorder(nil)
31:   end
32: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class PermalinkRedirector
4:   include RoutingHelper
5: 
6:   def initialize(path)
7:     @path = path
8:     @object = nil
9:   end
10: 
11:   def object
12:     @object ||= begin
13:       if at_username_status_request? || statuses_status_request?
14:         status = Status.find_by(id: second_segment)
15:         status if status&.distributable? && !status&.local? && !status&.account&.suspended?
16:       elsif at_username_request?
17:         username, domain = first_segment.delete_prefix('@').split('@')
18:         domain = nil if TagManager.instance.local_domain?(domain)
19:         account = Account.find_remote(username, domain)
20:         account if !account&.local? && !account&.suspended?
21:       elsif accounts_request? && record_integer_id_request?
22:         account = Account.find_by(id: second_segment)
23:         account if !account&.local? && !account&.suspended?
24:       elsif collections_request? && record_integer_id_request?
25:         collection = Collection.find_by(id: second_segment)
26:         collection if !collection&.local? && !collection&.account&.suspended?
27:       end
28:     end
29:   end
30: 
31:   def redirect_path
32:     return ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.url_for(object) || ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(object) if object.present?
33: 
34:     @path.delete_prefix('/deck') if @path.start_with?('/deck')
35:   end
36: 
37:   def redirect_uri
38:     return ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(object) if object.present?
39: 
40:     @path.delete_prefix('/deck') if @path.start_with?('/deck')
41:   end
42: 
43:   def redirect_confirmation_path
44:     case object.class.name
45:     when 'Account'
46:       redirect_account_path(object.id)
47:     when 'Status'
48:       redirect_status_path(object.id)
49:     when 'Collection'
50:       redirect_collection_path(object.id)
51:     else
52:       @path.delete_prefix('/deck') if @path.start_with?('/deck')
53:     end
54:   end
55: 
56:   private
57: 
58:   def at_username_status_request?
59:     at_username_request? && record_integer_id_request?
60:   end
61: 
62:   def statuses_status_request?
63:     statuses_request? && record_integer_id_request?
64:   end
65: 
66:   def at_username_request?
67:     first_segment.present? && first_segment.start_with?('@')
68:   end
69: 
70:   def statuses_request?
71:     first_segment == 'statuses'
72:   end
73: 
74:   def accounts_request?
75:     first_segment == 'accounts'
76:   end
77: 
78:   def collections_request?
79:     first_segment == 'collections'
80:   end
81: 
82:   def record_integer_id_request?
83:     second_segment =~ /\d/
84:   end
85: 
86:   def first_segment
87:     path_segments.first
88:   end
89: 
90:   def second_segment
91:     path_segments.second
92:   end
93: 
94:   def path_segments
95:     @path_segments ||= @path.split('?')[0].delete_prefix('/deck').delete_prefix('/').split('/')
96:   end
97: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class Antispam
4:   include Redisable
5: 
6:   ACCOUNT_AGE_EXEMPTION = 1.week.freeze
7: 
8:   class DummyStatus < SimpleDelegator
9:     def self.model_name
10:       Mention.model_name
11:     end
12: 
13:     def active_mentions
14:       # Don't use the scope but the in-memory array
15:       mentions.filter { |mention| !mention.silent? }
16:     end
17:   end
18: 
19:   class SilentlyDrop < StandardError
20:     attr_reader :status
21: 
22:     def initialize(status)
23:       super()
24: 
25:       status.created_at = Time.now.utc
26:       status.id = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(status.created_at)
27:       status.in_reply_to_account_id = status.thread&.account_id
28: 
29:       status.delete # Make sure this is not persisted
30: 
31:       @status = DummyStatus.new(status)
32:     end
33:   end
34: 
35:   def initialize(status)
36:     @status = status
37:   end
38: 
39:   def local_preflight_check!
40:     return unless considered_spam?
41: 
42:     report_if_needed!
43: 
44:     raise SilentlyDrop, @status
45:   end
46: 
47:   private
48: 
49:   def considered_spam?
50:     (all_time_suspicious? || recent_suspicious?) && suspicious_reply_or_mention?
51:   end
52: 
53:   def all_time_suspicious?
54:     all_time_spammy_texts.any? { |spammy_text| status_text.include?(spammy_text) }
55:   end
56: 
57:   def recent_suspicious?
58:     @status.account.created_at >= ACCOUNT_AGE_EXEMPTION.ago && spammy_texts.any? { |spammy_text| status_text.include?(spammy_text) }
59:   end
60: 
61:   def spammy_texts
62:     redis.smembers('antispam:spammy_texts')
63:   end
64: 
65:   def all_time_spammy_texts
66:     redis.smembers('antispam:all_time_spammy_texts')
67:   end
68: 
69:   def suspicious_reply_or_mention?
70:     account_ids = ([@status.in_reply_to_account_id] + @status.mentions.map(&:account_id)).uniq
71:     !Follow.exists?(account_id: account_ids, target_account_id: @status.account.id)
72:   end
73: 
74:   def report_if_needed!
75:     return if system_reports.unresolved.exists?(target_account: @status.account)
76: 
77:     system_reports.create!(
78:       category: :spam,
79:       comment: 'Account automatically reported for posting a banned URL',
80:       target_account: @status.account
81:     )
82:   end
83: 
84:   def system_reports
85:     Account.representative.reports
86:   end
87: 
88:   def status_text
89:     @status_text ||= @status.text.unicode_normalize(:nfkc).downcase
90:   end
91: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class Admin::Metrics::Measure::InstanceStatusesMeasure < Admin::Metrics::Measure::BaseMeasure
4:   include Admin::Metrics::Measure::QueryHelper
5: 
6:   def self.with_params?
7:     true
8:   end
9: 
10:   def key
11:     'instance_statuses'
12:   end
13: 
14:   def total_in_time_range?
15:     false
16:   end
17: 
18:   protected
19: 
20:   def perform_total_query
21:     domain = params[:domain]
22:     domain = Instance.by_domain_and_subdomains(params[:domain]).select(:domain) if params[:include_subdomains]
23:     Status.joins(:account).merge(Account.where(domain: domain)).count
24:   end
25: 
26:   def perform_previous_total_query
27:     nil
28:   end
29: 
30:   def sql_array
31:     [sql_query_string, { start_at: @start_at, end_at: @end_at, domain: params[:domain], earliest_status_id:, latest_status_id: }]
32:   end
33: 
34:   def sql_query_string
35:     <<~SQL.squish
36:       SELECT axis.*, (
37:         WITH new_statuses AS (
38:           SELECT statuses.id
39:           FROM statuses
40:           INNER JOIN accounts ON accounts.id = statuses.account_id
41:           WHERE statuses.id BETWEEN :earliest_status_id AND :latest_status_id
42:             AND #{account_domain_sql(params[:include_subdomains])}
43:             AND date_trunc('day', statuses.created_at)::date = axis.period
44:         )
45:         SELECT count(*) FROM new_statuses
46:       ) AS value
47:       FROM (
48:         #{generated_series_days}
49:       ) AS axis
50:     SQL
51:   end
52: 
53:   def params
54:     @params.permit(:domain, :include_subdomains)
55:   end
56: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ActivityPub::Activity::Create < ActivityPub::Activity
4:   DISTRIBUTE_DELAY = 1.minute
5:   PROCESSING_DELAY = (30.seconds)..(10.minutes)
6: 
7:   def perform
8:     @account.schedule_refresh_if_stale!
9: 
10:     dereference_object!
11: 
12:     create_status
13:   end
14: 
15:   private
16: 
17:   def create_status
18:     return reject_payload! if unsupported_object_type? || non_matching_uri_hosts?(@account.uri, object_uri) || tombstone_exists? || !related_to_local_activity?
19: 
20:     with_redis_lock("create:#{object_uri}") do
21:       Status.uncached do
22:         return if delete_arrived_first?(object_uri) || poll_vote?
23: 
24:         @status = find_existing_status
25:       end
26: 
27:       if @status.nil?
28:         process_status
29:       elsif @options[:delivered_to_account_id].present?
30:         postprocess_audience_and_deliver
31:       end
32:     end
33: 
34:     @status
35:   end
36: 
37:   def audience_to
38:     as_array(@object['to'] || @json['to']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
39:   end
40: 
41:   def audience_cc
42:     as_array(@object['cc'] || @json['cc']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
43:   end
44: 
45:   def process_status
46:     @tags                 = []
47:     @mentions             = []
48:     @tagged_objects       = []
49:     @unresolved_mentions  = []
50:     @unresolved_collections = []
51:     @silenced_account_ids = []
52:     @params               = {}
53:     @quote                = nil
54:     @quote_uri            = nil
55:     @quote_approval_uri   = nil
56: 
57:     process_status_params
58:     process_tags
59:     process_quote
60:     process_audience
61: 
62:     ApplicationRecord.transaction do
63:       @status = Status.create!(@params.merge(quote: @quote))
64:       attach_tags(@status)
65:       attach_tagged_objects(@status)
66:       attach_mentions(@status)
67:       attach_counts(@status)
68:     end
69: 
70:     resolve_thread(@status)
71:     resolve_unresolved_mentions(@status)
72:     resolve_unresolved_collections(@status)
73:     fetch_replies(@status)
74:     fetch_and_verify_quote
75:     distribute
76:     forward_for_reply
77:   end
78: 
79:   def distribute
80:     # Spread out crawling randomly to avoid DDoSing the link
81:     LinkCrawlWorker.perform_in(rand(DISTRIBUTE_DELAY), @status.id)
82: 
83:     # Distribute into home and list feeds and notify mentioned accounts
84:     ::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id, { 'silenced_account_ids' => @silenced_account_ids }) if @options[:override_timestamps] || @status.within_realtime_window?
85:   end
86: 
87:   def find_existing_status
88:     status   = status_from_uri(object_uri)
89:     status ||= Status.find_by(uri: @object['atomUri']) if @object['atomUri'].present?
90:     status if status&.account_id == @account.id
91:   end
92: 
93:   def process_status_params
94:     @status_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::StatusParser.new(
95:       @json,
96:       followers_collection: @account.followers_url,
97:       following_collection: @account.following_url,
98:       actor_uri: ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(@account),
99:       object: @object
100:     )
101: 
102:     attachment_ids = process_attachments.take(Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT).map(&:id)
103: 
104:     @params = {
105:       uri: @status_parser.uri,
106:       url: @status_parser.url || @status_parser.uri,
107:       account: @account,
108:       text: @status_parser.processed_text,
109:       language: @status_parser.language,
110:       spoiler_text: @status_parser.processed_spoiler_text,
111:       created_at: @status_parser.created_at,
112:       edited_at: @status_parser.edited_at && @status_parser.edited_at != @status_parser.created_at ? @status_parser.edited_at : nil,
113:       override_timestamps: @options[:override_timestamps],
114:       reply: @status_parser.reply,
115:       sensitive: @account.sensitized? || @status_parser.sensitive || false,
116:       visibility: @status_parser.visibility,
117:       thread: replied_to_status,
118:       conversation: conversation_from_uri(@object['conversation']),
119:       media_attachment_ids: attachment_ids,
120:       ordered_media_attachment_ids: attachment_ids,
121:       poll: process_poll,
122:       quote_approval_policy: @status_parser.quote_policy,
123:     }
124:   end
125: 
126:   def process_audience
127:     # Unlike with tags, there is no point in resolving accounts we don't already
128:     # know here, because silent mentions would only be used for local access control anyway
129:     accounts_in_audience = (audience_to + audience_cc).uniq.filter_map do |audience|
130:       account_from_uri(audience) unless ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.public_collection?(audience)
131:     end
132: 
133:     # If the payload was delivered to a specific inbox, the inbox owner must have
134:     # access to it, unless they already have access to it anyway
135:     if @options[:delivered_to_account_id]
136:       accounts_in_audience << delivered_to_account
137:       accounts_in_audience.uniq!
138:     end
139: 
140:     accounts_in_audience.each do |account|
141:       # This runs after tags are processed, and those translate into non-silent
142:       # mentions, which take precedence
143:       next if @mentions.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == account.id }
144: 
145:       @mentions << Mention.new(account: account, silent: true)
146: 
147:       # If there is at least one silent mention, then the status can be considered
148:       # as a limited-audience status, and not strictly a direct message, but only
149:       # if we considered a direct message in the first place
150:       @params[:visibility] = :limited if @params[:visibility] == :direct
151:     end
152: 
153:     # Accounts that are tagged but are not in the audience are not
154:     # supposed to be notified explicitly
155:     @silenced_account_ids = @mentions.filter_map { |mention| mention.account_id if mention.account.local? } - accounts_in_audience.map(&:id)
156:   end
157: 
158:   def postprocess_audience_and_deliver
159:     return if @status.mentions.find_by(account_id: @options[:delivered_to_account_id])
160: 
161:     @status.mentions.create(account: delivered_to_account, silent: true)
162:     @status.update(visibility: :limited) if @status.direct_visibility?
163: 
164:     return unless delivered_to_account.following?(@account)
165: 
166:     FeedInsertWorker.perform_async(@status.id, delivered_to_account.id, 'home')
167:   end
168: 
169:   def delivered_to_account
170:     @delivered_to_account ||= Account.find(@options[:delivered_to_account_id])
171:   end
172: 
173:   def attach_tags(status)
174:     @tags.each do |tag|
175:       status.tags << tag
176:       tag.update(last_status_at: status.created_at) if tag.last_status_at.nil? || (tag.last_status_at < status.created_at && tag.last_status_at < 12.hours.ago)
177:     end
178: 
179:     # If we're processing an old status, this may register tags as being used now
180:     # as opposed to when the status was really published, but this is probably
181:     # not a big deal
182:     Trends.tags.register(status)
183: 
184:     # Update featured tags
185:     return if @tags.empty? || !status.distributable?
186: 
187:     @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: @tags.pluck(:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
188:       featured_tag.increment(status.created_at)
189:     end
190:   end
191: 
192:   def attach_tagged_objects(status)
193:     @tagged_objects.each do |tagged_object|
194:       tagged_object.status = status
195:       tagged_object.save
196:     end
197:   end
198: 
199:   def attach_mentions(status)
200:     @mentions.each do |mention|
201:       mention.status = status
202:       mention.save
203:     end
204:   end
205: 
206:   def attach_counts(status)
207:     likes = @status_parser.favourites_count
208:     shares = @status_parser.reblogs_count
209:     return if likes.nil? && shares.nil?
210: 
211:     status.status_stat.tap do |status_stat|
212:       status_stat.untrusted_reblogs_count = shares unless shares.nil?
213:       status_stat.untrusted_favourites_count = likes unless likes.nil?
214:       status_stat.save if status_stat.changed?
215:     end
216:   end
217: 
218:   def process_tags
219:     return if @object['tag'].nil?
220: 
221:     as_array(@object['tag']).each do |tag|
222:       if equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Hashtag')
223:         process_hashtag tag
224:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Mention')
225:         process_mention tag
226:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'Emoji')
227:         process_emoji tag
228:       elsif equals_or_includes?(tag['type'], 'FeaturedCollection')
229:         process_tagged_collection tag
230:       end
231:     end
232:   end
233: 
234:   def process_quote
235:     @quote_uri = @status_parser.quote_uri
236:     return unless @status_parser.quote?
237: 
238:     @quote_approval_uri = @status_parser.quote_approval_uri
239:     @quote_approval_uri = nil if unsupported_uri_scheme?(@quote_approval_uri) || TagManager.instance.local_url?(@quote_approval_uri)
240:     @quote = Quote.new(account: @account, approval_uri: nil, legacy: @status_parser.legacy_quote?, state: @status_parser.deleted_quote? ? :deleted : :pending)
241:   end
242: 
243:   def process_hashtag(tag)
244:     return if tag['name'].blank?
245: 
246:     Tag.find_or_create_by_names(tag['name']) do |hashtag|
247:       @tags << hashtag unless @tags.include?(hashtag) || !hashtag.valid?
248:     end
249:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
250:     nil
251:   end
252: 
253:   def process_mention(tag)
254:     return if tag['href'].blank?
255: 
256:     account = account_from_uri(tag['href'])
257:     account = ActivityPub::FetchRemoteAccountService.new.call(tag['href'], request_id: @options[:request_id]) if account.nil?
258: 
259:     return if account.nil?
260: 
261:     @mentions << Mention.new(account: account, silent: false)
262:   rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
263:     @unresolved_mentions << tag['href']
264:   end
265: 
266:   def process_emoji(tag)
267:     return if skip_download?
268: 
269:     custom_emoji_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::CustomEmojiParser.new(tag)
270: 
271:     return if custom_emoji_parser.shortcode.blank? || custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url.blank?
272: 
273:     emoji = CustomEmoji.find_by(shortcode: custom_emoji_parser.shortcode, domain: @account.domain)
274: 
275:     return unless emoji.nil? || custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url != emoji.image_remote_url || (custom_emoji_parser.updated_at && custom_emoji_parser.updated_at >= emoji.updated_at)
276: 
277:     begin
278:       emoji ||= CustomEmoji.new(domain: @account.domain, shortcode: custom_emoji_parser.shortcode, uri: custom_emoji_parser.uri)
279:       emoji.image_remote_url = custom_emoji_parser.image_remote_url
280:       emoji.save
281:     rescue Seahorse::Client::NetworkingError => e
282:       Rails.logger.warn "Error storing emoji: #{e}"
283:     end
284:   end
285: 
286:   def process_tagged_collection(tag)
287:     return if tag['id'].blank?
288: 
289:     # TODO: We probably want to resolve unknown objects and push them to an `@unresolved_tagged_objects` on failure
290:     collection = ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(tag['id'], Collection)
291:     collection ||= ActivityPub::FetchRemoteFeaturedCollectionService.new.call(tag['id'], request_id: @options[:request_id])
292: 
293:     @tagged_objects << TaggedObject.new(uri: ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(collection), object: collection, ap_type: 'FeaturedCollection') if collection.present?
294:   rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
295:     @unresolved_collections << tag['id']
296:   end
297: 
298:   def process_attachments
299:     return [] if @object['attachment'].nil?
300: 
301:     media_attachments = []
302: 
303:     as_array(@object['attachment']).each do |attachment|
304:       media_attachment_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::MediaAttachmentParser.new(attachment)
305: 
306:       next if media_attachment_parser.remote_url.blank? || media_attachments.size >= Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT
307: 
308:       begin
309:         media_attachment = MediaAttachment.create(
310:           account: @account,
311:           remote_url: media_attachment_parser.remote_url,
312:           thumbnail_remote_url: media_attachment_parser.thumbnail_remote_url,
313:           description: media_attachment_parser.description,
314:           focus: media_attachment_parser.focus,
315:           blurhash: media_attachment_parser.blurhash
316:         )
317: 
318:         media_attachments << media_attachment
319: 
320:         next if unsupported_media_type?(media_attachment_parser.file_content_type) || skip_download?
321: 
322:         media_attachment.download_file!
323:         media_attachment.download_thumbnail!
324:         media_attachment.save
325:       rescue Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
326:         RedownloadMediaWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), media_attachment.id)
327:       rescue Seahorse::Client::NetworkingError => e
328:         Rails.logger.warn "Error storing media attachment: #{e}"
329:         RedownloadMediaWorker.perform_async(media_attachment.id)
330:       end
331:     end
332: 
333:     media_attachments
334:   rescue Addressable::URI::InvalidURIError => e
335:     Rails.logger.debug { "Invalid URL in attachment: #{e}" }
336:     media_attachments
337:   end
338: 
339:   def process_poll
340:     poll_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::PollParser.new(@object)
341: 
342:     return unless poll_parser.valid?
343: 
344:     @account.polls.new(
345:       multiple: poll_parser.multiple,
346:       expires_at: poll_parser.expires_at,
347:       options: poll_parser.options,
348:       cached_tallies: poll_parser.cached_tallies,
349:       voters_count: poll_parser.voters_count
350:     )
351:   end
352: 
353:   def poll_vote?
354:     return false if replied_to_status.nil? || replied_to_status.preloadable_poll.nil? || !replied_to_status.local? || !replied_to_status.preloadable_poll.options.include?(@object['name'])
355: 
356:     poll_vote! unless replied_to_status.preloadable_poll.expired?
357: 
358:     true
359:   end
360: 
361:   def poll_vote!
362:     poll = replied_to_status.preloadable_poll
363:     already_voted = true
364: 
365:     with_redis_lock("vote:#{replied_to_status.poll_id}:#{@account.id}") do
366:       already_voted = poll.votes.exists?(account: @account)
367:       poll.votes.create!(account: @account, choice: poll.options.index(@object['name']), uri: object_uri)
368:     end
369: 
370:     increment_voters_count! unless already_voted
371:     ActivityPub::DistributePollUpdateWorker.perform_in(3.minutes, replied_to_status.id) unless replied_to_status.preloadable_poll.hide_totals?
372:   end
373: 
374:   def resolve_thread(status)
375:     return unless status.reply? && status.thread.nil? && Request.valid_url?(in_reply_to_uri)
376: 
377:     ThreadResolveWorker.perform_async(status.id, in_reply_to_uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id] })
378:   end
379: 
380:   def resolve_unresolved_mentions(status)
381:     @unresolved_mentions.uniq.each do |uri|
382:       MentionResolveWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), status.id, uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id] })
383:     end
384:   end
385: 
386:   def resolve_unresolved_collections(status)
387:     @unresolved_collections.uniq.each do |uri|
388:       TaggedCollectionResolveWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), status.id, uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id] })
389:     end
390:   end
391: 
392:   def fetch_replies(status)
393:     collection = @object['replies']
394:     return if collection.blank?
395: 
396:     replies = ActivityPub::FetchRepliesService.new.call(status.account.uri, collection, allow_synchronous_requests: false, request_id: @options[:request_id])
397:     return unless replies.nil?
398: 
399:     uri = value_or_id(collection)
400:     ActivityPub::FetchRepliesWorker.perform_async(status.id, uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id] }) unless uri.nil?
401:   rescue => e
402:     Rails.logger.warn "Error fetching replies: #{e}"
403:   end
404: 
405:   def fetch_and_verify_quote
406:     return if @quote.nil?
407: 
408:     embedded_quote = safe_prefetched_embed(@account, @status_parser.quoted_object, @json['context'])
409:     ActivityPub::VerifyQuoteService.new.call(@quote, @quote_approval_uri, fetchable_quoted_uri: @quote_uri, prefetched_quoted_object: embedded_quote, request_id: @options[:request_id], depth: @options[:depth])
410:   rescue Mastodon::RecursionLimitExceededError, Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS
411:     ActivityPub::RefetchAndVerifyQuoteWorker.perform_in(rand(PROCESSING_DELAY), @quote.id, @quote_uri, { 'request_id' => @options[:request_id], 'approval_uri' => @quote_approval_uri })
412:   end
413: 
414:   def conversation_from_uri(uri)
415:     return nil if uri.nil?
416:     return Conversation.find_by(id: OStatus::TagManager.instance.unique_tag_to_local_id(uri, 'Conversation')) if OStatus::TagManager.instance.local_id?(uri)
417:     return ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(uri, Conversation) if ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.local_uri?(uri)
418: 
419:     begin
420:       Conversation.find_or_create_by!(uri: uri)
421:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
422:       retry
423:     end
424:   end
425: 
426:   def replied_to_status
427:     return @replied_to_status if defined?(@replied_to_status)
428: 
429:     if in_reply_to_uri.blank?
430:       @replied_to_status = nil
431:     else
432:       @replied_to_status   = status_from_uri(in_reply_to_uri)
433:       @replied_to_status ||= status_from_uri(@object['inReplyToAtomUri']) if @object['inReplyToAtomUri'].present?
434:       @replied_to_status
435:     end
436:   end
437: 
438:   def in_reply_to_uri
439:     value_or_id(@object['inReplyTo'])
440:   end
441: 
442:   def unsupported_media_type?(mime_type)
443:     mime_type.present? && !MediaAttachment.supported_mime_types.include?(mime_type)
444:   end
445: 
446:   def skip_download?
447:     return @skip_download if defined?(@skip_download)
448: 
449:     @skip_download ||= DomainBlock.reject_media?(@account.domain)
450:   end
451: 
452:   def reply_to_local?
453:     !replied_to_status.nil? && replied_to_status.account.local?
454:   end
455: 
456:   def related_to_local_activity?
457:     fetch? || followed_by_local_accounts? || requested_through_relay? ||
458:       responds_to_followed_account? || addresses_local_accounts?
459:   end
460: 
461:   def responds_to_followed_account?
462:     !replied_to_status.nil? && (replied_to_status.account.local? || replied_to_status.account.passive_relationships.exists?)
463:   end
464: 
465:   def addresses_local_accounts?
466:     return true if @options[:delivered_to_account_id]
467: 
468:     ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uris_to_local_accounts((audience_to + audience_cc).uniq).exists?
469:   end
470: 
471:   def tombstone_exists?
472:     Tombstone.exists?(uri: object_uri)
473:   end
474: 
475:   def forward_for_reply
476:     return unless @status.distributable? && @json['signature'].present? && reply_to_local?
477: 
478:     ActivityPub::RawDistributionWorker.perform_async(JSON.generate(@json), replied_to_status.account_id, [@account.preferred_inbox_url])
479:   end
480: 
481:   def increment_voters_count!
482:     poll = replied_to_status.preloadable_poll
483: 
484:     unless poll.voters_count.nil?
485:       poll.voters_count = poll.voters_count + 1
486:       poll.save
487:     end
488:   rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
489:     poll.reload
490:     retry
491:   end
492: end
