1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class Fasp::AnnounceTrendWorker < Fasp::BaseWorker
4:   sidekiq_options retry: 5
5: 
6:   def perform(status_id, trend_source)
7:     status = ::Status.includes(:account).find(status_id)
8:     return unless status.account.indexable?
9: 
10:     Fasp::Subscription.includes(:fasp_provider).category_content.trends.each do |subscription|
11:       with_provider(subscription.fasp_provider) do
12:         announce(subscription, status.uri) if trending?(subscription, status, trend_source)
13:       end
14:     end
15:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
16:     # status might not exist anymore, in which case there is nothing to do
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def trending?(subscription, status, trend_source)
22:     scope = scope_for(status, trend_source)
23:     threshold = threshold_for(subscription, trend_source)
24:     scope.where(created_at: subscription.timeframe_start..).count >= threshold
25:   end
26: 
27:   def scope_for(status, trend_source)
28:     case trend_source
29:     when 'favourite'
30:       status.favourites
31:     when 'reblog'
32:       status.reblogs
33:     when 'reply'
34:       status.replies
35:     end
36:   end
37: 
38:   def threshold_for(subscription, trend_source)
39:     case trend_source
40:     when 'favourite'
41:       subscription.threshold_likes
42:     when 'reblog'
43:       subscription.threshold_shares
44:     when 'reply'
45:       subscription.threshold_replies
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def announce(subscription, uri)
50:     Fasp::Request.new(subscription.fasp_provider).post('/data_sharing/v0/announcements', body: {
51:       source: {
52:         subscription: {
53:           id: subscription.id.to_s,
54:         },
55:       },
56:       category: 'content',
57:       eventType: 'trending',
58:       objectUris: [uri],
59:     })
60:   end
61: end

3: class RevokeQuoteService < BaseService
4:   include Payloadable
5: 
6:   def call(quote)
7:     @quote = quote
8:     @account = quote.quoted_account
9: 
10:     @quote.reject!
11:     distribute_update!
12:     distribute_stamp_deletion!
13:   end

15:   private
16: 
17:   def distribute_update!
18:     return if @quote.status_id.nil?
19: 
20:     DistributionWorker.perform_async(@quote.status_id, { 'update' => true })
21:   end
22: 
23:   def distribute_stamp_deletion!
24:     # It is possible the quoted status has been soft-deleted.
25:     # In this case, `signed_activity_json` would fail, but we can just ignore
26:     # that, as we have already federated deletion.
27:     return if @quote.quoted_status.nil?
28: 
29:     ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker.push_bulk(inboxes, limit: 1_000) do |inbox_url|
30:       [signed_activity_json, @account.id, inbox_url]
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   def inboxes
35:     [
36:       @quote.status,
37:       @quote.quoted_status,
38:     ].compact.map { |status| StatusReachFinder.new(status, unsafe: true).inboxes }.flatten.uniq
39:   end
40:

42:     @signed_activity_json ||= serialize_payload(@quote, ActivityPub::DeleteQuoteAuthorizationSerializer, signer: @account, always_sign: true, force_approval_id: true).to_json

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ActivityPub::VerifyQuoteService < BaseService
4:   include JsonLdHelper
5: 
6:   MAX_SYNCHRONOUS_DEPTH = 2
7: 
8:   # Optionally fetch quoted post, and verify the quote is authorized
9:   def call(quote, approval_uri, fetchable_quoted_uri: nil, prefetched_quoted_object: nil, prefetched_approval: nil, request_id: nil, depth: nil)
10:     @request_id = request_id
11:     @depth = depth || 0
12:     @quote = quote
13:     @approval_uri = approval_uri.presence || @quote.approval_uri
14:     @fetching_error = nil
15: 
16:     fetch_quoted_post_if_needed!(fetchable_quoted_uri, prefetched_body: prefetched_quoted_object)
17:     return if quote.quoted_account&.local?
18:     return if fast_track_approval! || @approval_uri.blank?
19: 
20:     @json = fetch_approval_object(@approval_uri, prefetched_body: prefetched_approval)
21:     return quote.reject! if @json.nil?
22: 
23:     return if non_matching_uri_hosts?(@approval_uri, value_or_id(@json['attributedTo']))
24:     return unless matching_type? && matching_quote_uri?
25: 
26:     # Opportunistically import embedded posts if needed
27:     return if import_quoted_post_if_needed!(fetchable_quoted_uri) && fast_track_approval!
28: 
29:     # Raise an error if we failed to fetch the status
30:     raise @fetching_error if @quote.status.nil? && @fetching_error
31: 
32:     return unless matching_quoted_post? && matching_quoted_author?
33: 
34:     quote.accept!(approval_uri: @approval_uri)
35:   end
36: 
37:   private
38: 
39:   # FEP-044f defines rules that don't require the approval flow
40:   def fast_track_approval!
41:     return false if @quote.quoted_status_id.blank?
42: 
43:     # Always allow someone to quote themselves
44:     if @quote.account_id == @quote.quoted_account_id
45:       @quote.accept!
46: 
47:       true
48:     end
49: 
50:     false
51:   end
52: 
53:   def fetch_approval_object(uri, prefetched_body: nil)
54:     if prefetched_body.nil?
55:       fetch_resource(uri, true, @quote.account.followers.local.first, raise_on_error: :temporary)
56:     else
57:       body_to_json(prefetched_body, compare_id: uri)
58:     end
59:   end
60: 
61:   def matching_type?
62:     supported_context?(@json) && equals_or_includes?(@json['type'], 'QuoteAuthorization')
63:   end
64: 
65:   def matching_quote_uri?
66:     ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(@quote.status) == value_or_id(@json['interactingObject'])
67:   end
68: 
69:   def fetch_quoted_post_if_needed!(uri, prefetched_body: nil)
70:     return if uri.nil? || @quote.quoted_status.present?
71: 
72:     status = ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_to_resource(uri, Status)
73:     raise Mastodon::RecursionLimitExceededError if @depth > MAX_SYNCHRONOUS_DEPTH && status.nil?
74: 
75:     status ||= ActivityPub::FetchRemoteStatusService.new.call(uri, on_behalf_of: @quote.account.followers.local.first, prefetched_body:, request_id: @request_id, depth: @depth + 1)
76: 
77:     @quote.update(quoted_status: status) if status.present? && !status.reblog?
78:   rescue Mastodon::RecursionLimitExceededError, Mastodon::UnexpectedResponseError, *Mastodon::HTTP_CONNECTION_ERRORS => e
79:     @fetching_error = e
80:   end
81: 
82:   def import_quoted_post_if_needed!(uri)
83:     # No need to fetch if we already have a post
84:     return if uri.nil? || @quote.quoted_status_id.present? || !@json['interactionTarget'].is_a?(Hash)
85: 
86:     # NOTE: Replacing the object's context by that of the parent activity is
87:     # not sound, but it's consistent with the rest of the codebase
88:     object = @json['interactionTarget'].merge({ '@context' => @json['@context'] })
89: 
90:     # It's not safe to fetch if the inlined object is cross-origin or doesn't match expectations
91:     return if object['id'] != uri || non_matching_uri_hosts?(@approval_uri, object['id'])
92: 
93:     status = ActivityPub::FetchRemoteStatusService.new.call(object['id'], prefetched_body: object, on_behalf_of: @quote.account.followers.local.first, request_id: @request_id, depth: @depth)
94: 
95:     if status.present? && !status.reblog?
96:       @quote.update(quoted_status: status)
97:       true
98:     else
99:       false
100:     end
101:   end
102: 
103:   def matching_quoted_post?
104:     return false if @quote.quoted_status_id.blank?
105: 
106:     ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(@quote.quoted_status) == value_or_id(@json['interactionTarget'])
107:   end
108: 
109:   def matching_quoted_author?
110:     ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(@quote.quoted_account) == value_or_id(@json['attributedTo'])
111:   end
112: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Account::Counters
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   ALLOWED_COUNTER_KEYS = %i(statuses_count following_count followers_count).freeze
7: 
8:   included do
9:     has_one :account_stat, inverse_of: :account, dependent: nil
10:     after_save :save_account_stat
11:   end
12: 
13:   delegate :statuses_count,
14:            :statuses_count=,
15:            :following_count,
16:            :following_count=,
17:            :followers_count,
18:            :followers_count=,
19:            :last_status_at,
20:            to: :account_stat
21: 
22:   # @param [Symbol] key
23:   def increment_count!(key, status_created_at: nil)
24:     update_count!(key, 1, status_created_at:)
25:   end
26: 
27:   # @param [Symbol] key
28:   def decrement_count!(key)
29:     update_count!(key, -1)
30:   end
31: 
32:   # @param [Symbol] key
33:   # @param [Integer] value
34:   def update_count!(key, value, status_created_at: nil)
35:     raise ArgumentError, "Invalid key #{key}" unless ALLOWED_COUNTER_KEYS.include?(key)
36:     raise ArgumentError, 'Do not call update_count! on dirty objects' if association(:account_stat).loaded? && account_stat&.changed? && account_stat.changed_attribute_names_to_save == %w(id)
37: 
38:     result = updated_account_stat(key, value.to_i, status_created_at:)
39: 
40:     # Reload account_stat if it was loaded, taking into account newly-created unsaved records
41:     if association(:account_stat).loaded?
42:       account_stat.id = result.first['id'] if account_stat.new_record?
43:       account_stat.reload
44:     end
45:   end
46: 
47:   def account_stat
48:     super || build_account_stat
49:   end
50: 
51:   private
52: 
53:   def updated_account_stat(key, value, status_created_at: nil)
54:     status_created_at = Time.now.utc if status_created_at.nil? || status_created_at > Time.now.utc
55: 
56:     AccountStat.upsert(
57:       initial_values(key, value, status_created_at:),
58:       on_duplicate: Arel.sql(
59:         duplicate_values(key, value, status_created_at:).join(', ')
60:       ),
61:       unique_by: :account_id
62:     )
63:   end
64: 
65:   def initial_values(key, value, status_created_at: nil)
66:     { :account_id => id, key => [value, 0].max }.tap do |values|
67:       values.merge!(last_status_at: status_created_at) if key == :statuses_count
68:     end
69:   end
70: 
71:   def duplicate_values(key, value, status_created_at: nil)
72:     ["#{key} = (account_stats.#{key} + #{value})", 'updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP'].tap do |values|
73:       values << AccountStat.sanitize_sql_array(['last_status_at = GREATEST(account_stats.last_status_at, ?::timestamp)', status_created_at]) if key == :statuses_count && value.positive?
74:     end
75:   end
76: 
77:   def save_account_stat
78:     return unless association(:account_stat).loaded? && account_stat&.changed?
79: 
80:     account_stat.save
81:   end
82: end

22: class Tag < ApplicationRecord
23:   include Paginable
24:   include Reviewable
25: 
26:   # rubocop:disable Rails/HasAndBelongsToMany
27:   has_and_belongs_to_many :statuses
28:   has_and_belongs_to_many :accounts

33:   has_many :followers, through: :passive_relationships, source: :account
34: 
35:   has_one :trend, class_name: 'TagTrend', inverse_of: :tag, dependent: :destroy
36: 
37:   HASHTAG_SEPARATORS = "_\u00B7\u30FB\u200c"
38:   HASHTAG_FIRST_SEQUENCE_CHUNK_ONE = "[[:word:]_][[:word:]#{HASHTAG_SEPARATORS}]*[[:alpha:]#{HASHTAG_SEPARATORS}]".freeze
39:   HASHTAG_FIRST_SEQUENCE_CHUNK_TWO = "[[:word:]#{HASHTAG_SEPARATORS}]*[[:word:]_]".freeze
40:   HASHTAG_FIRST_SEQUENCE = "(#{HASHTAG_FIRST_SEQUENCE_CHUNK_ONE}#{HASHTAG_FIRST_SEQUENCE_CHUNK_TWO})".freeze
41:   HASHTAG_LAST_SEQUENCE = '([[:word:]_]*[[:alpha:]][[:word:]_]*)'
42:   HASHTAG_NAME_PAT = "#{HASHTAG_FIRST_SEQUENCE}|#{HASHTAG_LAST_SEQUENCE}".freeze
43: 
44:   HASHTAG_RE = /(?<=^|[[:space:]])[#＃](#{HASHTAG_NAME_PAT})/
45:   HASHTAG_NAME_RE = /\A(#{HASHTAG_NAME_PAT})\z/i
46:   HASHTAG_INVALID_CHARS_RE = /[^[:alnum:]\u0E47-\u0E4E#{HASHTAG_SEPARATORS}]/
47: 
48:   RECENT_STATUS_LIMIT = 1000
49: 
50:   validates :name, presence: true, format: { with: HASHTAG_NAME_RE }
51:   validates :display_name, format: { with: HASHTAG_NAME_RE }
52:   validate :validate_name_change, on: :update, if: :name_changed?
53:   validate :validate_display_name_change, on: :update, if: :display_name_changed?
54: 
55:   scope :pending_review, -> { unreviewed.where.not(requested_review_at: nil) }
56:   scope :usable, -> { where(usable: [true, nil]) }
57:   scope :not_usable, -> { where(usable: false) }
58:   scope :listable, -> { where(listable: [true, nil]) }
59:   scope :trendable, -> { Setting.trendable_by_default ? where(trendable: [true, nil]) : where(trendable: true) }
60:   scope :not_trendable, -> { where(trendable: false) }
61:   scope :suggestions_for_account, ->(account) { recently_used(account).not_featured_by(account) }
62:   scope :not_featured_by, ->(account) { where.not(id: account.featured_tags.select(:tag_id)) }
63:   scope :recently_used, lambda { |account|
64:                           joins(:statuses)
65:                             .where(statuses: { id: account.statuses.select(:id).limit(RECENT_STATUS_LIMIT) })
66:                             .group(:id).order(Arel.star.count.desc)
67:                         }
68:   scope :matches_name, ->(term) { where(arel_table[:name].lower.matches(arel_table.lower("#{sanitize_sql_like(normalize_value_for(:name, term))}%"), nil, true)) } # Search with case-sensitive to use B-tree index
69: 
70:   normalizes :name, with: ->(value) { HashtagNormalizer.new.normalize(value) }
71:   normalizes :display_name, with: ->(value) { value.gsub(HASHTAG_INVALID_CHARS_RE, '') }
72: 
73:   update_index('tags', :self)
74: 
75:   def to_param
76:     name
77:   end
78: 
79:   def display_name
80:     attributes['display_name'] || name
81:   end
82: 
83:   def formatted_name
84:     "##{display_name}"
85:   end
86: 
87:   def usable
88:     boolean_with_default('usable', true)
89:   end
90: 
91:   alias usable? usable
92: 
93:   def listable
94:     boolean_with_default('listable', true)
95:   end
96: 
97:   alias listable? listable
98: 
99:   def trendable
100:     boolean_with_default('trendable', Setting.trendable_by_default)
101:   end
102: 
103:   alias trendable? trendable
104: 
105:   def decaying?
106:     max_score_at && max_score_at >= Trends.tags.options[:max_score_cooldown].ago && max_score_at < 1.day.ago
107:   end
108: 
109:   def history
110:     @history ||= Trends::History.new('tags', id)
111:   end
112: 
113:   class << self
114:     def find_or_create_by_names(name_or_names)
115:       names = Array(name_or_names).map { |str| [normalize_value_for(:name, str), str] }.uniq(&:first)
116: 
117:       names.map do |name, display_name|
118:         tag = begin
119:           matching_name(name).first || create!(name:, display_name:)
120:         rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
121:           find_normalized(name)
122:         end
123: 
124:         yield tag if block_given?
125: 
126:         tag
127:       end
128:     end
129: 
130:     def search_for(term, limit = 5, offset = 0, options = {})
131:       stripped_term = term.to_s.strip
132:       options.reverse_merge!({ exclude_unlistable: true, exclude_unreviewed: false })
133: 
134:       query = Tag.matches_name(stripped_term)
135:       query = query.merge(Tag.listable) if options[:exclude_unlistable]
136:       query = query.merge(matching_name(stripped_term).or(reviewed)) if options[:exclude_unreviewed]
137: 
138:       query.order(Arel.sql('LENGTH(name)').asc, name: :asc)
139:         .limit(limit)
140:         .offset(offset)
141:     end
142: 
143:     def find_normalized(name)
144:       matching_name(name).first
145:     end
146: 
147:     def find_normalized!(name)
148:       find_normalized(name) || raise(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
149:     end
150: 
151:     def matching_name(name_or_names)
152:       names = Array(name_or_names).map { |name| arel_table.lower(normalize_value_for(:name, name)) }
153: 
154:       if names.size == 1
155:         where(arel_table[:name].lower.eq(names.first))
156:       else
157:         where(arel_table[:name].lower.in(names))
158:       end
159:     end
160:   end
161: 
162:   private
163: 
164:   def validate_name_change
165:     errors.add(:name, I18n.t('tags.does_not_match_previous_name')) unless name_was.casecmp(name).zero?
166:   end
167: 
168:   def validate_display_name_change
169:     errors.add(:display_name, I18n.t('tags.does_not_match_previous_name')) unless display_name_matches_name?
170:   end
171: 
172:   def display_name_matches_name?
173:     self.class.normalize_value_for(:name, display_name).casecmp(name).zero?
174:   end
175: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class StatusRelationshipsPresenter
4:   PINNABLE_VISIBILITIES = %w(public unlisted private).freeze
5: 
6:   attr_reader :reblogs_map, :favourites_map, :mutes_map, :pins_map,
7:               :bookmarks_map, :filters_map, :attributes_map
8: 
9:   def initialize(statuses, current_account_id = nil, **options)
10:     @current_account_id = current_account_id
11: 
12:     # Keeping a reference to @statuses is ok since `StatusRelationshipsPresenter`
13:     # basically never outlives the statuses collection it is passed
14:     @statuses = statuses
15: 
16:     if current_account_id.nil?
17:       @preloaded_account_relations = {}
18:       @filters_map     = {}
19:       @reblogs_map     = {}
20:       @favourites_map  = {}
21:       @bookmarks_map   = {}
22:       @mutes_map       = {}
23:       @pins_map        = {}
24:       @attributes_map  = {}
25:     else
26:       @preloaded_account_relations = nil
27: 
28:       statuses            = statuses.compact
29:       status_ids          = statuses.flat_map { |s| [s.id, s.reblog_of_id, s.proper.quote&.quoted_status_id] }.uniq.compact
30:       conversation_ids    = statuses.flat_map { |s| [s.proper.conversation_id, s.proper.quote&.quoted_status&.conversation_id] }.uniq.compact
31:       pinnable_status_ids = statuses.flat_map { |s| [s.proper, s.proper.quote&.quoted_status] }.compact.filter_map { |s| s.id if s.account_id == current_account_id && PINNABLE_VISIBILITIES.include?(s.visibility) }
32: 
33:       @filters_map     = build_filters_map(statuses.flat_map { |s| [s, s.proper.quote&.quoted_status] }.compact.uniq, current_account_id).merge(options[:filters_map] || {})
34:       @reblogs_map     = Status.reblogs_map(status_ids, current_account_id).merge(options[:reblogs_map] || {})
35:       @favourites_map  = Status.favourites_map(status_ids, current_account_id).merge(options[:favourites_map] || {})
36:       @bookmarks_map   = Status.bookmarks_map(status_ids, current_account_id).merge(options[:bookmarks_map] || {})
37:       @mutes_map       = Status.mutes_map(conversation_ids, current_account_id).merge(options[:mutes_map] || {})
38:       @pins_map        = Status.pins_map(pinnable_status_ids, current_account_id).merge(options[:pins_map] || {})
39:       @attributes_map  = options[:attributes_map] || {}
40:     end
41:   end
42: 
43:   # This one is currently on-demand as it is only used for quote posts
44:   def authoring_accounts
45:     @authoring_accounts ||= @statuses.compact.flat_map { |s| [s.account, s.proper.account, s.proper.quote&.quoted_account] }.uniq.compact
46:   end
47: 
48:   private
49: 
50:   def build_filters_map(statuses, current_account_id)
51:     active_filters = CustomFilter.cached_filters_for(current_account_id)
52: 
53:     @filters_map = statuses.each_with_object({}) do |status, h|
54:       filter_matches = CustomFilter.apply_cached_filters(active_filters, status)
55: 
56:       unless filter_matches.empty?
57:         h[status.id] = filter_matches
58:         h[status.reblog_of_id] = filter_matches if status.reblog?
59:       end
60:     end
61:   end
62: end

5:     {
6:       top_statuses: {
7:         by_reblogs: status_identifier(most_reblogged_status),
8:         by_favourites: nil,
9:         by_replies: nil,
10:       },
11:     }
12:   end

16:   end

21:     status.id.to_s if status.present?
22:   end
23: 
24:   def most_reblogged_status
25:     base_scope
26:       .order(reblogs_count: :desc)
27:       .first
28:   end
29: 
30:   def most_favourited_status
31:     base_scope
32:       .excluding(most_reblogged_status)
33:       .order(favourites_count: :desc)
34:       .first
35:   end
36: 
37:   def most_replied_status
38:     base_scope
39:       .excluding(most_reblogged_status, most_favourited_status)
40:       .order(replies_count: :desc)
41:       .first
42:   end
43: 
44:   def base_scope
45:     report_statuses
46:       .distributable_visibility
47:       .joins(:status_stat)
48:   end
49: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: class AnnualReport::TopHashtags < AnnualReport::Source
4:   MINIMUM_TAGGINGS = 1
5:   SET_SIZE = 1
6: 
7:   def generate
8:     {
9:       top_hashtags: top_hashtags.map do |(name, count)|
10:                       {
11:                         name: name,
12:                         count: count,
13:                       }
14:                     end,
15:     }
16:   end
17: 
18:   def eligible?
19:     report_statuses.joins(:tags).exists?
20:   end
21: 
22:   private
23: 
24:   def top_hashtags
25:     Tag.joins(:statuses).where(statuses: { id: report_statuses.select(:id) }).group(coalesced_tag_names).having(minimum_taggings_count).order(count_all: :desc).limit(SET_SIZE).count
26:   end
27: 
28:   def minimum_taggings_count
29:     Arel.star.count.gt(MINIMUM_TAGGINGS)
30:   end
31: 
32:   def coalesced_tag_names
33:     Arel.sql(<<~SQL.squish)
34:       COALESCE(tags.display_name, tags.name)
35:     SQL
36:   end
37: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class Feed
4:   include Redisable
5: 
6:   def initialize(type, id)
7:     @type = type
8:     @id   = id
9:   end
10: 
11:   def get(limit, max_id = nil, since_id = nil, min_id = nil)
12:     limit    = limit.to_i
13:     max_id   = max_id.to_i if max_id.present?
14:     since_id = since_id.to_i if since_id.present?
15:     min_id   = min_id.to_i if min_id.present?
16: 
17:     from_redis(limit, max_id, since_id, min_id)
18:   end
19: 
20:   protected
21: 
22:   def from_redis(limit, max_id, since_id, min_id)
23:     max_id = '+inf' if max_id.blank?
24:     if min_id.blank?
25:       since_id   = '-inf' if since_id.blank?
26:       unhydrated = redis.zrevrangebyscore(key, "(#{max_id}", "(#{since_id}", limit: [0, limit], with_scores: true).map { |id| id.first.to_i }
27:     else
28:       unhydrated = redis.zrangebyscore(key, "(#{min_id}", "(#{max_id}", limit: [0, limit], with_scores: true).map { |id| id.first.to_i }
29:     end
30: 
31:     Status.where(id: unhydrated)
32:   end
33: 
34:   def key
35:     FeedManager.instance.key(@type, @id)
36:   end
37: end

3: class HomeFeed < Feed
4:   def initialize(account)
5:     @account = account
6:     super(:home, account.id)
7:   end
8: 
9:   def async_refresh
10:     @async_refresh ||= AsyncRefresh.new(redis_regeneration_key)
11:   end
12: 
13:   def regenerating?
14:     async_refresh.running?
15:   rescue Redis::CommandError
16:     retry if upgrade_redis_key!
17:   end
18: 
19:   def regeneration_in_progress!
20:     @async_refresh = AsyncRefresh.create(redis_regeneration_key)
21:   rescue Redis::CommandError
22:     upgrade_redis_key!
23:   end
24: 
25:   def regeneration_finished!
26:     async_refresh.finish!
27:   rescue Redis::CommandError
28:     retry if upgrade_redis_key!
29:   end
30: 
31:   private
32: 
33:   def redis_regeneration_key
34:     @redis_regeneration_key = "account:#{@account.id}:regeneration"
35:   end
36: 
37:   def upgrade_redis_key!
38:     if redis.type(redis_regeneration_key) == 'string'
39:       redis.del(redis_regeneration_key)
40:       regeneration_in_progress!
41:       true
42:     end
43:   end
44: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class PublicFeed
4:   # @param [Account] account
5:   # @param [Hash] options
6:   # @option [Boolean] :with_replies
7:   # @option [Boolean] :with_reblogs
8:   # @option [Boolean] :local
9:   # @option [Boolean] :remote
10:   # @option [Boolean] :only_media
11:   def initialize(account, options = {})
12:     @account = account
13:     @options = options
14:   end
15: 
16:   # @param [Integer] limit
17:   # @param [Integer] max_id
18:   # @param [Integer] since_id
19:   # @param [Integer] min_id
20:   # @return [Array<Status>]
21:   def get(limit, max_id = nil, since_id = nil, min_id = nil)
22:     return [] if incompatible_feed_settings?
23: 
24:     scope = public_scope
25: 
26:     scope.merge!(without_replies_scope) unless with_replies?
27:     scope.merge!(without_reblogs_scope) unless with_reblogs?
28:     scope.merge!(local_only_scope) if local_only?
29:     scope.merge!(remote_only_scope) if remote_only?
30:     scope.merge!(account_filters_scope) if account?
31:     scope.merge!(media_only_scope) if media_only?
32:     scope.merge!(language_scope) if account&.chosen_languages.present?
33: 
34:     scope.to_a_paginated_by_id(limit, max_id: max_id, since_id: since_id, min_id: min_id)
35:   end
36: 
37:   private
38: 
39:   attr_reader :account, :options
40: 
41:   def incompatible_feed_settings?
42:     (local_only? && !user_has_access_to_feed?(local_feed_setting)) || (remote_only? && !user_has_access_to_feed?(remote_feed_setting))
43:   end
44: 
45:   def user_has_access_to_feed?(setting)
46:     case setting
47:     when 'public'
48:       true
49:     when 'authenticated'
50:       @account&.user&.functional?
51:     when 'disabled'
52:       @account&.user&.can?(:view_feeds)
53:     end
54:   end
55: 
56:   def with_reblogs?
57:     options[:with_reblogs]
58:   end
59: 
60:   def with_replies?
61:     options[:with_replies]
62:   end
63: 
64:   def local_feed_setting
65:     Setting.local_live_feed_access
66:   end
67: 
68:   def remote_feed_setting
69:     Setting.remote_live_feed_access
70:   end
71: 
72:   def local_only?
73:     (options[:local] && !options[:remote]) || !user_has_access_to_feed?(remote_feed_setting)
74:   end
75: 
76:   def remote_only?
77:     (options[:remote] && !options[:local]) || !user_has_access_to_feed?(local_feed_setting)
78:   end
79: 
80:   def account?
81:     account.present?
82:   end
83: 
84:   def media_only?
85:     options[:only_media]
86:   end
87: 
88:   def public_scope
89:     Status.public_visibility.joins(:account).merge(Account.without_suspended.without_silenced)
90:   end
91: 
92:   def local_only_scope
93:     Status.local
94:   end
95: 
96:   def remote_only_scope
97:     Status.remote
98:   end
99: 
100:   def without_replies_scope
101:     Status.without_replies
102:   end
103: 
104:   def without_reblogs_scope
105:     Status.without_reblogs
106:   end
107: 
108:   def media_only_scope
109:     Status.joins(:media_attachments).group(:id)
110:   end
111: 
112:   def language_scope
113:     Status.where(language: account.chosen_languages)
114:   end
115: 
116:   def account_filters_scope
117:     Status.not_excluded_by_account(account).tap do |scope|
118:       scope.merge!(Status.not_domain_blocked_by_account(account)) unless local_only?
119:     end
120:   end
121: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class Api::V1::StatusesController < Api::BaseController
4:   include Authorization
5:   include Api::InteractionPoliciesConcern
6: 
7:   before_action -> { authorize_if_got_token! :read, :'read:statuses' }, except: [:create, :update, :destroy]
8:   before_action -> { doorkeeper_authorize! :write, :'write:statuses' }, only:   [:create, :update, :destroy]
9:   before_action :require_user!, except:      [:index, :show]
10:   before_action :set_statuses, only:         [:index]
11:   before_action :set_status, only:           [:show]
12:   before_action :set_thread, only:           [:create]
13:   before_action :set_quoted_status, only:    [:create]
14:   before_action :check_statuses_limit, only: [:index]
15: 
16:   override_rate_limit_headers :create, family: :statuses
17:   override_rate_limit_headers :update, family: :statuses
18: 
19:   def index
20:     @statuses = preload_collection(@statuses, Status)
21:     render json: @statuses, each_serializer: REST::StatusSerializer
22:   end
23: 
24:   def show
25:     cache_if_unauthenticated!
26:     @status = preload_collection([@status], Status).first
27:     render json: @status, serializer: REST::StatusSerializer
28:   end
29: 
30:   def create
31:     @status = PostStatusService.new.call(
32:       current_user.account,
33:       text: status_params[:status],
34:       thread: @thread,
35:       quoted_status: @quoted_status,
36:       quote_approval_policy: quote_approval_policy,
37:       media_ids: status_params[:media_ids],
38:       sensitive: status_params[:sensitive],
39:       spoiler_text: status_params[:spoiler_text],
40:       visibility: status_params[:visibility],
41:       language: status_params[:language],
42:       scheduled_at: status_params[:scheduled_at],
43:       application: doorkeeper_token.application,
44:       poll: status_params[:poll],
45:       allowed_mentions: status_params[:allowed_mentions],
46:       idempotency: request.headers['Idempotency-Key'],
47:       with_rate_limit: true
48:     )
49: 
50:     render json: @status, serializer: serializer_for_status
51:   rescue PostStatusService::UnexpectedMentionsError => e
52:     render json: unexpected_accounts_error_json(e), status: 422
53:   end
54: 
55:   def update
56:     @status = Status.where(account: current_account).find(params[:id])
57:     authorize @status, :update?
58: 
59:     update_options = {
60:       text: status_params[:status],
61:       media_ids: status_params[:media_ids],
62:       media_attributes: status_params[:media_attributes],
63:       sensitive: status_params[:sensitive],
64:       language: status_params[:language],
65:       spoiler_text: status_params[:spoiler_text],
66:       poll: status_params[:poll],
67:     }
68: 
69:     update_options[:quote_approval_policy] = quote_approval_policy if status_params[:quote_approval_policy].present?
70: 
71:     UpdateStatusService.new.call(@status, current_account.id, update_options)
72: 
73:     render json: @status, serializer: REST::StatusSerializer
74:   end
75: 
76:   def destroy
77:     @status = Status.where(account: current_account).find(params[:id])
78:     authorize @status, :destroy?
79: 
80:     # JSON is generated before `discard_with_reblogs` in order to have the proper URL
81:     # for media attachments, as it would otherwise redirect to the media proxy
82:     json = render_to_body json: @status, serializer: REST::StatusSerializer, source_requested: true
83: 
84:     @status.discard_with_reblogs
85:     StatusPin.find_by(status: @status)&.destroy
86:     @status.account.statuses_count = @status.account.statuses_count - 1
87: 
88:     RemovalWorker.perform_async(@status.id, { 'redraft' => !truthy_param?(:delete_media) })
89: 
90:     render json: json
91:   end
92: 
93:   private
94: 
95:   def set_statuses
96:     @statuses = Status.permitted_statuses_from_ids(status_ids, current_account)
97:   end
98: 
99:   def set_status
100:     @status = Status.find(params[:id])
101:     authorize @status, :show?
102:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, Mastodon::NotPermittedError
103:     not_found
104:   end
105: 
106:   def set_thread
107:     @thread = Status.find(status_params[:in_reply_to_id]) if status_params[:in_reply_to_id].present?
108:     authorize(@thread, :show?) if @thread.present?
109:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, Mastodon::NotPermittedError
110:     render json: { error: I18n.t('statuses.errors.in_reply_not_found') }, status: 404
111:   end
112: 
113:   def set_quoted_status
114:     @quoted_status = Status.find(status_params[:quoted_status_id])&.proper if status_params[:quoted_status_id].present?
115:     authorize(@quoted_status, :quote?) if @quoted_status.present?
116:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, Mastodon::NotPermittedError
117:     # TODO: distinguish between non-existing and non-quotable posts
118:     render json: { error: I18n.t('statuses.errors.quoted_status_not_found') }, status: 404
119:   end
120: 
121:   def check_statuses_limit
122:     raise(Mastodon::ValidationError) if status_ids.size > DEFAULT_STATUSES_LIMIT
123:   end
124: 
125:   def status_ids
126:     Array(statuses_params[:id]).uniq.map(&:to_i)
127:   end
128: 
129:   def statuses_params
130:     params.permit(id: [])
131:   end
132: 
133:   def status_params
134:     params.permit(
135:       :status,
136:       :in_reply_to_id,
137:       :quoted_status_id,
138:       :quote_approval_policy,
139:       :sensitive,
140:       :spoiler_text,
141:       :visibility,
142:       :language,
143:       :scheduled_at,
144:       allowed_mentions: [],
145:       media_ids: [],
146:       media_attributes: [
147:         :id,
148:         :thumbnail,
149:         :description,
150:         :focus,
151:       ],
152:       poll: [
153:         :multiple,
154:         :hide_totals,
155:         :expires_in,
156:         options: [],
157:       ]
158:     )
159:   end
160: 
161:   def serializer_for_status
162:     @status.is_a?(ScheduledStatus) ? REST::ScheduledStatusSerializer : REST::StatusSerializer
163:   end
164: 
165:   def unexpected_accounts_error_json(error)
166:     {
167:       error: error.message,
168:       unexpected_accounts: serialized_accounts(error.accounts),
169:     }
170:   end
171: 
172:   def serialized_accounts(accounts)
173:     ActiveModel::Serializer::CollectionSerializer.new(accounts, serializer: REST::AccountSerializer, scope_name: :current_user, scope: current_user)
174:   end
175: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class StatusesController < ApplicationController
4:   include WebAppControllerConcern
5:   include SignatureAuthentication
6:   include Authorization
7:   include AccountOwnedConcern
8: 
9:   vary_by -> { public_fetch_mode? ? 'Accept, Accept-Language, Cookie' : 'Accept, Accept-Language, Cookie, Signature' }
10: 
11:   before_action :require_account_signature!, only: [:show, :activity], if: -> { request.format == :json && authorized_fetch_mode? }
12:   before_action :set_status
13:   before_action :redirect_to_original, only: :show
14:   before_action :verify_embed_allowed, only: :embed
15: 
16:   after_action :set_link_headers
17: 
18:   skip_around_action :set_locale, if: -> { request.format == :json }
19:   skip_before_action :require_functional!, only: [:show, :embed], unless: :limited_federation_mode?
20: 
21:   content_security_policy only: :embed do |policy|
22:     policy.frame_ancestors(false)
23:   end
24: 
25:   def show
26:     respond_to do |format|
27:       format.html do
28:         expires_in 10.seconds, public: true if current_account.nil?
29: 
30:         redirect_to short_account_status_path(@account, @status) if account_id_param.present? && username_param.blank?
31:       end
32: 
33:       format.json do
34:         expires_in @status.quote&.pending? ? 5.seconds : 3.minutes, public: true if @status.distributable? && public_fetch_mode?
35:         render_with_cache json: @status, content_type: 'application/activity+json', serializer: ActivityPub::NoteSerializer, adapter: ActivityPub::Adapter
36:       end
37:     end
38:   end
39: 
40:   def activity
41:     expires_in 3.minutes, public: @status.distributable? && public_fetch_mode?
42:     render_with_cache json: @status, content_type: 'application/activity+json', serializer: activity_serializer, adapter: ActivityPub::Adapter
43:   end
44: 
45:   def embed
46:     expires_in 180, public: true
47:     response.headers.delete('X-Frame-Options')
48: 
49:     render layout: 'embedded'
50:   end
51: 
52:   private
53: 
54:   def verify_embed_allowed
55:     not_found if @status.hidden? || @status.reblog?
56:   end
57: 
58:   def set_link_headers
59:     response.headers['Link'] = LinkHeader.new(
60:       [[ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(@status), [%w(rel alternate), %w(type application/activity+json)]]]
61:     ).to_s
62:   end
63: 
64:   def set_status
65:     @status = @account.statuses.find(params[:id])
66:     authorize @status, :show?
67:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, Mastodon::NotPermittedError
68:     not_found
69:   end
70: 
71:   def redirect_to_original
72:     redirect_to(ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.url_for(@status.reblog), allow_other_host: true) if @status.reblog?
73:   end
74: 
75:   def activity_serializer
76:     @status.reblog? ? ActivityPub::AnnounceNoteSerializer : ActivityPub::CreateNoteSerializer
77:   end
78: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Schema Information
4: #
5: # Table name: custom_filters
6: #
7: #  id         :bigint(8)        not null, primary key
8: #  action     :integer          default("warn"), not null
9: #  context    :string           default([]), not null, is an Array
10: #  expires_at :datetime
11: #  phrase     :text             default(""), not null
12: #  created_at :datetime         not null
13: #  updated_at :datetime         not null
14: #  account_id :bigint(8)        not null
15: #
16: 
17: class CustomFilter < ApplicationRecord
18:   self.ignored_columns += %w(whole_word irreversible)
19: 
20:   alias_attribute :title, :phrase
21:   alias_attribute :filter_action, :action
22: 
23:   VALID_CONTEXTS = %w(
24:     home
25:     notifications
26:     public
27:     thread
28:     account
29:   ).freeze
30: 
31:   EXPIRATION_DURATIONS = [30.minutes, 1.hour, 6.hours, 12.hours, 1.day, 1.week].freeze
32: 
33:   TITLE_LENGTH_LIMIT = 256
34: 
35:   include Expireable
36:   include Redisable
37: 
38:   enum :action, { warn: 0, hide: 1, blur: 2 }, suffix: :action, validate: true
39: 
40:   belongs_to :account
41:   has_many :keywords, class_name: 'CustomFilterKeyword', inverse_of: :custom_filter, dependent: :destroy
42:   has_many :statuses, class_name: 'CustomFilterStatus', inverse_of: :custom_filter, dependent: :destroy
43:   accepts_nested_attributes_for :keywords, reject_if: :all_blank, allow_destroy: true
44: 
45:   validates :title, :context, presence: true
46:   validates :title, length: { maximum: TITLE_LENGTH_LIMIT }
47:   validate :context_must_be_valid
48: 
49:   normalizes :context, with: ->(context) { context.map(&:strip).filter_map(&:presence) }
50:   scope :unexpired, -> { where(expires_at: nil).or where.not(expires_at: ..Time.zone.now) }
51: 
52:   before_save :prepare_cache_invalidation!
53:   before_destroy :prepare_cache_invalidation!
54:   after_commit :invalidate_cache!
55: 
56:   def expires_in
57:     return nil if expires_at.nil?
58: 
59:     EXPIRATION_DURATIONS.find { |expires_in| expires_in.from_now >= expires_at }
60:   end
61: 
62:   def irreversible=(value)
63:     self.action = ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(value) ? :hide : :warn
64:   end
65: 
66:   def irreversible?
67:     hide_action?
68:   end
69: 
70:   def self.cached_filters_for(account_id)
71:     active_filters = Rails.cache.fetch("filters:v3:#{account_id}") do
72:       filters_hash = {}
73: 
74:       scope = CustomFilterKeyword.left_outer_joins(:custom_filter).merge(unexpired.where(account_id: account_id))
75: 
76:       scope.to_a.group_by(&:custom_filter).each do |filter, keywords|
77:         keywords.map!(&:to_regex)
78: 
79:         filters_hash[filter.id] = { keywords: Regexp.union(keywords), filter: filter }
80:       end.to_h
81: 
82:       scope = CustomFilterStatus.left_outer_joins(:custom_filter).merge(unexpired.where(account_id: account_id))
83: 
84:       scope.to_a.group_by(&:custom_filter).each do |filter, statuses|
85:         filters_hash[filter.id] ||= { filter: filter }
86:         filters_hash[filter.id].merge!(status_ids: statuses.map(&:status_id))
87:       end
88: 
89:       filters_hash.values.map { |cache| [cache.delete(:filter), cache] }
90:     end.to_a
91: 
92:     active_filters.reject { |custom_filter, _| custom_filter.expired? }
93:   end
94: 
95:   def self.apply_cached_filters(cached_filters, status)
96:     cached_filters.filter_map do |filter, rules|
97:       match = rules[:keywords].match(status.proper.searchable_text) if rules[:keywords].present?
98:       keyword_matches = [match.to_s] unless match.nil?
99: 
100:       status_matches = [status.id, status.reblog_of_id].compact & rules[:status_ids] if rules[:status_ids].present?
101: 
102:       next if keyword_matches.blank? && status_matches.blank?
103: 
104:       FilterResultPresenter.new(filter: filter, keyword_matches: keyword_matches, status_matches: status_matches)
105:     end
106:   end
107: 
108:   def prepare_cache_invalidation!
109:     @should_invalidate_cache = true
110:   end
111: 
112:   def invalidate_cache!
113:     return unless @should_invalidate_cache
114: 
115:     @should_invalidate_cache = false
116: 
117:     Rails.cache.delete("filters:v3:#{account_id}")
118:     redis.publish("timeline:#{account_id}", { event: :filters_changed }.to_json)
119:     redis.publish("timeline:system:#{account_id}", { event: :filters_changed }.to_json)
120:   end
121: 
122:   private
123: 
124:   def context_must_be_valid
125:     errors.add(:context, I18n.t('filters.errors.invalid_context')) if invalid_context_value?
126:   end
127: 
128:   def invalid_context_value?
129:     context.blank? || context.difference(VALID_CONTEXTS).any?
130:   end
131: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Schema Information
4: #
5: # Table name: preview_cards
6: #
7: #  id                           :bigint(8)        not null, primary key
8: #  author_name                  :string           default(""), not null
9: #  author_url                   :string           default(""), not null
10: #  blurhash                     :string
11: #  description                  :string           default(""), not null
12: #  embed_url                    :string           default(""), not null
13: #  height                       :integer          default(0), not null
14: #  html                         :text             default(""), not null
15: #  image_content_type           :string
16: #  image_description            :string           default(""), not null
17: #  image_file_name              :string
18: #  image_file_size              :integer
19: #  image_storage_schema_version :integer
20: #  image_updated_at             :datetime
21: #  language                     :string
22: #  link_type                    :integer
23: #  max_score                    :float
24: #  max_score_at                 :datetime
25: #  provider_name                :string           default(""), not null
26: #  provider_url                 :string           default(""), not null
27: #  published_at                 :datetime
28: #  title                        :string           default(""), not null
29: #  trendable                    :boolean
30: #  type                         :integer          default("link"), not null
31: #  url                          :string           default(""), not null
32: #  width                        :integer          default(0), not null
33: #  created_at                   :datetime         not null
34: #  updated_at                   :datetime         not null
35: #  author_account_id            :bigint(8)
36: #  unverified_author_account_id :bigint(8)
37: #
38: 
39: class PreviewCard < ApplicationRecord
40:   include Attachmentable
41: 
42:   IMAGE_MIME_TYPES = ['image/jpeg', 'image/png', 'image/gif', 'image/webp'].freeze
43:   LIMIT = 8.megabytes
44: 
45:   BLURHASH_OPTIONS = {
46:     x_comp: 4,
47:     y_comp: 4,
48:   }.freeze
49: 
50:   # URL size limit to safely store in PosgreSQL's unique indexes
51:   # Technically this is a byte-size limit but we use it as a
52:   # character limit to work with length validation
53:   URL_CHARACTER_LIMIT = 2692
54: 
55:   self.inheritance_column = false
56: 
57:   enum :type, { link: 0, photo: 1, video: 2, rich: 3 }
58:   enum :link_type, { unknown: 0, article: 1 }
59: 
60:   has_many :preview_cards_statuses, dependent: :delete_all, inverse_of: :preview_card
61:   has_many :statuses, through: :preview_cards_statuses
62: 
63:   has_one :trend, class_name: 'PreviewCardTrend', inverse_of: :preview_card, dependent: :destroy
64:   belongs_to :author_account, class_name: 'Account', optional: true
65:   belongs_to :unverified_author_account, class_name: 'Account', optional: true
66: 
67:   has_attached_file :image,
68:                     processors: [:lazy_thumbnail, :blurhash_transcoder],
69:                     styles: ->(f) { image_styles(f) },
70:                     convert_options: { all: '-quality 90 +profile "!icc,*" +set date:modify +set date:create +set date:timestamp' },
71:                     validate_media_type: false
72: 
73:   validates :url, presence: true, uniqueness: true, url: true, length: { maximum: URL_CHARACTER_LIMIT }
74:   validates_attachment_content_type :image, content_type: IMAGE_MIME_TYPES
75:   validates_attachment_size :image, less_than: LIMIT
76:   remotable_attachment :image, LIMIT
77: 
78:   scope :cached, -> { where.not(image_file_name: [nil, '']) }
79: 
80:   before_save :extract_dimensions, if: :link?
81: 
82:   # This can be set by the status when retrieving the preview card using the join model
83:   attr_accessor :original_url
84: 
85:   def appropriate_for_trends?
86:     link? && article? && title.present? && description.present? && image.present? && provider_name.present?
87:   end
88: 
89:   def domain
90:     @domain ||= Addressable::URI.parse(url).normalized_host
91:   end
92: 
93:   def provider
94:     @provider ||= PreviewCardProvider.matching_domain(domain)
95:   end
96: 
97:   def trendable?
98:     if attributes['trendable'].nil?
99:       provider&.trendable?
100:     else
101:       attributes['trendable']
102:     end
103:   end
104: 
105:   def requires_review?
106:     attributes['trendable'].nil? && (provider.nil? || provider.requires_review?)
107:   end
108: 
109:   def requires_review_notification?
110:     attributes['trendable'].nil? && (provider.nil? || provider.requires_review_notification?)
111:   end
112: 
113:   def decaying?
114:     max_score_at && max_score_at >= Trends.links.options[:max_score_cooldown].ago && max_score_at < 1.day.ago
115:   end
116: 
117:   attr_writer :provider
118: 
119:   def local?
120:     false
121:   end
122: 
123:   def missing_image?
124:     width.present? && height.present? && image_file_name.blank?
125:   end
126: 
127:   def save_with_optional_image!
128:     save!
129:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
130:     self.image = nil
131:     save!
132:   end
133: 
134:   def history
135:     @history ||= Trends::History.new('links', id)
136:   end
137: 
138:   def authors
139:     @authors ||= Array(serialized_authors)
140:   end
141: 
142:   class Author < ActiveModelSerializers::Model
143:     attributes :name, :url, :account
144: 
145:     def initialize(preview_card)
146:       super(
147:         name: preview_card.author_name,
148:         url: preview_card.author_url,
149:         account: preview_card.author_account,
150:       )
151:     end
152:   end
153: 
154:   class << self
155:     private
156: 
157:     def image_styles(file)
158:       styles = {
159:         original: {
160:           pixels: 230_400, # 640x360px
161:           file_geometry_parser: FastGeometryParser,
162:           convert_options: '-coalesce',
163:           blurhash: BLURHASH_OPTIONS,
164:         },
165:       }
166: 
167:       styles[:original][:format] = 'jpg' if file.instance.image_content_type == 'image/gif'
168:       styles
169:     end
170:   end
171: 
172:   private
173: 
174:   def serialized_authors
175:     return unless author_name? || author_url? || author_account_id?
176: 
177:     PreviewCard::Author.new(self)
178:   end
179: 
180:   def extract_dimensions
181:     file = image.queued_for_write[:original]
182: 
183:     return if file.nil?
184: 
185:     width, height = FastImage.size(file.path)
186: 
187:     return nil if width.nil?
188: 
189:     self.width  = width
190:     self.height = height
191:   end
192: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Schema Information
4: #
5: # Table name: polls
6: #
7: #  id              :bigint(8)        not null, primary key
8: #  cached_tallies  :bigint(8)        default([]), not null, is an Array
9: #  expires_at      :datetime
10: #  hide_totals     :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
11: #  last_fetched_at :datetime
12: #  lock_version    :integer          default(0), not null
13: #  multiple        :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
14: #  options         :string           default([]), not null, is an Array
15: #  voters_count    :bigint(8)
16: #  votes_count     :bigint(8)        default(0), not null
17: #  created_at      :datetime         not null
18: #  updated_at      :datetime         not null
19: #  account_id      :bigint(8)        not null
20: #  status_id       :bigint(8)        not null
21: #
22: 
23: class Poll < ApplicationRecord
24:   include Expireable
25: 
26:   MAKE_FETCH_HAPPEN = 1.minute
27: 
28:   belongs_to :account
29:   belongs_to :status
30: 
31:   has_many :votes, class_name: 'PollVote', inverse_of: :poll, dependent: :delete_all
32: 
33:   with_options class_name: 'Account', source: :account, through: :votes do
34:     has_many :voters, -> { group(accounts: [:id]) }
35:     has_many :local_voters, -> { group(accounts: [:id]).merge(Account.local) }
36:   end
37: 
38:   has_many :notifications, as: :activity, dependent: :destroy
39: 
40:   validates :options, presence: true
41:   validates :expires_at, presence: true, if: :local?
42:   validates_with PollOptionsValidator, if: :local?
43:   validates_with PollExpirationValidator, if: -> { local? && expires_at_changed? }
44: 
45:   before_validation :prepare_options, if: :local?
46:   before_validation :prepare_votes_count
47:   before_validation :prepare_cached_tallies
48: 
49:   after_commit :reset_parent_cache, on: :update
50: 
51:   def loaded_options
52:     options.map.with_index { |title, key| Option.new(self, key.to_s, title, show_totals_now? ? (cached_tallies[key] || 0) : nil) }
53:   end
54: 
55:   def possibly_stale?
56:     remote? && last_fetched_before_expiration? && time_passed_since_last_fetch?
57:   end
58: 
59:   def voted?(account)
60:     account.id == account_id || votes.exists?(account: account)
61:   end
62: 
63:   def own_votes(account)
64:     votes.where(account: account).pluck(:choice)
65:   end
66: 
67:   delegate :local?, :remote?, to: :account
68: 
69:   def emojis
70:     @emojis ||= CustomEmoji.from_text(options.join(' '), account.domain)
71:   end
72: 
73:   class Option < ActiveModelSerializers::Model
74:     attributes :id, :title, :votes_count, :poll
75: 
76:     def initialize(poll, id, title, votes_count)
77:       super(
78:         poll: poll,
79:         id: id,
80:         title: title,
81:         votes_count: votes_count,
82:       )
83:     end
84:   end
85: 
86:   def reset_votes!
87:     self.cached_tallies = options.map { 0 }
88:     self.votes_count = 0
89:     self.voters_count = 0
90:     votes.delete_all unless new_record?
91:   end
92: 
93:   private
94: 
95:   def prepare_cached_tallies
96:     self.cached_tallies = options.map { 0 } if cached_tallies.empty?
97:   end
98: 
99:   def prepare_votes_count
100:     self.votes_count = cached_tallies.sum unless cached_tallies.empty?
101:   end
102: 
103:   def prepare_options
104:     self.options = options.map(&:strip).compact_blank
105:   end
106: 
107:   def reset_parent_cache
108:     return if status_id.nil?
109: 
110:     Rails.cache.delete("v3:statuses/#{status_id}")
111:   end
112: 
113:   def last_fetched_before_expiration?
114:     last_fetched_at.nil? || expires_at.nil? || last_fetched_at < expires_at
115:   end
116: 
117:   def time_passed_since_last_fetch?
118:     last_fetched_at.nil? || last_fetched_at < MAKE_FETCH_HAPPEN.ago
119:   end
120: 
121:   def show_totals_now?
122:     expired? || !hide_totals?
123:   end
124: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require_relative 'base'
4: 
5: module Mastodon::CLI
6:   class Statuses < Base
7:     include ActionView::Helpers::NumberHelper
8: 
9:     option :days, type: :numeric, default: 90
10:     option :batch_size, type: :numeric, default: 1_000, aliases: [:b], desc: 'Number of records in each batch'
11:     option :continue, type: :boolean, default: false, desc: 'If remove is not completed, execute from the previous continuation'
12:     option :clean_followed, type: :boolean, default: false, desc: 'Include the status of remote accounts that are followed by local accounts as candidates for remove'
13:     option :skip_status_remove, type: :boolean, default: false, desc: 'Skip status remove (run only cleanup tasks)'
14:     option :skip_media_remove, type: :boolean, default: false, desc: 'Skip remove orphaned media attachments'
15:     option :compress_database, type: :boolean, default: false, desc: 'Compress database and update the statistics. This option locks the table for a long time, so run it offline'
16:     desc 'remove', 'Remove unreferenced statuses'
17:     long_desc <<~LONG_DESC
18:       Remove statuses that are not referenced by local user activity, such as
19:       ones that came from relays, or belonging to users that were once followed
20:       by someone locally but no longer are.
21: 
22:       It also removes orphaned records and performs additional cleanup tasks
23:       such as updating statistics and recovering disk space.
24: 
25:       This is a computationally heavy procedure that creates extra database
26:       indices before commencing, and removes them afterward.
27:     LONG_DESC
28:     def remove
29:       fail_with_message 'Cannot run with this batch_size setting, must be at least 1' if options[:batch_size] < 1
30: 
31:       remove_statuses
32:       vacuum_and_analyze_statuses
33:       remove_orphans_media_attachments
34:       remove_orphans_conversations
35:       vacuum_and_analyze_conversations
36:     end
37: 
38:     private
39: 
40:     def remove_statuses
41:       return if options[:skip_status_remove]
42: 
43:       start_at = Time.now.to_f
44: 
45:       max_id   = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(options[:days].days.ago, with_random: false)
46: 
47:       unless options[:continue] && ActiveRecord::Base.connection.table_exists?('statuses_to_be_deleted')
48:         say('Extract the deletion target from statuses... This might take a while...')
49: 
50:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.create_table('statuses_to_be_deleted', force: true)
51: 
52:         # Skip accounts followed by local accounts
53:         clean_followed_sql = 'AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM follows WHERE statuses.account_id = follows.target_account_id)' unless options[:clean_followed]
54: 
55:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_insert(<<~SQL.squish, 'SQL', [max_id])
56:           INSERT INTO statuses_to_be_deleted (id)
57:           SELECT statuses.id FROM statuses WHERE deleted_at IS NULL AND NOT local AND uri IS NOT NULL AND (id < $1)
58:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM statuses AS statuses1 WHERE statuses.id = statuses1.in_reply_to_id)
59:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM statuses AS statuses1 WHERE statuses1.id = statuses.reblog_of_id AND (statuses1.uri IS NULL OR statuses1.local))
60:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM statuses AS statuses1 WHERE statuses.id = statuses1.reblog_of_id AND (statuses1.uri IS NULL OR statuses1.local OR statuses1.id >= $1))
61:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM status_pins WHERE statuses.id = status_id)
62:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM mentions WHERE statuses.id = mentions.status_id AND mentions.account_id IN (SELECT accounts.id FROM accounts WHERE domain IS NULL))
63:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM favourites WHERE statuses.id = favourites.status_id AND favourites.account_id IN (SELECT accounts.id FROM accounts WHERE domain IS NULL))
64:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM bookmarks WHERE statuses.id = bookmarks.status_id AND bookmarks.account_id IN (SELECT accounts.id FROM accounts WHERE domain IS NULL))
65:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM quotes JOIN statuses statuses1 ON quotes.status_id = statuses1.id WHERE quotes.quoted_status_id = statuses.id AND (statuses1.uri IS NULL OR statuses1.local))
66:           AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM quotes JOIN statuses statuses1 ON quotes.quoted_status_id = statuses1.id WHERE quotes.status_id = statuses.id AND (statuses1.uri IS NULL OR statuses1.local))
67:           #{clean_followed_sql}
68:         SQL
69: 
70:         say('Removing temporary database indices to restore write performance...')
71:       end
72: 
73:       say('Beginning statuses removal... This might take a while...')
74: 
75:       klass = Class.new(ApplicationRecord) do |c|
76:         c.table_name = 'statuses_to_be_deleted'
77:       end
78: 
79:       Object.const_set(:StatusToBeDeleted, klass)
80: 
81:       scope     = StatusToBeDeleted
82:       processed = 0
83:       removed   = 0
84:       progress  = create_progress_bar(scope.count.fdiv(options[:batch_size]).ceil)
85: 
86:       scope.reorder(nil).in_batches(of: options[:batch_size]) do |relation|
87:         ids        = relation.pluck(:id)
88:         processed += ids.count
89:         removed   += Status.unscoped.where(id: ids).delete_all
90:         progress.increment
91:       end
92: 
93:       progress.stop
94: 
95:       ActiveRecord::Base.connection.drop_table('statuses_to_be_deleted')
96: 
97:       say("Done after #{Time.now.to_f - start_at}s, removed #{removed} out of #{processed} statuses.", :green)
98:     end
99: 
100:     def remove_orphans_media_attachments
101:       return if options[:skip_media_remove]
102: 
103:       start_at = Time.now.to_f
104: 
105:       say('Beginning removal of now-orphaned media attachments to free up disk space...')
106: 
107:       scope     = MediaAttachment.unattached.created_before(options[:days].pred.days.ago)
108:       processed = 0
109:       removed   = 0
110:       progress  = create_progress_bar(scope.count)
111: 
112:       scope.find_each do |media_attachment|
113:         media_attachment.destroy!
114: 
115:         removed += 1
116:       rescue => e
117:         progress.log pastel.red("Error processing #{media_attachment.id}: #{e}")
118:       ensure
119:         progress.increment
120:         processed += 1
121:       end
122: 
123:       progress.stop
124: 
125:       say("Done after #{Time.now.to_f - start_at}s, removed #{removed} out of #{processed} media_attachments.", :green)
126:     end
127: 
128:     def remove_orphans_conversations
129:       start_at = Time.now.to_f
130: 
131:       unless options[:continue] && ActiveRecord::Base.connection.table_exists?('conversations_to_be_deleted')
132:         say('Creating temporary database indices...')
133: 
134:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.add_index(:statuses, :conversation_id, name: :index_statuses_conversation_id, algorithm: :concurrently, if_not_exists: true)
135: 
136:         say('Extract the deletion target from conversations... This might take a while...')
137: 
138:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.create_table('conversations_to_be_deleted', force: true)
139: 
140:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_insert(<<~SQL.squish, 'SQL')
141:           INSERT INTO conversations_to_be_deleted (id)
142:           SELECT id FROM conversations WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM statuses WHERE statuses.conversation_id = conversations.id)
143:         SQL
144: 
145:         say('Removing temporary database indices to restore write performance...')
146:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.remove_index(:statuses, name: :index_statuses_conversation_id, if_exists: true)
147:       end
148: 
149:       say('Beginning orphans removal... This might take a while...')
150: 
151:       klass = Class.new(ApplicationRecord) do |c|
152:         c.table_name = 'conversations_to_be_deleted'
153:       end
154: 
155:       Object.const_set(:ConversationsToBeDeleted, klass)
156: 
157:       scope     = ConversationsToBeDeleted
158:       processed = 0
159:       removed   = 0
160:       progress  = create_progress_bar(scope.count.fdiv(options[:batch_size]).ceil)
161: 
162:       scope.in_batches(of: options[:batch_size]) do |relation|
163:         ids        = relation.pluck(:id)
164:         processed += ids.count
165:         removed   += Conversation.unscoped.where(id: ids).delete_all
166:         progress.increment
167:       end
168: 
169:       progress.stop
170: 
171:       ActiveRecord::Base.connection.drop_table('conversations_to_be_deleted')
172: 
173:       say("Done after #{Time.now.to_f - start_at}s, removed #{removed} out of #{processed} conversations.", :green)
174:     ensure
175:       say('Removing temporary database indices to restore write performance...')
176:       ActiveRecord::Base.connection.remove_index(:statuses, name: :index_statuses_conversation_id, if_exists: true)
177:     end
178: 
179:     def vacuum_and_analyze_statuses
180:       if options[:compress_database]
181:         say('Running "VACUUM FULL ANALYZE statuses"...')
182:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute('VACUUM FULL ANALYZE statuses')
183:         say('Running "REINDEX TABLE statuses"...')
184:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute('REINDEX TABLE statuses')
185:       else
186:         say('Running "ANALYZE statuses"...')
187:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute('ANALYZE statuses')
188:       end
189:     end
190: 
191:     def vacuum_and_analyze_conversations
192:       if options[:compress_database]
193:         say('Running "VACUUM FULL ANALYZE conversations"...')
194:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute('VACUUM FULL ANALYZE conversations')
195:         say('Running "REINDEX TABLE conversations"...')
196:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute('REINDEX TABLE conversations')
197:       else
198:         say('Running "ANALYZE conversations"...')
199:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute('ANALYZE conversations')
200:       end
201:     end
202:   end
203: end

(End of file - total 203 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require_relative 'base'
4: 
5: module Mastodon::CLI
6:   class Cache < Base
7:     desc 'clear', 'Clear out the cache storage'
8:     def clear
9:       Rails.cache.clear
10:       say('OK', :green)
11:     end
12: 
13:     option :concurrency, type: :numeric, default: 5, aliases: [:c]
14:     option :verbose, type: :boolean, aliases: [:v]
15:     desc 'recount TYPE', 'Update hard-cached counters'
16:     long_desc <<~LONG_DESC
17:       Update hard-cached counters of TYPE by counting referenced
18:       records from scratch. TYPE can be "accounts" or "statuses".
19: 
20:       It may take a very long time to finish, depending on the
21:       size of the database.
22:     LONG_DESC
23:     def recount(type)
24:       case type
25:       when 'accounts'
26:         processed, = parallelize_with_progress(accounts_with_stats) do |account|
27:           recount_account_stats(account)
28:         end
29:       when 'statuses'
30:         processed, = parallelize_with_progress(statuses_with_stats) do |status|
31:           recount_status_stats(status)
32:         end
33:       else
34:         fail_with_message "Unknown type: #{type}"
35:       end
36: 
37:       say
38:       say("OK, recounted #{processed} records", :green)
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     def accounts_with_stats
44:       Account.local.includes(:account_stat)
45:     end
46: 
47:     def statuses_with_stats
48:       Status.includes(:status_stat)
49:     end
50: 
51:     def recount_account_stats(account)
52:       account.account_stat.tap do |account_stat|
53:         account_stat.following_count = account.active_relationships.count
54:         account_stat.followers_count = account.passive_relationships.count
55:         account_stat.statuses_count  = account.statuses.not_direct_visibility.count
56: 
57:         account_stat.save if account_stat.changed?
58:       end
59:     end
60: 
61:     def recount_status_stats(status)
62:       status.status_stat.tap do |status_stat|
63:         status_stat.replies_count    = status.replies.not_direct_visibility.count
64:         status_stat.reblogs_count    = status.reblogs.count
65:         status_stat.favourites_count = status.favourites.count
66:         status_stat.quotes_count     = status.quotes.accepted.count
67: 
68:         status_stat.save if status_stat.changed?
69:       end
70:     end
71:   end
72: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require_relative 'base'
4: 
5: module Mastodon::CLI
6:   class Maintenance < Base
7:     MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION = 2019_10_01_213028
8:     MAX_SUPPORTED_VERSION = 2023_10_23_105620
9: 
10:     # Stubs to enjoy ActiveRecord queries while not depending on a particular
11:     # version of the code/database
12: 
13:     class Status < ApplicationRecord; end
14:     class StatusPin < ApplicationRecord; end
15:     class Poll < ApplicationRecord; end
16:     class Report < ApplicationRecord; end
17:     class Tombstone < ApplicationRecord; end
18:     class Favourite < ApplicationRecord; end
19:     class Follow < ApplicationRecord; end
20:     class FollowRequest < ApplicationRecord; end
21:     class Block < ApplicationRecord; end
22:     class Mute < ApplicationRecord; end
23:     class AccountIdentityProof < ApplicationRecord; end
24:     class AccountModerationNote < ApplicationRecord; end
25:     class AccountPin < ApplicationRecord; end
26:     class ListAccount < ApplicationRecord; end
27:     class PollVote < ApplicationRecord; end
28:     class Mention < ApplicationRecord; end
29:     class NotificationPermission < ApplicationRecord; end
30:     class NotificationRequest < ApplicationRecord; end
31:     class AccountDomainBlock < ApplicationRecord; end
32:     class AnnouncementReaction < ApplicationRecord; end
33:     class FeaturedTag < ApplicationRecord; end
34:     class CustomEmoji < ApplicationRecord; end
35:     class CustomEmojiCategory < ApplicationRecord; end
36:     class Bookmark < ApplicationRecord; end
37:     class WebauthnCredential < ApplicationRecord; end
38:     class FollowRecommendationSuppression < ApplicationRecord; end
39:     class CanonicalEmailBlock < ApplicationRecord; end
40:     class Appeal < ApplicationRecord; end
41:     class Webhook < ApplicationRecord; end
42:     class SeveredRelationship < ApplicationRecord; end
43:     class TagFollow < ApplicationRecord; end
44: 
45:     class DomainBlock < ApplicationRecord
46:       enum :severity, { silence: 0, suspend: 1, noop: 2 }
47:       scope :by_severity, -> { in_order_of(:severity, %w(noop silence suspend)).order(:domain) }
48:     end
49: 
50:     class PreviewCard < ApplicationRecord
51:       self.inheritance_column = false
52:     end
53: 
54:     class MediaAttachment < ApplicationRecord
55:       self.inheritance_column = nil
56:     end
57: 
58:     class Notification < ApplicationRecord
59:       self.inheritance_column = nil
60:     end
61: 
62:     class BulkImport < ApplicationRecord
63:       self.inheritance_column = nil
64:     end
65: 
66:     class SoftwareUpdate < ApplicationRecord
67:       self.inheritance_column = nil
68:     end
69: 
70:     class AccountStat < ApplicationRecord
71:       belongs_to :account, inverse_of: :account_stat
72:     end
73: 
74:     # Dummy class, to make migration possible across version changes
75:     class Account < ApplicationRecord
76:       has_one :user, inverse_of: :account
77:       has_one :account_stat, inverse_of: :account
78: 
79:       scope :local, -> { where(domain: nil) }
80: 
81:       def local?
82:         domain.nil?
83:       end
84: 
85:       def acct
86:         local? ? username : "#{username}@#{domain}"
87:       end
88: 
89:       def db_table_exists?(table)
90:         ActiveRecord::Base.connection.table_exists?(table)
91:       end
92: 
93:       # This is a duplicate of the Account::Merging concern because we need it
94:       # to be independent from code version.
95:       def merge_with!(other_account)
96:         # Since it's the same remote resource, the remote resource likely
97:         # already believes we are following/blocking, so it's safe to
98:         # re-attribute the relationships too. However, during the presence
99:         # of the index bug users could have *also* followed the reference
100:         # account already, therefore mass update will not work and we need
101:         # to check for (and skip past) uniqueness errors
102: 
103:         owned_classes = [
104:           Status, StatusPin, MediaAttachment, Poll, Report, Tombstone, Favourite,
105:           Follow, FollowRequest, Block, Mute,
106:           AccountModerationNote, AccountPin, AccountStat, ListAccount,
107:           PollVote, Mention
108:         ]
109:         owned_classes << AccountDeletionRequest if db_table_exists?(:account_deletion_requests)
110:         owned_classes << AccountNote if db_table_exists?(:account_notes)
111:         owned_classes << FollowRecommendationSuppression if db_table_exists?(:follow_recommendation_suppressions)
112:         owned_classes << AccountIdentityProof if db_table_exists?(:account_identity_proofs)
113:         owned_classes << Appeal if db_table_exists?(:appeals)
114:         owned_classes << BulkImport if db_table_exists?(:bulk_imports)
115:         owned_classes << TagFollow if db_table_exists?(:tag_follows)
116: 
117:         owned_classes.each do |klass|
118:           klass.where(account_id: other_account.id).find_each do |record|
119:             record.update_attribute(:account_id, id)
120:           rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
121:             next
122:           end
123:         end
124: 
125:         from_classes = [Notification]
126:         from_classes << NotificationPermission if db_table_exists?(:notification_permissions)
127:         from_classes << NotificationRequest if db_table_exists?(:notification_requests)
128: 
129:         from_classes.each do |klass|
130:           klass.where(from_account_id: other_account.id).find_each do |record|
131:             record.update_attribute(:from_account_id, id)
132:           rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
133:             next
134:           end
135:         end
136: 
137:         target_classes = [Follow, FollowRequest, Block, Mute, AccountModerationNote, AccountPin]
138:         target_classes << AccountNote if db_table_exists?(:account_notes)
139: 
140:         target_classes.each do |klass|
141:           klass.where(target_account_id: other_account.id).find_each do |record|
142:             record.update_attribute(:target_account_id, id)
143:           rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
144:             next
145:           end
146:         end
147: 
148:         if db_table_exists?(:canonical_email_blocks)
149:           CanonicalEmailBlock.where(reference_account_id: other_account.id).find_each do |record|
150:             record.update_attribute(:reference_account_id, id)
151:           end
152:         end
153: 
154:         if db_table_exists?(:appeals)
155:           Appeal.where(account_warning_id: other_account.id).find_each do |record|
156:             record.update_attribute(:account_warning_id, id)
157:           end
158:         end
159: 
160:         if db_table_exists?(:severed_relationships)
161:           SeveredRelationship.where(local_account_id: other_account.id).reorder(nil).find_each do |record|
162:             record.update_attribute(:local_account_id, id)
163:           rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
164:             next
165:           end
166: 
167:           SeveredRelationship.where(remote_account_id: other_account.id).reorder(nil).find_each do |record|
168:             record.update_attribute(:remote_account_id, id)
169:           rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
170:             next
171:           end
172:         end
173:       end
174:     end
175: 
176:     class User < ApplicationRecord
177:       belongs_to :account, inverse_of: :user
178:     end
179: 
180:     desc 'fix-duplicates', 'Fix duplicates in database and rebuild indexes'
181:     long_desc <<~LONG_DESC
182:       Delete or merge duplicate accounts, statuses, emojis, etc. and rebuild indexes.
183: 
184:       This is useful if your database indexes are corrupted because of issues such as https://wiki.postgresql.org/wiki/Locale_data_changes
185: 
186:       Mastodon has to be stopped to run this task, which will take a long time and may be destructive.
187:     LONG_DESC
188:     def fix_duplicates
189:       verify_system_ready!
190: 
191:       process_deduplications
192: 
193:       deduplication_cleanup_tasks
194: 
195:       say 'Finished!'
196:     end
197: 
198:     private
199: 
200:     def verify_system_ready!
201:       verify_schema_version!
202:       verify_sidekiq_not_active!
203:       verify_backup_warning!
204:       disable_timeout!
205:     end
206: 
207:     def process_deduplications
208:       deduplicate_users!
209:       deduplicate_account_domain_blocks!
210:       deduplicate_account_identity_proofs!
211:       deduplicate_announcement_reactions!
212:       deduplicate_conversations!
213:       deduplicate_custom_emojis!
214:       deduplicate_custom_emoji_categories!
215:       deduplicate_domain_allows!
216:       deduplicate_domain_blocks!
217:       deduplicate_unavailable_domains!
218:       deduplicate_email_domain_blocks!
219:       deduplicate_media_attachments!
220:       deduplicate_preview_cards!
221:       deduplicate_statuses!
222:       deduplicate_accounts!
223:       deduplicate_tags!
224:       deduplicate_webauthn_credentials!
225:       deduplicate_webhooks!
226:       deduplicate_software_updates!
227:     end
228: 
229:     def deduplication_cleanup_tasks
230:       refresh_instances_view if schema_has_instances_view?
231:       Rails.cache.clear
232:     end
233: 
234:     def refresh_instances_view
235:       Scenic.database.refresh_materialized_view('instances', concurrently: true, cascade: false)
236:     end
237: 
238:     def schema_has_instances_view?
239:       migrator_version >= 2020_12_06_004238
240:     end
241: 
242:     def verify_schema_version!
243:       if migrator_version < MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION
244:         fail_with_message <<~ERROR
245:           Your version of the database schema is too old and is not supported by this script.
246:           Please update to at least Mastodon 3.0.0 before running this script.
247:         ERROR
248:       elsif migrator_version > MAX_SUPPORTED_VERSION
249:         say 'Your version of the database schema is more recent than this script, this may cause unexpected errors.', :yellow
250:         fail_with_message 'Stopping maintenance script because data is more recent than script version.' unless yes?('Continue anyway? (Yes/No)')
251:       end
252:     end
253: 
254:     def verify_sidekiq_not_active!
255:       fail_with_message 'It seems Sidekiq is running. All Mastodon processes need to be stopped when using this script.' if Sidekiq::ProcessSet.new.any?
256:     end
257: 
258:     def verify_backup_warning!
259:       say 'This task will take a long time to run and is potentially destructive.', :yellow
260:       say 'Please make sure to stop Mastodon and have a backup.', :yellow
261:       fail_with_message 'Maintenance process stopped.' unless yes?('Continue? (Yes/No)')
262:     end
263: 
264:     def disable_timeout!
265:       # Remove server-configured timeout if present
266:       database_connection.execute(<<~SQL.squish)
267:         SET statement_timeout = 0
268:       SQL
269:     end
270: 
271:     def deduplicate_accounts!
272:       remove_index_if_exists!(:accounts, 'index_accounts_on_username_and_domain_lower')
273: 
274:       say 'Deduplicating accounts… for local accounts, you will be asked to chose which account to keep unchanged.'
275: 
276:       duplicate_record_ids(:accounts, "lower(username), COALESCE(lower(domain), '')").each do |row|
277:         accounts = Account.where(id: row['ids'].split(','))
278: 
279:         if accounts.first.local?
280:           deduplicate_local_accounts!(accounts)
281:         else
282:           deduplicate_remote_accounts!(accounts)
283:         end
284:       end
285:     ensure
286:       say 'Restoring index_accounts_on_username_and_domain_lower…'
287: 
288:       if migrator_version < 2020_06_20_164023
289:         database_connection.add_index :accounts, 'lower (username), lower(domain)', name: 'index_accounts_on_username_and_domain_lower', unique: true
290:       else
291:         database_connection.add_index :accounts, "lower (username), COALESCE(lower(domain), '')", name: 'index_accounts_on_username_and_domain_lower', unique: true
292:       end
293: 
294:       say 'Reindexing textual indexes on accounts…'
295:       rebuild_index(:search_index)
296:       rebuild_index(:index_accounts_on_uri)
297:       rebuild_index(:index_accounts_on_url)
298:       rebuild_index(:index_accounts_on_domain_and_id) if migrator_version >= 2023_05_24_190515
299:     end
300: 
301:     def deduplicate_users!
302:       remove_index_if_exists!(:users, 'index_users_on_confirmation_token')
303:       remove_index_if_exists!(:users, 'index_users_on_email')
304:       remove_index_if_exists!(:users, 'index_users_on_remember_token')

667:     def deduplicate_local_accounts!(scope)
668:       accounts = scope.order(id: :desc).includes(:account_stat, :user).to_a
669: 
670:       say "Multiple local accounts were found for username '#{accounts.first.username}'.", :yellow
671:       say 'All those accounts are distinct accounts but only the most recently-created one is fully-functional.', :yellow
672: 
673:       accounts.each_with_index do |account, idx|
674:         say format(
675:           '%<index>2d. %<username>s: created at: %<created_at>s; updated at: %<updated_at>s; last logged in at: %<last_log_in_at>s; statuses: %<status_count>5d; last status at: %<last_status_at>s',
676:           index: idx,
677:           username: account.username,
678:           created_at: account.created_at,
679:           updated_at: account.updated_at,
680:           last_log_in_at: account.user&.last_sign_in_at&.to_s || 'N/A',
681:           status_count: account.account_stat&.statuses_count || 0,
682:           last_status_at: account.account_stat&.last_status_at || 'N/A'
683:         )
684:       end
685: 
686:       say 'Please chose the one to keep unchanged, other ones will be automatically renamed.'
687: 
688:       ref_id = ask('Account to keep unchanged:', required: true, default: 0).to_i
689: 
690:       accounts.delete_at(ref_id)
691: 
692:       i = 0
693:       accounts.each do |account|
694:         i += 1
695:         username = account.username + "_#{i}"
696: 
697:         while Account.local.exists?(username: username)
698:           i += 1
699:           username = account.username + "_#{i}"
700:         end
701: 
702:         account.update!(username: username)
703:       end
704:     end

706:     def deduplicate_remote_accounts!(scope)
707:       accounts = scope.order(updated_at: :desc).to_a
708: 
709:       reference_account = accounts.shift
710: 
711:       accounts.each do |other_account|
712:         if other_account.public_key == reference_account.public_key
713:           # The accounts definitely point to the same resource, so
714:           # it's safe to re-attribute content and relationships
715:           reference_account.merge_with!(other_account)
716:         end
717: 
718:         other_account.destroy
719:       end
720:     end
